needs_fix_triples.note: Granger2020 is cited with role 'contradicts' but the claim references it as evidence for varying %ChAT+ VIP fractions and differing denominators; this is a 'supports' or 'qualifies' role, not 'contradicts'.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Mayer2018` and {cite:t}`Liu2021` already showed that the cardinal CGE lineages are pre-specified at progenitor stages by transcriptional and chromatin signatures; {cite:t}`Demarcogarcia2011` showed that activity-dependent migration before P3 is required for Reelin+ and CR+ CGE-derived interneurons but not for VIP+ interneurons, suggesting differential activity-dependence of subtype maturation; and {cite:t}`Frazer2017` distinguished three main molecular types within developing Htr3a-GFP+ CGE-derived precursors.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: DNA methylation atlas of the mouse brain at single-cell resolution
needs_fix_triples.note: Liu2021 profiled adult mouse brain epigenomes and identified mature cell types; it did not show that CGE lineages are 'pre-specified at progenitor stages by transcriptional and chromatin signatures,' which is a developmental claim not supported by this atlas of adult cells.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Embryonic conditional deletion of *Prox1* impairs the integration of CGE-derived precursors into superficial layers and differentially regulates the postnatal maturation of RELN+, *Calb2*/VIP+ and VIP+ subtypes — establishing PROX1 as the first identified TF specifically required for CGE-lineage acquisition of subtype-specific properties {cite:p}`Miyoshi2015`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Prox1Regulates the Subtype-Specific Development of Caudal Ganglionic Eminence-Derived GABAergic Cortical Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper's findings match the claim content exactly, so the role should be 'supports' not 'contradicts'; Miyoshi2015 establishes PROX1 as the first CGE-specific TF with these conditional-KO phenotypes.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Schematic methods-only summary of the cross-species CGE-lineage comparison. **(A)** Reported fraction of cortical GABAergic neurons assigned to the CGE/5-HT3AR/ADARB2 class — mouse fate-mapping (Mash1/Olig2-CreER) gives ~30% across whole cortex {cite:p}`Miyoshi2010`; human snRNA-seq in MTG gives ~50% (LHX6+ MGE 44% / ADARB2+ CGE 50%) {cite:p}`Hodge2019`. **(B)** The proposed partitioning across VIP / Lamp5 / Sncg / Serpinf1 subclasses across mouse, marmoset, macaque and human is shown as a methods placeholder only: no audited cross-paper panel was achievable for this stacked comparison because the underlying datasets use non-matched subclass definitions and reference areas {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Hodge2019,Krienen2023,Schmitz2022`. **(C)** Side timeline of the CGE-progenitor TF cascade — Sp8/Sp9 → COUP-TFII → PROX1 → postmitotic Prox1 / Elfn1 / activity-dependent refinement — with conditional-KO outcome annotations {cite:p}`Wei2019,Kanatani2008,Miyoshi2015,Stachniak2021,Demarcogarcia2011,Karayannis2012,Akgul2019`. *Caveat:* No quantitative cross-paper comparison was achievable for this comparison; the figure is methods-only/schematic, with the directional claim (mouse < primate CGE-class fraction) supported but the precise gap dependent on measurement modality.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Prox1Regulates the Subtype-Specific Development of Caudal Ganglionic Eminence-Derived GABAergic Cortical Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: The role is listed as 'contradicts' but the paper's findings are entirely consistent with the claims made; Miyoshi2015 supports the CGE-progenitor TF cascade and conditional-KO outcome annotations described in the claim.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The chemoattractant Cxcl14, induced by Sp8/Sp9, similarly couples CGE migration choices to layer-specific cues during the same window {cite:p}`Wei2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Transcription factors Sp8 and Sp9 regulate the development of caudal ganglionic eminence-derived cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: The abstract states Cxcl14 expression is dependent on SP8, not that it is 'induced by Sp8/Sp9' as a chemoattractant coupling migration to layer-specific cues; the paper describes migration defects (longer leading processes, ectopic accumulation) but does not characterize Cxcl14 as a chemoattractant guiding layer-specific migration choices.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Hansen2013` documented a massively expanded subventricular zone in second-trimester human ganglionic eminences that lacks the radial-fibre/oriented organisation seen in rodents and contains stem cells whose proliferation and migration patterns differ from the MGE programme — implying human-specific aspects of CGE-lineage development.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Non-epithelial stem cells and cortical interneuron production in the human ganglionic eminences
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim assigns 'contradicts' role, but the paper is actually being used to support the point about human-specific progenitor architecture diverging from rodents; Hansen2013 directly supports, not contradicts, this claim.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Homochronic transplantation of medial-ganglionic-eminence (MGE) versus caudal-ganglionic-eminence (CGE) explants into host mouse embryos established that PV and SST cardinal classes derive primarily from the MGE, whereas reelin-, calretinin-, NPY- and VIP-expressing cardinal classes derive primarily from the CGE {cite:p}`Xu2004,Bandler2017`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Origins of Cortical Interneuron Subtypes
needs_fix_triples.note: Xu et al. 2004 used an in vitro transplantation assay (PZ cells onto neonatal cortical feeder cells), not homochronic transplantation of explants into host mouse embryos. Additionally, the paper specifically addresses PV and SST from MGE and calretinin from CGE, but does not mention reelin, NPY, or VIP as CGE-derived subtypes.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Niquille2018` showed that a substantial fraction of cortical NGCs is generated by a 5-HT3AR+ *Hmx3*+ pool in the preoptic area, expressing PROX1, NR2F2 and reelin but not VIP.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Neurogliaform cortical interneurons derive from cells in the preoptic area
needs_fix_triples.note: The manuscript assigns a 'contradicts' role to Niquille2018, but the claim accurately describes the paper's own findings (POA-derived Hmx3+ NGCs expressing PROX1, NR2F2, reelin, not VIP); the paper supports rather than contradicts this claim.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Second, the dorsal MGE contributes a calretinin+/SST+ lineage (controlled in part by *Nkx6-2*) that overlaps in marker space with CGE-derived calretinin+ VIP cells but corresponds to Martinotti-like rather than VIP-bipolar morphologies {cite:p}`Sousa2009,Cauli2014`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Characterization of Nkx6-2-Derived Neocortical Interneuron Lineages
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper identifies Nkx6-2 as contributing to SST/calretinin double-positive interneurons, but it does not describe these as 'Martinotti-like' morphologies nor does it discuss overlap with CGE-derived VIP-bipolar cells; the claim's characterization goes beyond what the paper supports.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The transcription-factor (TF) logic that specifies CGE-derived interneurons differs in detail from the *Lhx6*/*Sox6*-dominated MGE programme {cite:p}`Liodis2007,Christodoulou2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: The development of MGE-derived cortical interneurons: An Lhx6 tale
needs_fix_triples.note: The cited paper (Christodoulou2022) reviews the Lhx6/Sox6-dominated MGE programme; it does not serve as a 'contradicts' reference for the claim that CGE TF logic differs from the MGE programme — it would more naturally 'supports' or contextualizes the MGE side of the contrast, not contradict it.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Touzot2016` resolved a lateral migratory stream (LMS), a medial migratory stream (MMS) and the previously known caudal migratory stream (CMS), each expressing distinct combinations of SP8, PROX1, COUP-TFI and COUP-TFII; conditional COUP-TFI inactivation differentially perturbs LMS/MMS and produces laminar-specific deficits in adult cortex. *Lopezbendito2004* used GAD65-GFP transgenic mice to track a CGE-enriched subpopulation along the lower intermediate zone from E14–E15 and into superficial layers thereafter, consistent with the late-born CGE programme {cite:p}`Lopezbendito2004`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Molecular control of two novel migratory paths for CGE-derived interneurons in the developing mouse brain
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim assigns 'contradicts' role to Touzot2016, but the paper's findings directly support the claim's description of LMS/MMS/CMS and COUP-TFI inactivation phenotypes; 'supports' would be appropriate.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Reinchisi2012` confirmed that COUP-TFII expression peaks in human CGE during gestational weeks 9–22 with a temporo-occipital > frontal/dorsal gradient. scRNA-seq of human ganglionic eminences identifies regional and temporal progenitor diversity and an evolutionarily conserved transcriptional grammar but also human-specific transcriptomic features {cite:p}`Shi2021`, while {cite:t}`Velmeshev2023` profiled >700,000 nuclei across 106 prenatal/postnatal human cortex donors and prioritised interneuron lineages as key vulnerable trajectories.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: COUP-TFII Expressing Interneurons in Human Fetal Forebrain
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim assigns a 'contradicts' role to this paper, but the paper's findings (COUP-TFII peaks in CGE with temporo-occipital > frontal/dorsal gradient in human fetal forebrain) are directly restated in the claim as confirmed facts; the paper supports rather than contradicts the claim.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Sp8 is expressed in ~20% of adult cortical interneurons derived from both dorsal LGE and dorsal CGE {cite:p}`Ma2012`, weakly in MGE mantle but strongly in CGE/dLGE {cite:p}`Tao2019`, and acts cell-autonomously to specify olfactory-bulb and cortical CGE-related lineages {cite:p}`Waclaw2006`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A Subpopulation of Dorsal Lateral/Caudal Ganglionic Eminence-Derived Neocortical Interneurons Expresses the Transcription Factor Sp8
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim assigns the role 'contradicts' to Ma2012, but Ma2012 directly supports the assertion that Sp8 is expressed in ~20% of adult cortical interneurons derived from both dorsal LGE and dorsal CGE; there is no contradiction.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Sp8 is expressed in ~20% of adult cortical interneurons derived from both dorsal LGE and dorsal CGE {cite:p}`Ma2012`, weakly in MGE mantle but strongly in CGE/dLGE {cite:p}`Tao2019`, and acts cell-autonomously to specify olfactory-bulb and cortical CGE-related lineages {cite:p}`Waclaw2006`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: The Zinc Finger Transcription Factor Sp8 Regulates the Generation and Diversity of Olfactory Bulb Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim asserts Waclaw2006 is contradicted, but the paper's findings on Sp8 regulating olfactory bulb interneuron specification are consistent with the claim that Sp8 'acts cell-autonomously to specify olfactory-bulb ... lineages'; the paper supports rather than contradicts this aspect of the claim.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: CXCL12 attracts immature interneurons during tangential migration via CXCR4/CXCR7 streams in subpial and intermediate-zone routes {cite:p}`Li2008`, and BDNF/NT4 acting via TrkB stimulates tangential migration of MGE-derived cells, with a residual effect on CGE-associated calbindin+ populations {cite:p}`Polleux2002`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Regional Distribution of Cortical Interneurons and Development of Inhibitory Tone Are Regulated by Cxcl12/Cxcr4 Signaling
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper discusses CXCL12/CXCR4 signaling but does not mention CXCR7; attributing CXCR7 involvement to this citation is not supported by the abstract.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The chemokine CXCL12 acting via CXCR4/CXCR7 attracts cortical interneurons during these tangential streams, with loss of CXCR4 responsiveness producing dispersion deficits {cite:p}`Li2008`, and BDNF/TrkB signalling provides additional permissive drive that affects both MGE- and CGE-associated populations {cite:p}`Polleux2002`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Regional Distribution of Cortical Interneurons and Development of Inhibitory Tone Are Regulated by Cxcl12/Cxcr4 Signaling
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper addresses CXCL12/CXCR4 but does not mention CXCR7; citing this paper for CXCR4/CXCR7 signaling is partially inaccurate. Also, the paper describes premature cortical plate entry and altered regional/laminar distribution upon loss of CXCR4, not simply 'dispersion deficits'.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: CXCL12 attracts immature interneurons during tangential migration via CXCR4/CXCR7 streams in subpial and intermediate-zone routes {cite:p}`Li2008`, and BDNF/NT4 acting via TrkB stimulates tangential migration of MGE-derived cells, with a residual effect on CGE-associated calbindin+ populations {cite:p}`Polleux2002`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Control of cortical interneuron migration by neurotrophins and PI3-kinase signaling
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper demonstrates BDNF/NT4 via TrkB stimulates tangential migration of MGE-derived cells, but the abstract does not mention any effect on CGE-associated calbindin+ populations; calbindin+ neurons are used as a marker for MGE-derived tangentially migrating cells, not CGE-associated populations.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The chemokine CXCL12 acting via CXCR4/CXCR7 attracts cortical interneurons during these tangential streams, with loss of CXCR4 responsiveness producing dispersion deficits {cite:p}`Li2008`, and BDNF/TrkB signalling provides additional permissive drive that affects both MGE- and CGE-associated populations {cite:p}`Polleux2002`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Control of cortical interneuron migration by neurotrophins and PI3-kinase signaling
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper shows BDNF/TrkB signaling stimulates tangential migration of MGE-derived cells specifically; there is no evidence in the abstract that this affects CGE-associated populations. The claim misattributes the scope of the finding.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Touzot2016` resolved a lateral migratory stream (LMS), a medial migratory stream (MMS) and the previously known caudal migratory stream (CMS), each expressing distinct combinations of SP8, PROX1, COUP-TFI and COUP-TFII; conditional COUP-TFI inactivation differentially perturbs LMS/MMS and produces laminar-specific deficits in adult cortex. *Lopezbendito2004* used GAD65-GFP transgenic mice to track a CGE-enriched subpopulation along the lower intermediate zone from E14–E15 and into superficial layers thereafter, consistent with the late-born CGE programme {cite:p}`Lopezbendito2004`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Preferential Origin and Layer Destination of GAD65-GFP Cortical Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim assigns the role 'contradicts' but the description of Lopezbendito2004 is fully consistent with the paper's findings (CGE origin, lower IZ migration at E14–E15, superficial layer destination, late-born programme); the paper supports rather than contradicts the claim.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Bellion2005` characterised the cellular biomechanics of tangential migration as a two-phase nucleokinesis (centrosome–Golgi forward translocation followed by nuclear advance) — a mode shared between MGE- and CGE-derived precursors.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Nucleokinesis in Tangentially Migrating Neurons Comprises Two Alternating Phases: Forward Migration of the Golgi/Centrosome Associated with Centrosome Splitting and Myosin Contraction at the Rear
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper characterises two-phase nucleokinesis in MGE cells but does not study or mention CGE-derived precursors; the claim that this mode is shared between MGE- and CGE-derived precursors is not supported.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: By the end of the first postnatal week, CGE-derived cells have completed their characteristic preferential occupancy of layers II/III (~75%) {cite:p}`Miyoshi2010`, although in mouse this enrichment is graded rather than absolute and a substantial CGE-derived contingent settles in deeper layers and in layer 1 {cite:p}`Lee2010`. *Larimer2016* used heterochronic CGE transplants into neonatal mouse V1 to show that CGE-derived precursors disperse, acquire host-like laminar position, marker expression, intrinsic properties and visual response — but, unlike MGE transplants, they do not reactivate ocular dominance plasticity, with the residual ODP induction observed in CGE transplants attributable to a small contaminating fraction of MGE-lineage cells {cite:p}`Larimer2016`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Caudal Ganglionic Eminence Precursor Transplants Disperse and Integrate as Lineage-Specific Interneurons but Do Not Induce Cortical Plasticity
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim asserts that Larimer2016 contradicts a prior statement, but the paper's findings are used here as supporting evidence for the unique role of MGE neurons, not as contradicting the laminar distribution claim; the role should be 'supports' rather than 'contradicts'.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Conditional *Satb2* knockout in mouse projection neurons reprograms intratelencephalic-type pyramidal neurons toward a pyramidal-tract identity and selectively disrupts the lamination and circuit integration of CGE-derived (but not MGE-derived) interneurons {cite:p}`Wester2019`, providing causal evidence that pyramidal subtype identity instructs CGE-lineage interneuron development in a lineage-specific manner.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Neocortical Projection Neurons Instruct Inhibitory Interneuron Circuit Development in a Lineage-Dependent Manner
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper's findings directly support (not contradict) the claim that Satb2 knockout reprograms IT neurons toward PT identity and selectively disrupts CGE-derived interneuron lamination and circuit integration; the assigned role should be 'supports' rather than 'contradicts'.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Conditional removal of all AMPAR subunits (GluA1/2/3 cKO) in CGE-derived interneurons increases their early postnatal numbers but causes later apoptosis to converge with wild-type, almost completely eliminates sEPSCs, and reduces feedforward and feedback inhibition onto pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Akgul2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: The Role of AMPARs in the Maturation and Integration of Caudal Ganglionic Eminence-Derived Interneurons into Developing Hippocampal Microcircuits
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper's findings directly support the claim rather than contradict it; the abstract describes increased early postnatal numbers with later apoptosis convergence, near-complete sEPSC elimination, and decreased feedforward/feedback inhibition in GluA1-3 KO — all consistent with the claim.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Schematic methods-only summary of the cross-species CGE-lineage comparison. **(A)** Reported fraction of cortical GABAergic neurons assigned to the CGE/5-HT3AR/ADARB2 class — mouse fate-mapping (Mash1/Olig2-CreER) gives ~30% across whole cortex {cite:p}`Miyoshi2010`; human snRNA-seq in MTG gives ~50% (LHX6+ MGE 44% / ADARB2+ CGE 50%) {cite:p}`Hodge2019`. **(B)** The proposed partitioning across VIP / Lamp5 / Sncg / Serpinf1 subclasses across mouse, marmoset, macaque and human is shown as a methods placeholder only: no audited cross-paper panel was achievable for this stacked comparison because the underlying datasets use non-matched subclass definitions and reference areas {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Hodge2019,Krienen2023,Schmitz2022`. **(C)** Side timeline of the CGE-progenitor TF cascade — Sp8/Sp9 → COUP-TFII → PROX1 → postmitotic Prox1 / Elfn1 / activity-dependent refinement — with conditional-KO outcome annotations {cite:p}`Wei2019,Kanatani2008,Miyoshi2015,Stachniak2021,Demarcogarcia2011,Karayannis2012,Akgul2019`. *Caveat:* No quantitative cross-paper comparison was achievable for this comparison; the figure is methods-only/schematic, with the directional claim (mouse < primate CGE-class fraction) supported but the precise gap dependent on measurement modality.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: The Role of AMPARs in the Maturation and Integration of Caudal Ganglionic Eminence-Derived Interneurons into Developing Hippocampal Microcircuits
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper's findings on conditional-KO outcomes in CGE-derived interneurons support the claim's content rather than contradict it; the abstract describes AMPAR cKO effects on CGE interneuron maturation consistent with what is cited in the timeline.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The complementary excitatory side is captured by {cite:t}`Simacek2025b`, which described a sequential P3 → P30+ pattern in mouse S1 barrel cortex in which mEPSC frequency rises before P8–10, intrinsic firing matures next, and IPSC kinetics consolidate last.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Postnatal development of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing <scp>GABAergic</scp> interneurons in mouse somatosensory cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: The abstract describes mEPSC frequency increasing before P8-10 and mIPSC kinetics accelerating at P14-16, but does not characterize this as a strict sequential order where 'intrinsic firing matures next' between mEPSC frequency rise and IPSC kinetics consolidation. Additionally, the paper studies VIP-INs (inhibitory interneurons), so calling this 'the complementary excitatory side' mischaracterizes the paper's focus.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: While VIP cells were not the primary focus, the experiment illustrates the principle that experience-dependent neuromodulator drive and CGE-class circuit function are functionally coupled during postnatal critical periods, with implications for VIP-driven disinhibitory motifs that emerge in the same window {cite:p}`Chattopadhyaya2004`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Experience and Activity-Dependent Maturation of Perisomatic GABAergic Innervation in Primary Visual Cortex during a Postnatal Critical Period
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper studies perisomatic GABAergic (basket cell) innervation maturation in visual cortex, focusing on MGE-derived PV+ basket cells, not CGE-class interneurons or VIP cells. It does not address neuromodulator drive, CGE-class circuit function, or VIP-driven disinhibitory motifs.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Priya2018` showed that all ventral-eminence-derived cortical interneurons — including CGE-derived cells — undergo PCD, but that VIP interneuron survival, in contrast to MGE-derived PV/SST classes, is not activity-dependent; calcineurin in interneurons sequentially controls morphological maturation (E15–P5) and subsequent activity-dependent processes {cite:p}`Priya2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Activity Regulates Cell Death within Cortical Interneurons through a Calcineurin-Dependent Mechanism
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper's findings are accurately described in the claim, but the claim assigns a 'contradicts' role whereas the paper actually supports the statements made (CGE-derived cells undergo PCD, VIP survival is not activity-dependent, calcineurin sequentially controls maturation and survival).

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Priya2018` showed that all ventral-eminence-derived cortical interneurons — including CGE-derived cells — undergo PCD, but that VIP interneuron survival, in contrast to MGE-derived PV/SST classes, is not activity-dependent; calcineurin in interneurons sequentially controls morphological maturation (E15–P5) and subsequent activity-dependent processes {cite:p}`Priya2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Activity Regulates Cell Death within Cortical Interneurons through a Calcineurin-Dependent Mechanism
needs_fix_triples.note: Same issue: the claim accurately summarizes the paper but assigns it a 'contradicts' role; the paper directly supports these findings rather than contradicting them.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The hippocampal CGE neurogenic wave that produces these populations is itself temporally protracted from E12 to E16 {cite:p}`Tricoire2011`, generating not only IS3-type VIP cells but also nNOS+ NGC and Ivy populations from a shared CGE/MGE-mixed origin {cite:p}`Tricoire2010`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper shows nNOS+ NGC and Ivy cells arise from MGE (not CGE) progenitors; only nNOS-negative NGCs come from CGE. The claim that nNOS+ NGC and Ivy populations have a 'shared CGE/MGE-mixed origin' misrepresents the paper's findings.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The hippocampal CGE wave (E12–E16) generates the canonical IS3-type VIP/CR/CCK CA1 interneuron alongside CCK+ basket and NGC populations {cite:p}`Tricoire2011,Tricoire2010,Fuentealba2010`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper shows only nNOS-negative NGCs arise from CGE; nNOS+ NGCs and Ivy cells derive from MGE. Listing NGC populations as part of the CGE wave without distinguishing nNOS status is misleading.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Reinchisi2012` confirmed that COUP-TFII expression peaks in human CGE during gestational weeks 9–22 with a temporo-occipital > frontal/dorsal gradient. scRNA-seq of human ganglionic eminences identifies regional and temporal progenitor diversity and an evolutionarily conserved transcriptional grammar but also human-specific transcriptomic features {cite:p}`Shi2021`, while {cite:t}`Velmeshev2023` profiled >700,000 nuclei across 106 prenatal/postnatal human cortex donors and prioritised interneuron lineages as key vulnerable trajectories.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Mouse and human share conserved transcriptional programs for interneuron development
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim attributes 'human-specific transcriptomic features' to this paper, but the paper's abstract emphasizes evolutionarily conserved transcriptional programs between mouse and human, not human-specific features.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Reinchisi2012` confirmed that COUP-TFII expression peaks in human CGE during gestational weeks 9–22 with a temporo-occipital > frontal/dorsal gradient. scRNA-seq of human ganglionic eminences identifies regional and temporal progenitor diversity and an evolutionarily conserved transcriptional grammar but also human-specific transcriptomic features {cite:p}`Shi2021`, while {cite:t}`Velmeshev2023` profiled >700,000 nuclei across 106 prenatal/postnatal human cortex donors and prioritised interneuron lineages as key vulnerable trajectories.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Single-cell analysis of prenatal and postnatal human cortical development
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim asserts that Velmeshev2023 'prioritised interneuron lineages as key vulnerable trajectories,' but the abstract highlights autism-vulnerable cell types/lineages more broadly and specifically emphasizes excitatory cortical neurons and lineage-specific programs enriched for autism risk, not interneuron lineages specifically as the key vulnerable trajectories. Additionally, the cited role is 'contradicts,' but the paper does not contradict the claim's content; it partially supports it.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Jorstad2023b` confirmed across eight human cortical areas that 24 cell subclasses are highly consistent in presence but show areal differences in inhibitory-neuron sub-state composition.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic cytoarchitecture reveals principles of human neocortex organization
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper reports areal variation in excitatory (not inhibitory) neuron subclass proportions across areas; the abstract highlights differences in excitatory neuron proportions reflecting connectivity differences and laminar organization of glia, not inhibitory-neuron sub-state composition as stated.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Bershteyn2023` defined the directed-differentiation cascade (NKX2-1+/SOX1+/FOXG1+/DLX1/2+ → MGE progenitors → mature interneurons) for pallial MGE-type cells from human ESCs; analogous CGE protocols, exemplified by {cite:t}`Du2025`, generate ~80% calretinin+ cells via SHH activation plus IWP2-mediated WNT inhibition that show GABAergic action potentials in vitro and integrate after transplantation.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Directed and efficient generation of calretinin interneurons from human pluripotent stem cells
needs_fix_triples.note: The claim attributes the MGE-directed differentiation cascade (NKX2-1+/SOX1+/FOXG1+/DLX1/2+ → MGE progenitors → mature interneurons) to Bershteyn2023, but that cascade description is not established in Du2025's abstract; more importantly, the Du2025 protocol generates calretinin (CGE-like) interneurons via SHH/purmorphamine + IWP2, not MGE-type cells, so citing Du2025 as merely 'analogous CGE protocol' while attributing the MGE cascade to Bershteyn2023 may be mixing up the referenced contributio

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Garg2025` showed that mature CGE-IN subtypes arise from distinct spatial and temporal subdomains of the CGE — dorsal/ventral and rostral/caudal subregions and birthdate windows prefigure adult identities, mirroring the internal topography long appreciated in the MGE.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Mature interneuron subtypes arise from distinct spatial and temporal subdomains within the caudal ganglionic eminence
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper shows spatial biases in the CGE but explicitly states that temporal birthdate has a minimal relationship with interneuron subtype, contradicting the claim that 'birthdate windows prefigure adult identities.'

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Bright2025` complemented this with heterochronic transplantation, lineage tracing and perturb-seq experiments showing that progenitor competence is reset across embryonic time across all GEs, so that progenitor-stage temporal coding interacts with eminence-of-origin to set cardinal identity.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Temporal control of progenitor competence shapes maturation in GABAergic neuron development in mice
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper distinguishes maturation competence (which changes with temporal progression) from differentiation competence (which is maintained throughout neurogenesis). The claim states that 'progenitor competence is reset across embryonic time across all GEs,' but the paper finds maturation competence changes temporally while differentiation competence is preserved — it does not describe competence being 'reset.' Additionally, the paper does not emphasize that progenitor-stage temporal coding int

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Andrews2023` showed that dorsal forebrain organoids initially generate LGE-like INs at week 8 and progressively replace them with CGE-like INs by week 15, with LIF/LIFR signalling promoting outer radial glia identity in primary tissue.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: LIF signaling regulates outer radial glial to interneuron fate during human cortical development
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper shows LIF signaling regulates oRG proliferation and promotes interneuron production resembling CGE-derived INs, but the abstract does not describe a temporal switch from LGE-like to CGE-like INs at specific timepoints (week 8 vs week 15) in dorsal forebrain organoids.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: First, all of the major rodent reconstructed datasets — {cite:t}`Pronneke2015`, {cite:t}`Gouwens2019`, {cite:t}`Gouwens2020a`, {cite:t}`Gliko2024`, and {cite:t}`Schneidermizell2025` — rely on VIP-Cre genetic labelling, which captures the *Vip*-expressing transcriptomic subclass but does not perfectly resolve VIP-peptide expression on a cell-by-cell basis; the difference matters most for rodent-to-primate translation, where the calretinin-positive analogue is the operational handle {cite:p}`Cauli2014,Medalla2023,Hodge2019,Fishell2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: High-throughput analysis of dendrite and axonal arbors reveals transcriptomic correlates of neuroanatomy
needs_fix_triples.note: Gliko2024 uses Patch-seq data on 813 inhibitory neurons; the abstract does not indicate reliance on VIP-Cre genetic labelling — Patch-seq identifies transcriptomic identity post hoc rather than requiring Cre-line pre-selection, so citing this paper as dependent on VIP-Cre labelling is inaccurate.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: **Morphological diversity of cortical VIP interneurons.** **(A)** Idealised exemplar morphologies — bipolar, bitufted, small-basket, and arcade — drawn to illustrate the canonical VIP morphological repertoire described by {cite:t}`Kawaguchi1996`, {cite:t}`Cauli2014`, and {cite:t}`Apicella2022`. **(B)** Textual side-by-side of predominant VIP morphological subtype reported in two genetically defined VIP-Cre datasets in mouse primary sensory neocortex: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` (S1 barrel) versus {cite:t}`Gouwens2020a` (VISp). *Caveat (verbatim, Phase 6 audit):* Render as textual side-by-side, not as a quantitative bar (per restructure_notes). | State explicitly that Pronneke reports laminar dominance in S1 barrel cortex while Gouwens reports subtype enumeration across Patch-seq met-types in VISp — entries are not on the same axis. | Note difference in cortical area (S1 vs VISp) and method (fixed-slice morphology vs Patch-seq morpho-electric). **(C)** Subcellular target distribution of VIP-axon boutons in mouse S1 barrel L2/3 (immuno-EM, n=31 synapses) from {cite:t}`Zhou2017`. *Caveat (verbatim, Phase 6 audit):* Specify the sample size (n=31 synapses, L2/3) directly in the panel. | Verify in the published Methods/Results that 80/13/7 are L2/3-specific values (not the cross-laminar overall reported in abstract).
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Subcellular Targeting of VIP Boutons in Mouse Barrel Cortex is Layer-Dependent and not Restricted to Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Zhou 2017 is cited with role=contradicts, but the claim uses it to show subcellular target distribution of VIP boutons — the paper directly supports this use rather than contradicting it.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The same study reported a striking layer-dependent shift: in L6, ~40% of VIP boutons synapsed onto somata, contrasting with 8% in L2/3 and 8% in L5 — indicating that the canonical "perisomatic basket cell" function is not absent from the VIP class but compartmentalised into deep layers, and aligning with the layer-specific axon innervation that defines Patch-seq met-types {cite:p}`Zhou2017,Gouwens2020a,Schneidermizell2025,Gamlin2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Subcellular Targeting of VIP Boutons in Mouse Barrel Cortex is Layer-Dependent and not Restricted to Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Zhou 2017 is cited with role=contradicts, but the claim describes its own finding (L6 ~40% somatic VIP boutons vs ~8% in L2/3 and L5) — the paper supports this claim rather than contradicting it.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The classical estimate that approximately 40% of cortical VIP interneurons co-express calretinin {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Cauli2014,Cauli1997,Demarcogarcia2011` was challenged by region-specific counts in mouse V1 layers 4 and 5 reporting ~52% VIP/CR overlap {cite:p}`Xu2010a,Gonchar2008,Rudy2011,Cauli2014`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Immunochemical characterization of inhibitory mouse cortical neurons: Three chemically distinct classes of inhibitory cells
needs_fix_triples.note: The abstract reports VIP/CR overlap of 34.2% averaged across all layers and regions in mouse cortex, not ~52% in layers 4 and 5 of V1; the specific claim of ~52% VIP/CR overlap is not supported and may conflict with the reported average.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Rudy2011` summarised the rodent literature as reporting approximately 40% of cortical VIP-positive interneurons co-expressing calretinin {cite:p}`Rudy2011`, while {cite:t}`Xu2010a` reported a significantly higher VIP/CR overlap (~52%) restricted to layers 4 and 5 of mouse V1 {cite:p}`Xu2010a`. **Resolution:** The two estimates are likely not contradictory: co-expression depends on cortical region, laminar position, and developmental stage, and both reports remain valid for their respective contexts {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Xu2010a,Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021`. :::
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Immunochemical characterization of inhibitory mouse cortical neurons: Three chemically distinct classes of inhibitory cells
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper reports average VIP/CR overlap of 34.2% across mouse cortex, not ~52% restricted to layers 4 and 5 of V1; the claim misattributes a ~52% figure to this paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Rudy2011` summarised the rodent literature as reporting approximately 40% of cortical VIP-positive interneurons co-expressing calretinin {cite:p}`Rudy2011`, while {cite:t}`Xu2010a` reported a significantly higher VIP/CR overlap (~52%) restricted to layers 4 and 5 of mouse V1 {cite:p}`Xu2010a`. **Resolution:** The two estimates are likely not contradictory: co-expression depends on cortical region, laminar position, and developmental stage, and both reports remain valid for their respective contexts {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Xu2010a,Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021`. :::
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Immunochemical characterization of inhibitory mouse cortical neurons: Three chemically distinct classes of inhibitory cells
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper reports average VIP/CR overlap of 34.2%, not ~52%; the claimed ~52% VIP/CR overlap restricted to V1 layers 4-5 is not supported by this paper's abstract.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Rudy2011` summarised the rodent literature as reporting approximately 40% of cortical VIP-positive interneurons co-expressing calretinin {cite:p}`Rudy2011`, while {cite:t}`Xu2010a` reported a significantly higher VIP/CR overlap (~52%) restricted to layers 4 and 5 of mouse V1 {cite:p}`Xu2010a`. **Resolution:** The two estimates are likely not contradictory: co-expression depends on cortical region, laminar position, and developmental stage, and both reports remain valid for their respective contexts {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Xu2010a,Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021`. :::
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Immunochemical characterization of inhibitory mouse cortical neurons: Three chemically distinct classes of inhibitory cells
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper reports average VIP/CR overlap of 34.2%, not ~52%; attributing a ~52% figure to this paper appears incorrect based on the abstract.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Medalla2023` reported by quantitative immunolabelling and 3D reconstruction that calretinin-positive interneurons — the rodent VIP-class analogue in primates — are markedly denser and more abundant in rhesus monkey V1 and frontal cortex than in mouse, while their somatodendritic morphologies and axonal targets remain comparable across species {cite:p}`Medalla2023,Hodge2019,Cauli2014,Cauli1997`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Comparative features of calretinin, calbindin, and parvalbumin expressing interneurons in mouse and monkey primary visual and frontal cortices
needs_fix_triples.note: The abstract confirms CR+ interneurons are denser in monkey and that CR+ somatodendritic morphology shows no species difference, but the paper does not address axonal targets; the claim that 'axonal targets remain comparable across species' is not supported by this paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Medalla2023` reported by quantitative immunolabelling and 3D reconstruction that calretinin-positive interneurons — the rodent VIP-class analogue in primates — are markedly denser and more abundant in rhesus monkey V1 and frontal cortex than in mouse, while their somatodendritic morphologies and axonal targets remain comparable across species {cite:p}`Medalla2023,Hodge2019,Cauli2014,Cauli1997`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Comparative features of calretinin, calbindin, and parvalbumin expressing interneurons in mouse and monkey primary visual and frontal cortices
needs_fix_triples.note: Same issue: density and morphology findings are supported, but the paper does not examine axonal targets, so the claim that axonal targets remain comparable across species is unsupported by this citation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The cross-region inheritance is mirrored by cross-species inheritance: {cite:t}`Medalla2023` reports that primate calretinin-positive interneurons consist of Cajal–Retzius cells, small double-bouquet cells, and most commonly bipolar cells that synapse onto dendritic shafts and spines of pyramidal neurons — preserving the bipolar morphology and the dendrite-targeting bias from rodent {cite:p}`Medalla2023,Cauli2014,Hodge2019,Cauli1997`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Comparative features of calretinin, calbindin, and parvalbumin expressing interneurons in mouse and monkey primary visual and frontal cortices
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper reports somatodendritic morphology of CR+ neurons (including bipolar forms) and finds no species difference in morphology, but it does not describe Cajal–Retzius cells, double-bouquet cells as separate categories, nor does it address synaptic targeting onto dendritic shafts and spines; those details are not within this paper's scope.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The cross-region inheritance is mirrored by cross-species inheritance: {cite:t}`Medalla2023` reports that primate calretinin-positive interneurons consist of Cajal–Retzius cells, small double-bouquet cells, and most commonly bipolar cells that synapse onto dendritic shafts and spines of pyramidal neurons — preserving the bipolar morphology and the dendrite-targeting bias from rodent {cite:p}`Medalla2023,Cauli2014,Hodge2019,Cauli1997`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Comparative features of calretinin, calbindin, and parvalbumin expressing interneurons in mouse and monkey primary visual and frontal cortices
needs_fix_triples.note: Same as above: the paper characterizes CR+ interneuron morphology and density but does not report on synaptic targets (dendritic shafts/spines) or classify subtypes as Cajal–Retzius or double-bouquet cells.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013` likewise reported substantial VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal neurons in auditory cortex, with the implication that direct inhibition must be considered alongside disinhibition. **Resolution:** the two reports agree that VIP→pyramidal inhibition is real and behaviourally relevant in some preparations; they disagree on whether the magnitude is large enough to compete with the canonical disinhibitory channel for control of pyramidal firing.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
needs_fix_triples.note: Pi2013 emphasizes VIP-mediated disinhibition (suppressing SST/PV interneurons), not 'substantial VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal neurons.' The abstract highlights disinhibitory control, not a direct VIP→pyramidal inhibitory channel.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Walker2016` confirmed at the paired-recording level that L2/3 V1 VIP cells exhibit an adapting firing pattern in direct contrast to PV fast-spiking — a fundamental intrinsic-physiology difference that subdivides the inhibitory taxonomy without specifying within-VIP class {cite:p}`Walker2016`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studies S1 (primary somatosensory cortex), not V1. It describes VIP→MC synaptic properties via paired recordings, not VIP firing patterns as adapting vs PV fast-spiking.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Walker2016` confirmed at the paired-recording level that L2/3 V1 VIP cells exhibit an adapting firing pattern in direct contrast to PV fast-spiking — a fundamental intrinsic-physiology difference that subdivides the inhibitory taxonomy without specifying within-VIP class {cite:p}`Walker2016`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studies mouse primary somatosensory cortex (S1), not V1. Also, VIP firing pattern details are not described in the abstract; the focus is on synaptic properties onto Martinotti cells.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Walker2016` showed that paired V1 recordings preserve the adapting/IS distinction in vivo {cite:p}`Walker2016`, and {cite:t}`Jackson2016` demonstrated that VIP cells are recruited heterogeneously across behavioural states with intrinsic-firing diversity that mirrors their state-dependent recruitment {cite:p}`Jackson2016`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
needs_fix_triples.note: Walker2016 studied PV and VIP inhibition onto Martinotti cells in somatosensory cortex using optogenetics/paired recordings, not paired V1 recordings preserving adapting/IS distinction in vivo.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Walker2016` showed that paired V1 recordings preserve the adapting/IS distinction in vivo {cite:p}`Walker2016`, and {cite:t}`Jackson2016` demonstrated that VIP cells are recruited heterogeneously across behavioural states with intrinsic-firing diversity that mirrors their state-dependent recruitment {cite:p}`Jackson2016`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
needs_fix_triples.note: Walker2016 studied PV/VIP inhibition of Martinotti cells in somatosensory cortex using optogenetics/paired recordings, not paired V1 recordings preserving adapting/IS distinction in vivo.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013` likewise emphasised long-range cortical drive in auditory cortex, while {cite:t}`Fu2014` reported that nicotinic cholinergic input from basal forebrain was sufficient to drive locomotion-evoked VIP recruitment in the same area. **Resolution:** partial.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
needs_fix_triples.note: Fu2014 studied primary visual cortex (V1), not auditory cortex. The manuscript incorrectly states nicotinic cholinergic VIP recruitment occurred in 'the same area' as auditory cortex findings.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Yu2019` then argued, on the basis of high-throughput electrophysiology and synaptic mapping, that VIP→pyramidal contacts are systematic in some preparations and absent in others.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
needs_fix_triples.note: Yu2019 studied interneuron recruitment during tactile behavior via in vivo electrophysiology, not 'high-throughput electrophysiology and synaptic mapping' showing VIP→pyramidal contacts are 'systematic in some preparations and absent in others.'

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In V1, locomotion in darkness activates VIP cells and disinhibits pyramidal neurons through suppression of SST cells {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pakan2016, Sabri2024, Dipoppa2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 found locomotion activates VIP, SST, and PV neurons during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model. In darkness, SST neurons were largely non-responsive, contradicting 'suppression of SST cells.'

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, the same conjunction recurs: arousal- or reinforcer-related signals from basal forebrain or LC raise VIP activity, the rise is sufficient to suppress local SST output, and the SST suppression in turn opens a dendritic disinhibitory window onto pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Fu2014, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pi2013, Pakan2016, Sabri2024, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Kamigaki2017`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 found locomotion increases VIP, SST, and PV activity during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model. The manuscript cites it as supporting the VIP→SST suppression→disinhibition chain, which contradicts the paper's findings.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In V1, locomotion drives VIP cells to depolarise by tens of percent above baseline ΔF/F at running onset (Fu 2014; sample size not reported in the cited row) and to suppress concurrently imaged SST cells {cite:p}`Fu2014,Pakan2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Dipoppa2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 found locomotion INCREASES SST activity during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model. The claim that SST cells are suppressed contradicts their findings.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Independent replication has come from {cite:t}`Pi2013` in auditory cortex, {cite:t}`Lee2013` in barrel cortex, {cite:t}`Karnani2016a` and {cite:t}`Karnani2016b` in V1 with single-cell two-photon resolution, and {cite:t}`Sabri2024` in V1 chemogenetics.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity
needs_fix_triples.note: Sabri2024 used chemogenetic inhibition of VIP interneurons to study behavioral state modulation of cortical activity, not to replicate the VIP→SST disinhibitory connectivity motif claimed in the manuscript.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Lee2013` emphasised top-down glutamatergic projections from primary motor cortex (vM1) onto VIP cells in S1, and {cite:t}`Zhang2014` documented analogous projections from cingulate (and other higher cortical areas) onto V1 VIP cells; deleting these inputs blocked attentional disinhibition.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Long-range and local circuits for top-down modulation of visual cortex processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Zhang2014 shows cingulate projections activate GABAergic circuits (SOM and VIP) in V1, but the abstract does not state that deleting these inputs 'blocked attentional disinhibition.' That claim is unsupported/exaggerated.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Zhang2014` provided one of the cleanest causal demonstrations: optogenetic recruitment of VIP cells in mouse V1 during top-down attention enhanced pyramidal stimulus-evoked firing rates and abolished the suppressive effect of SST tone.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Long-range and local circuits for top-down modulation of visual cortex processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Zhang et al. 2014 activated cingulate (Cg) cortex projections, not VIP cells directly. VIP interneurons mediated center facilitation, but the optogenetic target was Cg neurons, not VIP cells in V1.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A monosynaptic-rabies survey by {cite:t}`Wall2016` formalised this point at scale: VIP cells in mouse V1 receive disproportionately more long-range cortical input — relative to local pyramidal input — than do SST or PV cells in the same preparation, an asymmetry that has since been reproduced across primary sensory cortices and prefrontal cortex {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Wall2016 studied somatosensory (barrel) cortex, not V1. The finding that VIP cells receive more long-range cortical/thalamic input than SST is correct, but the region is misattributed.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A monosynaptic-rabies survey by {cite:t}`Wall2016` formalised this point at scale: VIP cells in mouse V1 receive disproportionately more long-range cortical input — relative to local pyramidal input — than do SST or PV cells in the same preparation, an asymmetry that has since been reproduced across primary sensory cortices and prefrontal cortex {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Wall2016 studied somatosensory (barrel) cortex, not V1. The finding that VIP cells receive more long-range cortical/thalamic input than SST is correct, but the region attribution is wrong.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In a complementary slice study, {cite:t}`Zhou2017` reported direct VIP→pyramidal connectivity in mPFC L5 with paired recordings, and {cite:t}`Anastasiades2021a` confirmed that this projection is dense enough to be physiologically consequential during MD-thalamic recruitment of VIP cells.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Subcellular Targeting of VIP Boutons in Mouse Barrel Cortex is Layer-Dependent and not Restricted to Interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Zhou2017 used electron microscopy in barrel cortex, not paired recordings in mPFC L5. The manuscript misattributes method (paired recordings) and region (mPFC) to this paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Behaviourally, the same receptor route accounts for locomotion-driven VIP activation in V1: local nicotinic blockade abolishes locomotion-evoked VIP firing while local muscarinic blockade does not, and silencing basal-forebrain cholinergic projections produces the same effect {cite:p}`Fu2014, Letzkus2011, Sabri2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory microcircuit for associative fear learning in the auditory cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Letzkus2011 studied layer 1 interneurons in auditory cortex during fear conditioning, not VIP cells in V1. It does not address locomotion-driven VIP activation or nicotinic blockade abolishing locomotion-evoked VIP firing.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Conditional microRNA-pathway deletion in VIP+ cells produces qualitatively similar circuit-level dysfunction {cite:p}`Qiu2020, Batistabrito2017, Favuzzi2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Developmental Dysfunction of VIP Interneurons Impairs Cortical Circuits
needs_fix_triples.note: Batista-Brito et al. 2017 used conditional ErbB4 deletion in VIP cells, not a microRNA-pathway deletion. The claim misattributes the molecular manipulation used in this study.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The afferent picture mirrors the output one: lateral entorhinal cortex provides the dominant glutamatergic drive onto VIP/IS3 cells in stratum lacunosum-moleculare {cite:p}`Bilash2023, Turi2019, Tyan2014, Booker2018`, and arousal- or novelty-related signals robustly increase IS3 activity during free spatial exploration {cite:p}`Tamboli2024, Francavilla2018a, Turi2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Connectivity and network state-dependent recruitment of long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in the mouse hippocampus
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper reports VIP-LRPs decrease activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness. This contradicts the claim that novelty/arousal signals 'robustly increase IS3 activity during free spatial exploration.'

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A subset of VIP/CR interneurons in CA1 projects out of the hippocampus to the medial septum {cite:p}`Toth1993, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`, recapitulating the rare "GABAergic projection neuron" wiring described in earlier classical anatomy {cite:p}`Toth1993, Booker2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Connectivity and network state-dependent recruitment of long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in the mouse hippocampus
needs_fix_triples.note: Francavilla2018a describes VIP-LRP neurons projecting from CA1 to subiculum, not to medial septum. The manuscript incorrectly attributes a medial septum projection to this paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In hippocampus, VIP cells implement a structurally distinct motif: rather than VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition, CA1 VIP cells preferentially target SST OLM cells and PV basket cells, producing temporally precise theta-rhythmic disinhibition that gates input-specific plasticity {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Connectivity and network state-dependent recruitment of long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in the mouse hippocampus
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper describes VIP-LRPs targeting interneurons in CA1 and both interneurons and pyramidal cells in subiculum. The claim about theta-rhythmic disinhibition gating input-specific plasticity is not supported; VIP-LRPs actually decrease activity during theta-run epochs.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: **Hippocampal VIP→OLM and VIP→basket-cell disinhibition.** In CA1 and dentate gyrus, VIP cells, including the calretinin-positive and CCK-positive subsets, form a structurally distinct motif: VIP/CR cells inhibit OLM cells (the SST analogue) and basket cells (the PV analogue) in a temporally precise, theta-rhythmic manner that gates Schaffer-collateral and entorhinal input streams {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020,Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Connectivity and network state-dependent recruitment of long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in the mouse hippocampus
needs_fix_triples.note: Francavilla2018a describes VIP long-range projection neurons targeting interneurons and pyramidal cells in CA1/subiculum, not specifically VIP/CR→OLM or basket cell theta-rhythmic disinhibition gating input streams.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Detailed paired-recording, electron-microscopic and intersectional optogenetic dissection of the IS3 class shows that — unlike its cortical VIP counterpart — its principal output is not the SST cell but the *oriens-lacunosum moleculare* (OLM) interneuron, with strong, facilitating IPSCs that suppress OLM firing during sharp-wave ripples and during theta-rhythmic dendritic input from entorhinal cortex {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Francavilla2018a, Tamboli2024, Turi2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
needs_fix_triples.note: Tyan2014 reports IS3→OLM IPSCs have small amplitude and low release probability, not 'strong, facilitating IPSCs.' The manuscript's characterization contradicts the abstract's findings.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In A1, reinforcement signals from basal forebrain recruit VIP→SST disinhibition during associative learning {cite:p}`Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Adaptive disinhibitory gating by VIP interneurons permits associative learning
needs_fix_triples.note: Krabbe2019 studied VIP→SST disinhibition in basolateral amygdala during fear conditioning, not in A1. The manuscript incorrectly attributes the finding to auditory cortex (A1).

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Granger2020` and {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` showed that cortical ChAT/VIP cells (~33% of VIP cells in mPFC) release ACh in addition to GABA, with target-specific GABA release that is robust onto interneurons and weaker onto pyramidal cells, and {cite:t}`Vonengelhardt2007` and {cite:t}`Dudai2020` demonstrated that the same cells provide a local cortical source of nicotinic excitation onto neighbouring fast-spiking and bipolar interneurons.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Functional Characterization of Intrinsic Cholinergic Interneurons in the Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper found nicotinic excitation onto pyramidal neurons via presynaptic mechanisms increasing spontaneous EPSCs, not onto fast-spiking or bipolar interneurons as the manuscript claims.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Cortical VIP and CCK interneurons are selectively depolarised by nicotinic agonists, whereas pyramidal cells, fast-spiking PV cells and SST cells are not — a result first established by {cite:t}`Porter1999` and {cite:t}`Kawaguchi1997b` and replicated across rodent neocortex with subtype-specific receptor profiling {cite:p}`Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Selective Cholinergic Modulation of Cortical GABAergic Cell Subtypes
needs_fix_triples.note: Kawaguchi1997b used muscarinic/cholinergic agonists (carbachol, muscarine), not nicotinic agonists. SST cells were depolarized, not unaffected. The claim misattributes nicotinic selectivity to this paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Layered on top is a smaller but reproducible muscarinic component: a subset of VIP cells is directly *inhibited* by mAChR activation in slice {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1997b, Obermayer2019, Granger2020`, and a further subset of VIP interneurons themselves co-release ACh and GABA — making them not just modulator targets but local cortical sources of ACh {cite:p}`Granger2020, Obermayer2019, Vonengelhardt2007, Dudai2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Selective Cholinergic Modulation of Cortical GABAergic Cell Subtypes
needs_fix_triples.note: Kawaguchi1997b found VIP cells were depolarized with spike firing by cholinergic agonists, not inhibited. Only larger CCK cells were hyperpolarized. The claim of direct mAChR inhibition of VIP cells is inconsistent.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Cluster_13 of the cholinergic literature contains an explicit opposite-sign disagreement: {cite:t}`Arroyo2012` and convergent work establish that the dominant ACh effect on cortical VIP / 5-HT3AR cells is nicotinic excitation through slow, β2-containing nAChRs {cite:p}`Arroyo2012, Porter1999, Fu2014`, while {cite:t}`Poorthuis2018` and matched studies emphasise a muscarinic *inhibition* of a VIP subset {cite:p}`Poorthuis2018, Kawaguchi1997b, Obermayer2019, Granger2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Selective Cholinergic Modulation of Cortical GABAergic Cell Subtypes
needs_fix_triples.note: Kawaguchi 1997 found VIP cells were depolarized/excited by muscarinic agonists, not inhibited. Only larger CCK cells were hyperpolarized. Paper does not support muscarinic inhibition of VIP subset.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In hippocampus, VIP cells implement a structurally distinct motif: rather than VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition, CA1 VIP cells preferentially target SST OLM cells and PV basket cells, producing temporally precise theta-rhythmic disinhibition that gates input-specific plasticity {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Barrel cortex VIP/ChAT interneurons suppress sensory responses in vivo
needs_fix_triples.note: Dudai2020 studies VIP/ChAT interneurons in barrel cortex, not hippocampus. The claim attributes hippocampal CA1 VIP cell findings to this paper, which is incorrect.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: **Hippocampal VIP→OLM and VIP→basket-cell disinhibition.** In CA1 and dentate gyrus, VIP cells, including the calretinin-positive and CCK-positive subsets, form a structurally distinct motif: VIP/CR cells inhibit OLM cells (the SST analogue) and basket cells (the PV analogue) in a temporally precise, theta-rhythmic manner that gates Schaffer-collateral and entorhinal input streams {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020,Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Barrel cortex VIP/ChAT interneurons suppress sensory responses in vivo
needs_fix_triples.note: Dudai2020 studies VIP/ChAT interneurons in barrel cortex (neocortex), not hippocampal CA1/dentate gyrus. The claim attributes hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket-cell disinhibition to this paper, which is incorrect.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Noradrenergic recruitment is the least monolithic of the three: α-adrenergic stimulation excites a fraction of VIP/CR cells in slice {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1998, Cauli2014, Caputi2009`, while β-adrenergic and tonic LC-driven effects vary across area, behavioural state and recording configuration {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1998, Cauli2014, Letzkus2011, Szadai2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Noradrenergic Excitation and Inhibition of GABAergic Cell Types in Rat Frontal Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Kawaguchi1998 studied effects on fast-spiking, late-spiking, somatostatin, and cholecystokinin cells. VIP/CR cells were not specifically identified or examined. The claim about α-adrenergic excitation of VIP/CR cells is not supported.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Noradrenergic recruitment is the least monolithic of the three: α-adrenergic stimulation excites a fraction of VIP/CR cells in slice {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1998, Cauli2014, Caputi2009`, while β-adrenergic and tonic LC-driven effects vary across area, behavioural state and recording configuration {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1998, Cauli2014, Letzkus2011, Szadai2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Noradrenergic Excitation and Inhibition of GABAergic Cell Types in Rat Frontal Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Kawaguchi1998 does not mention VIP/CR cells. It reports α-adrenergic excitation of somatostatin cells and heterogeneous effects on CCK cells, not VIP/CR interneurons specifically.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Ferezou2002` reported that 5-HT3 receptors mediate fast serotonergic EPSPs selectively in CCK/VIP-co-expressing GABAergic interneurons {cite:p}`Ferezou2002`, and {cite:t}`Chen2015a` showed that VIP and L1 interneurons in mouse V1 are facilitated by ACh at lower agonist doses than SOM neurons {cite:p}`Chen2015a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: An acetylcholine-activated microcircuit drives temporal dynamics of cortical activity
needs_fix_triples.note: The abstract states VIP neurons are activated at HIGH levels of cholinergic drive, not lower doses. The claim reverses the dose-sensitivity relationship described in the paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Mardinly2016` made a parallel point with conditional Igf1 deletion in VIP neurons: increased inhibitory drive specifically onto VIP cells decreases their firing, establishing experience-dependent regulation of VIP intrinsic firing through a synaptic, not channel-intrinsic, route {cite:p}`Mardinly2016`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Sensory experience regulates cortical inhibition by inducing IGF1 in VIP neurons
needs_fix_triples.note: The manuscript says Igf1 deletion decreased VIP firing by increasing inhibitory drive onto them. The paper actually shows IGF1 *increases* inhibitory input onto VIP neurons; deletion would reduce, not increase, that input.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Mardinly2016` made a parallel point with conditional Igf1 deletion in VIP neurons: increased inhibitory drive specifically onto VIP cells decreases their firing, establishing experience-dependent regulation of VIP intrinsic firing through a synaptic, not channel-intrinsic, route {cite:p}`Mardinly2016`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Sensory experience regulates cortical inhibition by inducing IGF1 in VIP neurons
needs_fix_triples.note: The manuscript reverses the finding. Mardinly2016 showed IGF1 increases inhibitory input onto VIP neurons (not that Igf1 deletion increases it). Also, the claim mischaracterizes VIP firing decrease as the main finding.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Symmetric synapses identifiable as VIP+ in immuno-EM target dendritic shafts and perisomatic compartments of CR+, CB+ and SOM+ interneurons in cat and rat visual cortex with substantially higher frequency than they target pyramidal somata {cite:p}`Hajos1996, Defelipe1999`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Target Selectivity and Neurochemical Characteristics of VIP-immunoreactive Interneurons in the Rat Dentate Gyrus
needs_fix_triples.note: Hajos1996 studied VIP interneurons in rat dentate gyrus, not visual cortex. It describes VIP+ targeting of interneurons and granule cells, not CR+/CB+/SOM+ interneurons in visual cortex via immuno-EM.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The pattern dovetails with the light-microscopic paired-recording data: ultrastructurally, VIP outputs converge on interneuron dendrites and proximal compartments rather than on pyramidal somata, and the small fraction of pyramidal-targeting boutons preferentially contacts apical dendrites in deep layers {cite:p}`Hajos1996, Defelipe1999`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Target Selectivity and Neurochemical Characteristics of VIP-immunoreactive Interneurons in the Rat Dentate Gyrus
needs_fix_triples.note: Hajos1996 studied VIP interneurons in rat dentate gyrus, not visual cortex. The claim attributes immuno-EM findings about VIP outputs in visual cortex to this paper, which is incorrect.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Cadwell2016` and {cite:t}`Fuzik2016` independently demonstrated the technique on cortical interneurons and showed concordance between firing-pattern class and transcriptomic class at the L1 level (n=67 cells in {cite:t}`Cadwell2016`).
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Electrophysiological, transcriptomic and morphologic profiling of single neurons using Patch-seq
needs_fix_triples.note: The manuscript claims n=67 cells for Cadwell2016, but the abstract states 58 neocortical cells were profiled. The technique description and concordance claim are broadly consistent.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Cadwell2016` and {cite:t}`Fuzik2016` independently demonstrated the technique on cortical interneurons and showed concordance between firing-pattern class and transcriptomic class at the L1 level (n=67 cells in {cite:t}`Cadwell2016`).
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Electrophysiological, transcriptomic and morphologic profiling of single neurons using Patch-seq
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper reports 58 neocortical cells, not 67. The claim attributes n=67 to Cadwell2016, which contradicts the abstract's stated sample size of 58.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Disrupting this refinement — by conditional deletion of ErbB4 in VIP cells {cite:p}`Batistabrito2017`, by microRNA deletion {cite:p}`Qiu2020` or by neonatal hypoxic-ischaemic injury {cite:p}`Abbah2022` — produces persistent miswiring of the disinhibitory motif into adulthood.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Oxidative Stress-Induced Damage to the Developing Hippocampus Is Mediated by GSK3
needs_fix_triples.note: Abbah2022 uses neonatal hyperoxia (not hypoxic-ischaemic injury) and studies hippocampal interneuron damage/GSK3β, not specifically miswiring of a disinhibitory motif into adulthood.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The picture from connectomics in primary visual cortex {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025` and from cross-species comparisons {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Vanderveer2021,Caulinorocha2022` is that VIP cells in different cortical areas and species have different output ratios, so there is no single answer to the question ‘how strong is VIP→pyramidal inhibition?’ in the way the canonical schematic implies.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Endogenous VIP VPAC1 Receptor Activation Modulates Hippocampal Theta Burst Induced LTP: Transduction Pathways and GABAergic Mechanisms
needs_fix_triples.note: Caulino-Rocha 2022 studies VIP modulation of hippocampal LTP via VPAC1 receptors, not cortical VIP cell output connectivity or cross-species VIP→pyramidal inhibition ratios in different cortical areas.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In primate and human cortex, comparative anatomy and Patch-seq data suggest that the VIP→SST bias is preserved but that the absolute density and laminar organisation of VIP cells differ substantially {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Vanderveer2021,Caulinorocha2022`, with implications discussed further in {ref}`sec-cross-areas`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Endogenous VIP VPAC1 Receptor Activation Modulates Hippocampal Theta Burst Induced LTP: Transduction Pathways and GABAergic Mechanisms
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper studies rat hippocampal slices, not primate/human cortex. It has no comparative anatomy, Patch-seq data, or analysis of VIP cell density/laminar organisation in cortex.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In hippocampus and amygdala, analogous VIP-driven disinhibitory motifs have been described in fear learning and memory updating {cite:p}`Krabbe2019,Turi2019,Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: VIP-expressing interneurons in the anterior insular cortex contribute to sensory processing to regulate adaptive behavior
needs_fix_triples.note: The paper studies VIP interneurons in anterior insular cortex (aIC), not hippocampus or amygdala. The claim attributes a hippocampus/amygdala VIP-driven disinhibitory motif to this paper, which is incorrect regarding brain region.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: **Hippocampal VIP→OLM and VIP→basket-cell disinhibition.** In CA1 and dentate gyrus, VIP cells, including the calretinin-positive and CCK-positive subsets, form a structurally distinct motif: VIP/CR cells inhibit OLM cells (the SST analogue) and basket cells (the PV analogue) in a temporally precise, theta-rhythmic manner that gates Schaffer-collateral and entorhinal input streams {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020,Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: VIP-expressing interneurons in the anterior insular cortex contribute to sensory processing to regulate adaptive behavior
needs_fix_triples.note: Ramos-Prats 2022 studies VIP interneurons in anterior insular cortex, not hippocampal CA1/dentate gyrus. The claim attributes hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket-cell disinhibition to this paper, which is incorrect.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In M1, VIP recruitment is tightly coupled to cholinergic drive during motor learning, and the canonical motif appears to gate dendritic spine plasticity in projection pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Cichon2017,Garciajuncoclemente2017`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: An inhibitory pull--push circuit in frontal cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Garcia-Junco-Clemente et al. 2017 studied frontal cortex, not M1 specifically, and focused on VIP pull-push inhibitory circuits during arousal, not motor learning or dendritic spine plasticity.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: As with nAChR excitation, the response is layer- and area-broad and is detectable in vivo as a short-latency increase in VIP firing following raphe stimulation {cite:p}`Frazer2017, Lee2010, Ressurreicao2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Rapid Disruption of Dishevelled Activity Uncovers an Intercellular Role in Maintenance of Prickle in Core Planar Polarity Protein Complexes
needs_fix_triples.note: The cited paper studies Dishevelled and planar polarity in tissue development (likely Drosophila), not serotonin, VIP interneurons, or raphe stimulation. This is a clear citation error.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In S1, top-down feedback in object detection and texture discrimination tasks recruits VIP cells whose suppression reduces task performance {cite:p}`Williams2019,Sermet2019,Bigelow2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Movement and VIP Interneuron Activation Differentially Modulate Encoding in Mouse Auditory Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Bigelow2019 studied auditory cortex (ACtx), not S1. It examined movement and VIP activation effects on encoding, not top-down feedback in object detection/texture discrimination tasks.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A parallel line of in-vivo work, originating with {cite:t}`Pi2013`, established that cortical VIP cells are recruited not only by motor state but by reinforcement: phasic reward and punishment delivered during operant behaviour produced rapid, large-amplitude VIP activation in auditory cortex, mPFC, and primary somatosensory cortex (S1), with optogenetic VIP inhibition disrupting performance on the discrimination task {cite:p}`Pi2013`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
needs_fix_triples.note: Same issue: role is 'contradicts' but claim recounts Pi2013's own findings. Also, S1 is not mentioned in Pi2013 abstract (only auditory and mPFC).

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Many *in vivo* perturbation studies use short, intense optogenetic activation that bypasses the gating logic by which VIP cells are normally recruited, producing larger and less selective effects than chemogenetic or behaviourally evoked recruitment {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Pakan2016,Kuchibhotla2017,Veit2023`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 used two-photon calcium imaging of behaviorally evoked responses, not optogenetic activation. It does not illustrate the short/intense optogenetic concern.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: First, the locomotion drive itself is not unconditional: {cite:t}`Pakan2016` reported that VIP↑/SST↓ antiphasic modulation in V1 holds only when concurrent visual stimuli are present, and that locomotion alone in darkness does not reliably recruit VIP cells, in contrast with the more invariant locomotion drive reported by {cite:t}`Dipoppa2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 found most VIP neurons REMAINED locomotion-responsive in darkness; claim says VIP cells were not reliably recruited in darkness.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pakan2016` reported that VIP locomotion modulation was context-dependent and effectively absent without visual stimuli, with VIP and SST showing antiphase modulation only when running was paired with visual drive.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract states most VIP neurons remained locomotion-responsive in darkness; claim that VIP modulation was 'effectively absent without visual stimuli' is wrong.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The picture from these and related studies {cite:p}`Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022,Veit2023,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b,Garciajuncoclemente2017` is that the canonical motif's *behavioural* contribution is largely about gating plasticity windows and sustaining attentional or learning-related disinhibition, not about producing transient gain on a millisecond-to-second timescale.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms
needs_fix_triples.note: Veit2023 shows VIP linearly scales gamma power locally — this is gain modulation, not plasticity/learning gating as the claim states.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The picture from these and related studies {cite:p}`Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022,Veit2023,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b,Garciajuncoclemente2017` is that the canonical motif's *behavioural* contribution is largely about gating plasticity windows and sustaining attentional or learning-related disinhibition, not about producing transient gain on a millisecond-to-second timescale. This is consistent with the kinetic profile of VIP→SST IPSCs, which is slower than VIP→PV {cite:p}`Pi2013`, and with the fact that VIP-cell perturbation typically produces slow-timescale changes in pyramidal firing rather than fast modulation of stimulus-evoked responses {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Kuchibhotla2017,Pinto2015,Veit2023`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms
