needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Bogaj and colleagues {cite:p}`Bogaj2025b` argued that "experimental disruption of VIP-IN function phenocopies behavioural abnormalities" of schizophrenia, strengthening the inference from correlative postmortem data.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of BK channels by GABAb receptors enhances intrinsic excitability of layer 2/3 vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons in mouse neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studies GABAbR modulation of VIP-INs in mouse cortex; no mention of schizophrenia or behavioural abnormalities.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The cross-disorder review of {cite:t}`Kiss2026` summarized that "dysregulation of PVALB-, SST-, and VIP-expressing interneurons has been implicated in a range of neuropsychiatric and neurodegenerative conditions including bipolar disorder, major depression, autism spectrum disorder, and Alzheimer's [disease]." We note that the most-discussed human-postmortem AD studies (the SEA-AD MTG snRNA-seq characterization of Vip+ inhibitory subtype loss in late-phase human AD, and recent neuropathology work reporting depletion of VIP subtypes) appear in the review plan's anticipated-conflicts list but were filtered out of the section's citation key map; we flag these as unmet citation needs and discuss their consequence below.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical GABAergic Neuron Dysregulation in Schizophrenia Is Age Dependent
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is an original SCZ study, not a cross-disorder review covering bipolar, MDD, ASD, and Alzheimer's disease.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The complementary human-postmortem account places this against single-nucleus transcriptomic evidence from the SEA-AD middle-temporal gyrus cohort {cite:p}`Gabitto2024` and from independent human-cortex postmortem profiling {cite:p}`Yi2025a`, both of which have been read as showing selective vulnerability of VIP-expressing inhibitory subtypes at late disease stage.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: From stress to Alzheimer's: A circuit-based framework for prefrontal cognitive dysfunction
needs_fix_triples.note: Yi2025a is a review of stress/AD PFC circuits, not a single-nucleus transcriptomic postmortem human-cortex profiling study of VIP-subtype vulnerability.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Resolution remains unsettled: mouse-model evidence favours circuit-functional dysregulation without overt cell loss, while contemporaneous human-postmortem snRNA-seq literature {cite:p}`Gabitto2024,Yi2025a` favours selective VIP-subtype depletion at late stage.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: From stress to Alzheimer's: A circuit-based framework for prefrontal cognitive dysfunction
needs_fix_triples.note: Yi2025a is a review paper, not a human-postmortem snRNA-seq study; it does not provide primary evidence of VIP-subtype depletion.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Cross-disease reviews acknowledge both readings without resolving them {cite:p}`Kiss2026,Gil2024,Bogaj2025b`. :::
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical GABAergic Neuron Dysregulation in Schizophrenia Is Age Dependent
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is not a cross-disease review; it is an original age-stratified SCZ study of GABAergic neuron dysregulation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Cross-disease reviews acknowledge both readings without resolving them {cite:p}`Kiss2026,Gil2024,Bogaj2025b`. :::
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of BK channels by GABAb receptors enhances intrinsic excitability of layer 2/3 vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons in mouse neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is an electrophysiology study of GABAbR/BK channels in VIP-INs, not a cross-disease review.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Three readout modalities anchor the translation question and seldom agree. *Mouse circuit work* — the dominant source of evidence in §6–§10 and in the disease subsections above — emphasizes intrinsic excitability, firing rate, synaptic transmission, and behavioural consequences in the intact brain {cite:p}`Goff2023,Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Chen2023b,Rahmatullah2023`. *Human postmortem* work, by contrast, is restricted to transcriptomic / morphological / proteomic readouts in fixed tissue: the human MTG, M1, and motor-cortex atlases of {cite:p}`Hodge2019,Bakken2021a,Lee2023a,Lake2018,Teymornejad2024a` provide quantitative cell-type proportions, t-type identities, and gene-expression profiles, but cannot directly access circuit-functional phenotypes. *Human iPSC-derived* and *neurosurgical-tissue* approaches {cite:p}`Somogyi2025,Lattke2026,Matthews2025` partially close the gap by providing live human GABAergic neurons that can be patched and manipulated, but currently report transcriptional, morphological, and single-cell-physiology phenotypes that do not yet map cleanly onto the mouse-model behavioural readouts.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Signature morphoelectric properties of diverse GABAergic interneurons in the human neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Lee2023a uses live neurosurgical tissue with Patch-seq (electrophysiology + scRNA-seq), not postmortem-only fixed tissue.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Three readout modalities anchor the translation question and seldom agree. *Mouse circuit work* — the dominant source of evidence in §6–§10 and in the disease subsections above — emphasizes intrinsic excitability, firing rate, synaptic transmission, and behavioural consequences in the intact brain {cite:p}`Goff2023,Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Chen2023b,Rahmatullah2023`. *Human postmortem* work, by contrast, is restricted to transcriptomic / morphological / proteomic readouts in fixed tissue: the human MTG, M1, and motor-cortex atlases of {cite:p}`Hodge2019,Bakken2021a,Lee2023a,Lake2018,Teymornejad2024a` provide quantitative cell-type proportions, t-type identities, and gene-expression profiles, but cannot directly access circuit-functional phenotypes. *Human iPSC-derived* and *neurosurgical-tissue* approaches {cite:p}`Somogyi2025,Lattke2026,Matthews2025` partially close the gap by providing live human GABAergic neurons that can be patched and manipulated, but currently report transcriptional, morphological, and single-cell-physiology phenotypes that do not yet map cleanly onto the mouse-model behavioural readouts.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Giant pyramidal neurons of the primary motor cortex express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP), a known marker of cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is on marmoset motor cortex, not human. Cited as a human postmortem atlas, but species is wrong.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Three readout modalities anchor the translation question and seldom agree. *Mouse circuit work* — the dominant source of evidence in §6–§10 and in the disease subsections above — emphasizes intrinsic excitability, firing rate, synaptic transmission, and behavioural consequences in the intact brain {cite:p}`Goff2023,Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Chen2023b,Rahmatullah2023`. *Human postmortem* work, by contrast, is restricted to transcriptomic / morphological / proteomic readouts in fixed tissue: the human MTG, M1, and motor-cortex atlases of {cite:p}`Hodge2019,Bakken2021a,Lee2023a,Lake2018,Teymornejad2024a` provide quantitative cell-type proportions, t-type identities, and gene-expression profiles, but cannot directly access circuit-functional phenotypes. *Human iPSC-derived* and *neurosurgical-tissue* approaches {cite:p}`Somogyi2025,Lattke2026,Matthews2025` partially close the gap by providing live human GABAergic neurons that can be patched and manipulated, but currently report transcriptional, morphological, and single-cell-physiology phenotypes that do not yet map cleanly onto the mouse-model behavioural readouts.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Single-cell atlas of the developing Down syndrome brain cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Lattke2026 is about DS fetal cortex scRNA-seq/scATAC-seq, not iPSC-derived or neurosurgical human models as cited in this sentence context.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: :::{admonition} Conflict — mouse circuit phenotype vs human iPSC readout :class: conflict {cite:t}`Simacek2025b` and the broader human iPSC literature emphasize *transcriptional* and *morphological* phenotypes that do not always predict the *intrinsic-excitability* and *synaptic-transmission* phenotypes that {cite:t}`Goff2023` and other mouse-model studies emphasize.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Postnatal development of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing <scp>GABAergic</scp> interneurons in mouse somatosensory cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Simacek2025b emphasizes intrinsic-excitability and synaptic-transmission phenotypes, not transcriptional/morphological ones.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: At the *disease* level, multiple disorders converge on VIP-IN dysfunction {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Chen2023b,Bhandari2024,Goff2023,Hanno2026,Vogt2014,Michaud2024,Miller2025,Batistabrito2017,Kiss2026,Gil2024`, but the *direction*, *magnitude*, and *circuit locus* of the deficit are heterogeneous and must be reported as such.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Lhx6 Directly Regulates Arx and CXCR7 to Determine Cortical Interneuron Fate and Laminar Position
needs_fix_triples.note: Vogt 2014 addresses Lhx6/MGE specification, not VIP-IN dysfunction in disease models.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Bars show two mismatched-denominator anchors: VIP+ as % of GABA+ cells in P9 rat primary auditory cortex {cite:p}`Ouellet2014` (8%, IHC), and VIP+ as % of total neurons in human M1 cytoarchitectural subdivisions A4a / A4b / A4c {cite:p}`Teymornejad2024a` (4.5% / 5.2% / 6.6%, RNAscope+IHC). **Caveat (audited):** the two species use *different denominators* (GABA+ vs total neurons), *different ages* (P9 vs adult postmortem), and *different methods*; the apparent quantitative similarity in the 5–10% range masks methodological non-comparability. **(B)** Cross-disease, cross-region, cross-axis qualitative direction-of-change in autism-spectrum mouse models.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Giant pyramidal neurons of the primary motor cortex express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP), a known marker of cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studied marmoset, not human M1. Percentages 4.5/5.2/6.6% not in abstract; abstract reports ~5-7%. Species mismatch.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The right panel cartoons the mouse-vs-human VIP-IN comparison anchored to {cite:t}`Hodge2019` and {cite:t}`Yao2023`: subclass conserved, t-type expansion in primate L1, human-specific *VIP PCDH20* and *MC4R* types {cite:p}`Chartrand2023,Boldog2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and morphophysiological evidence for a specialized human cortical GABAergic cell type
needs_fix_triples.note: Boldog2018 describes rosehip cells (GAD1+CCK+), not VIP PCDH20 or MC4R types specifically.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: First, the *VIP-Cre* driver line and its cross-species analogues do not capture identical populations: in mouse, VIP-Cre and VIP-IRES-Cre labelled cells overlap heavily with the transcriptomic *Vip* subclass {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Yao2023,Tasic2016`, but the human equivalent lacks a matched genetic-driver tool, and human VIP characterization relies on RNAscope, IHC, and enhancer-AAV approaches {cite:p}`Teymornejad2024a,Lee2023a,Matthews2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: RNA-programmable cell-type monitoring and manipulation in the human cortex with CellREADR
needs_fix_triples.note: Matthews2025 uses CellREADR (RNA sensor-effector), not RNAscope, IHC, or enhancer-AAV approaches for human VIP characterization.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Third, the VIP-IN role within the broader 5-HT3AR+ CGE-derived population varies across reports (~40% of the 5-HT3AR population in S1 by {cite:t}`Rudy2011`, with related estimates in {cite:p}`Hu2024`), and species-specific 5-HT3AR distribution complicates direct transfer.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Characterization of GABAergic marker expression in prefrontal cortex in dexamethasone induced depression/anxiety model
needs_fix_triples.note: Hu2024 does not report VIP as a proportion of the 5-HT3AR+ population; it only measures VIP expression reduction in PFC after DEX.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Fourth, the human neurosurgical-tissue and postmortem-tissue preparations used by {cite:p}`Somogyi2025,Boldog2018,Lake2018,Hodge2019,Lee2023a` are not interchangeable: neurosurgical samples allow live recording but are biased toward epilepsy / tumour cohorts, and postmortem material allows unbiased anatomical sampling but precludes live functional readouts.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Integrative single-cell analysis of transcriptional and epigenetic states in the human adult brain
needs_fix_triples.note: Lake2018 used postmortem tissue only (not neurosurgical); claim groups it with neurosurgical studies, but the sentence distinguishes both types, so partial match. Actually the sentence says both types are 'not interchangeable' and lists papers using either. Lake2018 is postmortem, which fits.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Fifth, mouse-model phenotypes are typically reported as point estimates with small *n* per condition and large between-laboratory variance; the behavioural-phenotype translation literature surveyed by {cite:p}`Voelkl2022` argues for systematic between-laboratory replication as a corrective.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Distinct histological alterations of cortical interneuron types in mouse models of Huntington's disease
needs_fix_triples.note: Wrong paper: this Voelkl 2022 is about cortical interneuron histology in HD mice, not about between-laboratory replication methodology.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Sixth, the developmental window in which VIP-INs are sampled differs across species: rodent characterizations frequently use juvenile (P14–P28) animals {cite:p}`Ouellet2014,Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Pakan2016,Mcfarlan2024a`, human samples are typically adult or fetal {cite:p}`Teymornejad2024a,Lattke2026,Hodge2019,Lake2018`, and disease penetrance is itself developmental {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Chen2023b,Lattke2026,Vormsteinschneider2020,Simacek2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Trajectory of the main GABAergic interneuron populations from early development to old age in the rat primary auditory cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Ouellet2014 sampled P9 through old age, not specifically P14-P28 juvenile. Calling it a 'juvenile' characterization is misleading.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Sixth, the developmental window in which VIP-INs are sampled differs across species: rodent characterizations frequently use juvenile (P14–P28) animals {cite:p}`Ouellet2014,Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Pakan2016,Mcfarlan2024a`, human samples are typically adult or fetal {cite:p}`Teymornejad2024a,Lattke2026,Hodge2019,Lake2018`, and disease penetrance is itself developmental {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Chen2023b,Lattke2026,Vormsteinschneider2020,Simacek2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 used adult mice, not juvenile P14–P28; no mention of developmental window in abstract.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Sixth, the developmental window in which VIP-INs are sampled differs across species: rodent characterizations frequently use juvenile (P14–P28) animals {cite:p}`Ouellet2014,Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Pakan2016,Mcfarlan2024a`, human samples are typically adult or fetal {cite:p}`Teymornejad2024a,Lattke2026,Hodge2019,Lake2018`, and disease penetrance is itself developmental {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Chen2023b,Lattke2026,Vormsteinschneider2020,Simacek2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Giant pyramidal neurons of the primary motor cortex express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP), a known marker of cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studied marmoset, not human. Claim cites it as a human sample reference.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Putting the disease subsections side-by-side reveals a consistent upstream-to-downstream architecture that {numref}`fig-vip-disease-convergence` makes explicit. *Upstream genetic perturbations* span ion channels (*SCN1A* {cite:p}`Goff2023,Vormsteinschneider2020`), synaptic-adhesion molecules (*CNTNAP2* {cite:p}`Vogt2014`), translational regulators (*FMR1* {cite:p}`Bhandari2024,Rahmatullah2023`), transcription factors (*TCF4* {cite:p}`Chen2023b`; *MECP2* {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Goff2021,Ferguson2023b`), and disease-specific proteostasis lesions (*APP*/*PSEN* in 5xFAD and 3xTg {cite:p}`Michaud2024`; expanded HTT in R6/2 ). *Mid-level VIP-IN phenotypes* recur: reduced intrinsic excitability and firing rate {cite:p}`Goff2023,Mossner2020`, increased input resistance with reduced sEPSC frequency {cite:p}`Chen2023b`, reduced sensory modulation {cite:p}`Rahmatullah2023`, and selective cell-number reductions {cite:p}`Hanno2026,Chen2023b`. *Downstream circuit consequences* converge on E/I imbalance, gain dysfunction, and oscillatory disruption {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Batistabrito2017,Kiss2026,Bogaj2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Lhx6 Directly Regulates Arx and CXCR7 to Determine Cortical Interneuron Fate and Laminar Position
needs_fix_triples.note: Vogt 2014 studied Lhx6/Arx/CXCR7 in MGE interneurons, not CNTNAP2 as a synaptic-adhesion molecule in VIP-INs.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Putting the disease subsections side-by-side reveals a consistent upstream-to-downstream architecture that {numref}`fig-vip-disease-convergence` makes explicit. *Upstream genetic perturbations* span ion channels (*SCN1A* {cite:p}`Goff2023,Vormsteinschneider2020`), synaptic-adhesion molecules (*CNTNAP2* {cite:p}`Vogt2014`), translational regulators (*FMR1* {cite:p}`Bhandari2024,Rahmatullah2023`), transcription factors (*TCF4* {cite:p}`Chen2023b`; *MECP2* {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Goff2021,Ferguson2023b`), and disease-specific proteostasis lesions (*APP*/*PSEN* in 5xFAD and 3xTg {cite:p}`Michaud2024`; expanded HTT in R6/2 ). *Mid-level VIP-IN phenotypes* recur: reduced intrinsic excitability and firing rate {cite:p}`Goff2023,Mossner2020`, increased input resistance with reduced sEPSC frequency {cite:p}`Chen2023b`, reduced sensory modulation {cite:p}`Rahmatullah2023`, and selective cell-number reductions {cite:p}`Hanno2026,Chen2023b`. *Downstream circuit consequences* converge on E/I imbalance, gain dysfunction, and oscillatory disruption {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Bhandari2024,Batistabrito2017,Kiss2026,Bogaj2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of BK channels by GABAb receptors enhances intrinsic excitability of layer 2/3 vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons in mouse neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper found GABAbR activation can increase VIP-IN excitability (high Ca2+) or decrease it (low Ca2+); not simply 'reduced intrinsic excitability.'

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Second, the mouse vs human AD comparison is anchored on the mouse side {cite:p}`Michaud2024` and on the cross-disorder review side {cite:p}`Kiss2026,Gil2024,Bogaj2025b`, with the human-postmortem snRNA-seq pole carried by {cite:p}`Gabitto2024,Yi2025a` (drawn from the master citation map under the same scope precedent applied to other master-only keys used in this section).
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical GABAergic Neuron Dysregulation in Schizophrenia Is Age Dependent
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is not a cross-disorder review; it is an original SCZ-focused study, not suited for 'cross-disorder review side' role.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Second, the mouse vs human AD comparison is anchored on the mouse side {cite:p}`Michaud2024` and on the cross-disorder review side {cite:p}`Kiss2026,Gil2024,Bogaj2025b`, with the human-postmortem snRNA-seq pole carried by {cite:p}`Gabitto2024,Yi2025a` (drawn from the master citation map under the same scope precedent applied to other master-only keys used in this section).
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of BK channels by GABAb receptors enhances intrinsic excitability of layer 2/3 vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons in mouse neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is a single electrophysiology study of GABAbR in VIP-INs, not a cross-disorder review.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Second, the mouse vs human AD comparison is anchored on the mouse side {cite:p}`Michaud2024` and on the cross-disorder review side {cite:p}`Kiss2026,Gil2024,Bogaj2025b`, with the human-postmortem snRNA-seq pole carried by {cite:p}`Gabitto2024,Yi2025a` (drawn from the master citation map under the same scope precedent applied to other master-only keys used in this section).
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: From stress to Alzheimer's: A circuit-based framework for prefrontal cognitive dysfunction
needs_fix_triples.note: Yi2025a is a review, not a human-postmortem snRNA-seq study; mischaracterized as carrying the human-postmortem transcriptomic pole.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: (iv) Whether human rosehip cells or human double-bouquet cells are the more prominent species-specialized type is unsettled; primate double-bouquet work {cite:p}`Lee2023b` foregrounds double-bouquet cells, whereas {cite:p}`Boldog2018` foreground rosehip cells, with no current direct cross-comparison.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Whole-brain mapping of long-range inputs to the VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons in the primary motor cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Cited paper maps inputs to VIP/SST/PV interneurons in mouse motor cortex; not about primate double-bouquet cells or species-specialized types.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: (v) On the developmental side, {cite:p}`Simacek2025a` reported a constant *VIP*-IN contribution to cortical E/I balance across early postnatal stages; the apparent-conflict partner (HosseiniFin2025, listed under unmet citation needs) reports an age-dependent decline in SST-IN density.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Presynaptic maturation of inhibitory connections onto vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing GABAergic interneurons in the mouse barrel field
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper reports significant increases in release probability and vesicle number across ages, indicating developmental maturation, not a constant VIP-IN contribution to E/I balance.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The disinhibition-motif framing reviewed in {ref}`sec-disinhibition-framework` retains its empirical anchor in mouse cortex but applies to human cortex with the qualifications that (i) primate CGE expansion may diversify the VIP→SST→Pyr motif {cite:p}`Pi2013` into multiple parallel motifs {cite:p}`Bakken2021a,Lee2023a,Chartrand2023`; (ii) human-specific VIP subtypes may implement connectivity rules that mouse mechanistic data do not constrain {cite:p}`Boldog2018,Chartrand2023,Somogyi2025`; and (iii) disease phenotypes converge on the *VIP* class but heterogeneously perturb different sub-mechanisms (number, intrinsic excitability, synaptic input, peptide release) {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Chen2023b,Bhandari2024,Goff2023,Hanno2026,Vogt2014`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and morphophysiological evidence for a specialized human cortical GABAergic cell type
needs_fix_triples.note: Boldog2018 rosehip cells are CCK+ interneurons, not VIP subtypes; citing them for human-specific VIP subtypes is inaccurate.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The disinhibition-motif framing reviewed in {ref}`sec-disinhibition-framework` retains its empirical anchor in mouse cortex but applies to human cortex with the qualifications that (i) primate CGE expansion may diversify the VIP→SST→Pyr motif {cite:p}`Pi2013` into multiple parallel motifs {cite:p}`Bakken2021a,Lee2023a,Chartrand2023`; (ii) human-specific VIP subtypes may implement connectivity rules that mouse mechanistic data do not constrain {cite:p}`Boldog2018,Chartrand2023,Somogyi2025`; and (iii) disease phenotypes converge on the *VIP* class but heterogeneously perturb different sub-mechanisms (number, intrinsic excitability, synaptic input, peptide release) {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Chen2023b,Bhandari2024,Goff2023,Hanno2026,Vogt2014`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Synaptic Targets and Cellular Sources of CB1 Cannabinoid Receptor and Vesicular Glutamate Transporter-3 Expressing Nerve Terminals in Relation to GABAergic Neurons in the Human Cerebral Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Somogyi2025 characterizes CB1/VGLUT3+ interneurons (CCK, VIP, calretinin types), not specifically human-specific VIP subtypes constraining connectivity rules beyond mouse data.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The disinhibition-motif framing reviewed in {ref}`sec-disinhibition-framework` retains its empirical anchor in mouse cortex but applies to human cortex with the qualifications that (i) primate CGE expansion may diversify the VIP→SST→Pyr motif {cite:p}`Pi2013` into multiple parallel motifs {cite:p}`Bakken2021a,Lee2023a,Chartrand2023`; (ii) human-specific VIP subtypes may implement connectivity rules that mouse mechanistic data do not constrain {cite:p}`Boldog2018,Chartrand2023,Somogyi2025`; and (iii) disease phenotypes converge on the *VIP* class but heterogeneously perturb different sub-mechanisms (number, intrinsic excitability, synaptic input, peptide release) {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Chen2023b,Bhandari2024,Goff2023,Hanno2026,Vogt2014`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Lhx6 Directly Regulates Arx and CXCR7 to Determine Cortical Interneuron Fate and Laminar Position
needs_fix_triples.note: Vogt 2014 is about Lhx6-mediated MGE interneuron specification, not VIP-IN disease phenotypes.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The molecular and developmental-lineage backbone surveyed in {ref}`sec-molecular-identity` and {ref}`sec-development-lineage` carries forward without revision: VIP cells are CGE-derived members of the 5-HT3AR+ lineage in all sampled species {cite:p}`Fishell2011,Lodato2011,Mayer2018,Tasic2018,Hodge2019,Yao2023,Tremblay2016,Rudy2011,Hu2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Characterization of GABAergic marker expression in prefrontal cortex in dexamethasone induced depression/anxiety model
needs_fix_triples.note: Hu2024 does not address CGE derivation, 5-HT3AR lineage, or developmental origins of VIP cells; it studies GABAergic marker expression in a depression model.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: - **Slabe2023** {cite:p}`Slabe2023` — proposed up-regulation of PACAP-related transcripts in postmortem cingulate cortex of subjects with schizophrenia, the apparent-conflict partner to {cite:t}`Krolewski2025` (which reports a reduced GABAergic / *VIP*-containing transcript signature in BA24c′). - **HosseiniFin2025** (no resolved DOI in master) — developmental trajectory in which SST-IN density declines with age, the apparent-conflict partner to {cite:t}`Simacek2025a` (constant E/I contribution from VIP-INs across early development). - **Tiwari2026** — primary 5xFAD VIP / β-amyloid attenuation source used in the AD and Therapeutics subsections only as a candidate mechanism (not as cited in-section evidence). - **Blumenstock2025** — primary R6/2 Huntington's-disease source for the upstream-VIP disinhibitory cascade reading. - **Liu2025a** — primary sleep-disruption source. - **Oh2022** — primary subgenual ACC CRH+/VIP+ overlap source.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Presynaptic maturation of inhibitory connections onto vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing GABAergic interneurons in the mouse barrel field
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows developmental maturation of inhibitory inputs to VIP-INs (changing release probability/vesicle number), not a constant E/I contribution across stages.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: (1) Cell-density estimates depend sharply on the choice of denominator (GABA+, total neurons, total cells, 5-HT3AR+, *Vip*-Cre-labelled): the Ouellet (8% of GABA+ in P9 rat A1) versus Teymornejad (4.5–6.6% of total neurons in adult human M1) disparity discussed above is a generic feature of the literature and not a species-specific finding {cite:p}`Ouellet2014,Teymornejad2024a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Giant pyramidal neurons of the primary motor cortex express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP), a known marker of cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is on marmoset, not adult human M1. Abstract reports ~5-7%, not 4.5-6.6%. Species and percentages wrong.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: (2) Cross-species transcriptomic *differential* gene expression also depends on the chosen reference genome and the alignment-by-orthology pipeline; {cite:t}`Hong2024` showed that the *GRIA2* stoichiometry signature is conserved across "ferrets, rodents, marmosets and humans" only when the same analysis pipeline is applied to all species, illustrating that cross-species claims are pipeline-dependent.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Calcium-permeable AMPA receptors govern PV neuron feature selectivity
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows conserved GRIA2 stoichiometry across species but does NOT claim this depends on using the same analysis pipeline; the pipeline-dependency claim is fabricated.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The literature now divides cleanly into two- and three-interneuron rate and spiking models that treat VIP as the disinhibitory gain knob {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Bos2025,Veit2023`, four-interneuron extensions that add NDNF/LAMP5 as a parallel input-gating channel {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Anastasiades2021a`, and circuit-level learning models in which VIP-mediated disinhibition implements top-down credit assignment {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models VIP for attentional modulation of oscillations, not top-down credit assignment or learning.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The same disinhibitory motif has been independently recruited as the substrate for predictive-coding error neurons {cite:p}`Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Rossbroich2025,Nemati2025a,Nemati2025b,Shipp2016`, for region-specific reinforcement-learning credit assignment {cite:p}`Chevy2024,Wagatsuma2023`, and for flexible decision-making attractor switching {cite:p}`Shen2023,Yang2016`, indicating that VIP is being asked to support several non-equivalent computational jobs at once.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models attentional modulation via VIP-SST disinhibition, not reinforcement-learning credit assignment.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Canonical three-interneuron (3-IN) circuits — Pyr+PV+SST+VIP — emerged from the connectivity surveys of {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013` and the disinhibitory experiments of {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014`, and have been formalised as rate models {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019`, spiking implementations {cite:p}`Bos2025,Veit2023,Sancheztodo2026`, and biophysically detailed mean-field circuits {cite:p}`Reimann2026,Jadi2012,Isbister2026`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 is a rate/analytical model (E-PV-SOM), not a spiking implementation as claimed in that citation group.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Canonical three-interneuron (3-IN) circuits — Pyr+PV+SST+VIP — emerged from the connectivity surveys of {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013` and the disinhibitory experiments of {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014`, and have been formalised as rate models {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019`, spiking implementations {cite:p}`Bos2025,Veit2023,Sancheztodo2026`, and biophysically detailed mean-field circuits {cite:p}`Reimann2026,Jadi2012,Isbister2026`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Fast Interneuron Dysfunction in Laminar Neural Mass Model Reproduces Alzheimer's Oscillatory Biomarkers
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is a laminar neural mass model, not a spiking implementation. It models PV interneurons but lacks SST/VIP and is not a 3-IN spiking model.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Across this family, VIP→SST disinhibition supplies the dominant route by which top-down or neuromodulatory signals release pyramidal dendrites from inhibition {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Abs2018,Pakan2016,Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 challenges the disinhibition model; does not support VIP→SST disinhibition as dominant route.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Across this family, VIP→SST disinhibition supplies the dominant route by which top-down or neuromodulatory signals release pyramidal dendrites from inhibition {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Abs2018,Pakan2016,Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows MD drives VIP cells and VM drives NDNF cells; does not show VIP→SST as dominant disinhibitory route.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Variants of the 3-IN scheme have been instantiated for primary visual cortex {cite:p}`Hahn2020,Kim2025b,Lee2017a`, primary auditory cortex {cite:p}`Park2020,Natan2017`, somatosensory cortex {cite:p}`Hua2022,Pedrosa2020`, and prefrontal areas {cite:p}`Lee2017a,Shen2023`, and the same motif now appears in cerebellar circuit theory {cite:p}`Park2023` and in subcortical–cortical loop models {cite:p}`Cattani2024`, indicating that the design has effectively become canonical.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: The interplay between somatic and dendritic inhibition promotes the emergence and stabilization of place fields
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models hippocampal CA1, not somatosensory cortex. Cited under somatosensory cortex is a mismatch.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Variants of the 3-IN scheme have been instantiated for primary visual cortex {cite:p}`Hahn2020,Kim2025b,Lee2017a`, primary auditory cortex {cite:p}`Park2020,Natan2017`, somatosensory cortex {cite:p}`Hua2022,Pedrosa2020`, and prefrontal areas {cite:p}`Lee2017a,Shen2023`, and the same motif now appears in cerebellar circuit theory {cite:p}`Park2023` and in subcortical–cortical loop models {cite:p}`Cattani2024`, indicating that the design has effectively become canonical.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Visual processing mode switching regulated by VIP cells
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models V1 (primary visual cortex), not prefrontal areas.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Iqbal2025` show that an L2/3 spiking circuit augmented with LAMP5 implements a soft winner-take-all alongside the classic VIP→SST disinhibition, and that disabling the LAMP5 channel collapses the network's ability to perform context-invariant gain normalisation; {cite:t}`Hartung2024` demonstrate complementarily that NDNF cells deliver volume inhibition with a slow time constant that none of the rate-based 3-IN models capture, providing a low-pass envelope on the otherwise fast VIP→SST disinhibition.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Biologically grounded neocortex computational primitives implemented on neuromorphic hardware improve vision transformer performance
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract says LAMP5 is one of four interneuron classes in sWTA; does not mention disabling LAMP5 collapsing context-invariant gain normalisation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Iqbal2025` show that an L2/3 spiking circuit augmented with LAMP5 implements a soft winner-take-all alongside the classic VIP→SST disinhibition, and that disabling the LAMP5 channel collapses the network's ability to perform context-invariant gain normalisation; {cite:t}`Hartung2024` demonstrate complementarily that NDNF cells deliver volume inhibition with a slow time constant that none of the rate-based 3-IN models capture, providing a low-pass envelope on the otherwise fast VIP→SST disinhibition.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Layer 1 NDNF interneurons are specialized top-down master regulators of cortical circuits
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract describes persistent firing and pervasive inhibition of L2/3 INs, but not 'volume inhibition with a slow time constant' specifically.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Compartmental models of NDNF dendrites further argue that the channel has its own internal computation, with backpropagating signals interacting with synaptic input to produce a second layer of dendritic disinhibition {cite:p}`Griesius2025`, and L1-resolved circuit models suggest the 4-IN architecture is necessary to reproduce layer-specific inhibitory dynamics observed *in vivo* {cite:p}`Vighagen2021,Emmenegger2018`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Morphological and Functional Characterization of Non-fast-Spiking GABAergic Interneurons in Layer 4 Microcircuitry of Rat Barrel Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper characterizes L4 nFS interneurons in barrel cortex; not about L1-resolved circuit models or layer-specific inhibitory dynamics in vivo.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The conflict between 3-IN sufficiency and 4-IN necessity is therefore neither philosophical nor purely numerical: 3-IN proponents {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Bos2025` show that the available behavioural and physiological signatures of state-dependent gain can be reproduced without an explicit NDNF compartment, while 4-IN proponents {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021` argue that doing so requires VIP→SST gain values that are inconsistent with paired-recording data and only the NDNF channel can absorb the missing slow inhibition.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Biologically grounded neocortex computational primitives implemented on neuromorphic hardware improve vision transformer performance
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract does not argue that 3-IN models require inconsistent VIP→SST gain values or that NDNF absorbs missing slow inhibition.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A third class, predictive-coding and dendritic-error models, places VIP in the prediction-error and credit-assignment loop {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Hertag2023,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Rossbroich2025,Nemati2025a,Nemati2025b,Shipp2016`, where disinhibition is no longer a static gain term but a temporally precise teaching signal whose magnitude depends on mismatch between top-down expectation and bottom-up drive {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Pakan2016,Hahn2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models attentional gain via VIP-SST disinhibition, not prediction-error or credit-assignment loops.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A third class, predictive-coding and dendritic-error models, places VIP in the prediction-error and credit-assignment loop {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Hertag2023,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Rossbroich2025,Nemati2025a,Nemati2025b,Shipp2016`, where disinhibition is no longer a static gain term but a temporally precise teaching signal whose magnitude depends on mismatch between top-down expectation and bottom-up drive {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Pakan2016,Hahn2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Paradoxical response reversal of top-down modulation in cortical circuits with three interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models top-down modulation/gain in 3-IN circuit, not predictive-coding or mismatch-dependent teaching signals.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A third class, predictive-coding and dendritic-error models, places VIP in the prediction-error and credit-assignment loop {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Hertag2023,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Rossbroich2025,Nemati2025a,Nemati2025b,Shipp2016`, where disinhibition is no longer a static gain term but a temporally precise teaching signal whose magnitude depends on mismatch between top-down expectation and bottom-up drive {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Pakan2016,Hahn2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Pakan2016 does not address predictive-coding mismatch signals; it studies locomotion modulation of interneurons.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: A third class, predictive-coding and dendritic-error models, places VIP in the prediction-error and credit-assignment loop {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Hertag2023,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Rossbroich2025,Nemati2025a,Nemati2025b,Shipp2016`, where disinhibition is no longer a static gain term but a temporally precise teaching signal whose magnitude depends on mismatch between top-down expectation and bottom-up drive {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Pakan2016,Hahn2022`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Rate and oscillatory switching dynamics of a multilayer visual microcircuit model
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows bistable switching via inhibitory motifs, not predictive-coding mismatch or teaching signals dependent on top-down vs bottom-up mismatch.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Wilmes2019` realised this most explicitly with a top-down inhibitory plasticity circuit in which VIP-mediated disinhibition opens the apical compartment to top-down feedback, allowing prediction errors to be encoded as compartment-specific deviations from a learned baseline; {cite:t}`Hertag2020` and {cite:t}`Hertag2021` extend this to spiking microcircuits in which prediction-error neurons emerge through plasticity, with VIP→SST disinhibition controlling the refinement of error coding and the formation of distinct positive- and negative-error populations.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory microcircuits for top-down plasticity of sensory representations
needs_fix_triples.note: Wilmes2019 uses SST→PV disinhibition gated by reward, not VIP-mediated disinhibition of apical compartments encoding prediction errors.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Wilmes2019` add an inhibitory-plasticity twist, arguing that the feedback projections that target VIP cells implement a supervisor signal that gates the timing of cortical learning, while {cite:p}`Chevy2024` and {cite:p}`Hertag2023` situate the same disinhibitory motif inside reinforcement-learning frameworks as the gate of region-specific credit assignment and feedforward/feedback uncertainty estimation respectively.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Knowing what you don't know: Estimating the uncertainty of feedforward and feedback inputs with prediction-error circuits
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper estimates feedforward/feedback uncertainty, not reinforcement-learning credit assignment. Claiming it is inside an RL framework is inaccurate.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Models in this class do not contradict the 3-IN gain-knob view; rather, they re-interpret the same connectivity as implementing a different objective function. This re-interpretation is consequential because the same VIP perturbation predicts different downstream effects depending on the assumed objective: under a gain-control objective, VIP activation linearly scales pyramidal responses {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023`; under a credit-assignment objective, it transiently opens a learning gate whose effect on output is mostly visible in the *change* of subsequent responses, not in steady-state firing {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Chevy2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Paradoxical response reversal of top-down modulation in cortical circuits with three interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows response reversal/paradoxical effects, not simple linear scaling of pyramidal responses by VIP activation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Models in this class do not contradict the 3-IN gain-knob view; rather, they re-interpret the same connectivity as implementing a different objective function. This re-interpretation is consequential because the same VIP perturbation predicts different downstream effects depending on the assumed objective: under a gain-control objective, VIP activation linearly scales pyramidal responses {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023`; under a credit-assignment objective, it transiently opens a learning gate whose effect on output is mostly visible in the *change* of subsequent responses, not in steady-state firing {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Chevy2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 shows VIP modulates attentional gain on oscillations, not a transient learning gate for credit assignment.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The conflict between "VIP as gain knob" and "VIP as credit/state signal" therefore corresponds to which loss the circuit is hypothesised to minimise — a regression error, in the gain-control framing, versus a temporal-difference or backpropagated dendritic error in the learning framing — and the two predict almost identical instantaneous physiology while diverging sharply in trial-over-trial dynamics {cite:p}`Hertag2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Krabbe2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 addresses attentional modulation of oscillations, not temporal-difference or backpropagated-error learning objectives.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Across the modelling literature, VIP enters in five overlapping capacities: as a gain term {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Veit2023,Bos2025,Richter2022`; as an attention/state controller {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hahn2022,Waitzmann2024,Wagatsuma2022,Poort2022`; as a learning/credit-assignment gate {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Chevy2024,Wilmes2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021`; as an oscillation organiser {cite:p}`Veit2023,Sancheztodo2026,Cattani2024`; and as a context selector that biases winner-take-all attractor dynamics {cite:p}`Shen2023,Yang2016,Furutachi2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 is cited under learning/credit-assignment gate, but the paper models VIP as attentional modulator of oscillations.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Across the modelling literature, VIP enters in five overlapping capacities: as a gain term {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Veit2023,Bos2025,Richter2022`; as an attention/state controller {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hahn2022,Waitzmann2024,Wagatsuma2022,Poort2022`; as a learning/credit-assignment gate {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Chevy2024,Wilmes2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021`; as an oscillation organiser {cite:p}`Veit2023,Sancheztodo2026,Cattani2024`; and as a context selector that biases winner-take-all attractor dynamics {cite:p}`Shen2023,Yang2016,Furutachi2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Fast Interneuron Dysfunction in Laminar Neural Mass Model Reproduces Alzheimer's Oscillatory Biomarkers
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models PV interneuron dysfunction in AD; it does not include VIP interneurons or address VIP as an oscillation organiser.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: At the coarsest end, mean-field rate models {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Kim2025b` collapse each interneuron class to a single firing-rate variable and study fixed-point structure analytically; at the spiking end, sparse and balanced E-I networks {cite:p}`Bos2025,Veit2023,Sancheztodo2026,Pedrosa2020,Rossbroich2025` add membrane dynamics, conductance-based synapses, and short-term plasticity {cite:p}`Seay2020,Cattani2024`; and at the most detailed end, multi-compartment biophysical models {cite:p}`Reimann2026,Jadi2012,Isbister2026,Morabito2024` resolve apical from perisomatic inhibition and reproduce *in vivo* dynamics across cell types.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 analyzes circuit models analytically (rate-level); cited here as spiking/balanced network, likely misclassified.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: At the coarsest end, mean-field rate models {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Kim2025b` collapse each interneuron class to a single firing-rate variable and study fixed-point structure analytically; at the spiking end, sparse and balanced E-I networks {cite:p}`Bos2025,Veit2023,Sancheztodo2026,Pedrosa2020,Rossbroich2025` add membrane dynamics, conductance-based synapses, and short-term plasticity {cite:p}`Seay2020,Cattani2024`; and at the most detailed end, multi-compartment biophysical models {cite:p}`Reimann2026,Jadi2012,Isbister2026,Morabito2024` resolve apical from perisomatic inhibition and reproduce *in vivo* dynamics across cell types.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Fast Interneuron Dysfunction in Laminar Neural Mass Model Reproduces Alzheimer's Oscillatory Biomarkers
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper uses a laminar neural mass model, not a spiking network with membrane dynamics and conductance-based synapses.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Compartmental models {cite:p}`Reimann2026,Jadi2012,Morabito2024,Dorsett2021` can in principle distinguish whether an observed pyramidal effect arises from somatic versus dendritic inhibition — a distinction that mean-field 3-IN circuits collapse — and therefore form the only natural arena in which the 3-IN-versus-4-IN comparison can be resolved without ad hoc assumptions.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A dendritic substrate for temporal diversity of cortical inhibition
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models interneuron dendrites (PV/SST), not pyramidal cell somatic vs dendritic inhibition compartments as claimed.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Hertag2019` produced the most influential 3-IN rate model, in which VIP-driven disinhibition operates **divisively** on pyramidal responses by withdrawing dendritic SST inhibition; this regime is recapitulated by {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Veit2023,Bos2025` and underlies most subsequent learning-rule analyses {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Hertag2020,Hertag2021`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 focuses on SOM-mediated modulation in E-PV-SOM (no VIP); claim attributes VIP-driven divisive disinhibition to it.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Hertag2019` produced the most influential 3-IN rate model, in which VIP-driven disinhibition operates **divisively** on pyramidal responses by withdrawing dendritic SST inhibition; this regime is recapitulated by {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Veit2023,Bos2025` and underlies most subsequent learning-rule analyses {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Hertag2020,Hertag2021`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 is not a learning-rule analysis; it models VIP-SST disinhibition for attentional oscillation modulation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The disagreement is not a modelling artefact; it tracks a genuine ambiguity in the biology, because SST cells target both the apical dendrites and (at lower density) the perisomatic compartment {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Hioki2013,Anastasiades2021a`, and the relative weight of these two synaptic loci is precisely what selects between divisive and subtractive operation in compartmental analyses {cite:p}`Jadi2012,Dorsett2021,Morabito2024,Pedrosa2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Pfeffer2013 characterized inhibitory connectivity between interneuron types, not SST synaptic targeting of apical vs perisomatic compartments.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The disagreement is not a modelling artefact; it tracks a genuine ambiguity in the biology, because SST cells target both the apical dendrites and (at lower density) the perisomatic compartment {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Hioki2013,Anastasiades2021a`, and the relative weight of these two synaptic loci is precisely what selects between divisive and subtractive operation in compartmental analyses {cite:p}`Jadi2012,Dorsett2021,Morabito2024,Pedrosa2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper focuses on NDNF+ and VIP+ L1 interneurons targeting PV/SOM; does not address SST targeting perisomatic compartment.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The disagreement is not a modelling artefact; it tracks a genuine ambiguity in the biology, because SST cells target both the apical dendrites and (at lower density) the perisomatic compartment {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Hioki2013,Anastasiades2021a`, and the relative weight of these two synaptic loci is precisely what selects between divisive and subtractive operation in compartmental analyses {cite:p}`Jadi2012,Dorsett2021,Morabito2024,Pedrosa2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A dendritic substrate for temporal diversity of cortical inhibition
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studies dendritic integration in interneurons (SST/PV), not SST synaptic targeting onto pyramidal compartments or divisive/subtractive operations.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Natan2017` make the same point empirically in auditory cortex by showing that PV and SST contribute divisive and subtractive components respectively to adaptation, and circuit fits using their data place the divisive-versus-subtractive boundary at a specific ratio of dendritic-to-somatic SST conductance — a parameter rarely measured *in vivo* and one that {cite:t}`Tahvili2025a` and {cite:t}`Hertag2019` set to opposite default values.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cortical Interneurons Differentially Shape Frequency Tuning following Adaptation
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows SOM drives gain-like (divisive) changes and PV drives bidirectional changes; claim reverses this, attributing divisive to PV and subtractive to SST.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Empirical reports of net facilitation under VIP optogenetic activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Fu2014,Lee2013,Krabbe2019,Myersjoseph2024` are typically obtained at moderate stimulation strengths and may probe the linear, non-paradoxical regime; reports of suppression or non-monotonic effects {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Veit2023,Bilash2023,Lenkey2025` may correspond to operation closer to the ISN boundary, or to states with elevated SST/PV recurrence.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Adaptive disinhibitory gating by VIP interneurons permits associative learning
needs_fix_triples.note: Krabbe2019 is in basolateral amygdala, not cortex; citing it for 'VIP optogenetic activation' net facilitation in cortical regime discussion is a region mismatch.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Empirical reports of net facilitation under VIP optogenetic activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Fu2014,Lee2013,Krabbe2019,Myersjoseph2024` are typically obtained at moderate stimulation strengths and may probe the linear, non-paradoxical regime; reports of suppression or non-monotonic effects {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Veit2023,Bilash2023,Lenkey2025` may correspond to operation closer to the ISN boundary, or to states with elevated SST/PV recurrence.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Disinhibition by VIP interneurons is orthogonal to cross-modal attentional modulation in primary visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper found VIP manipulation and attention effects were orthogonal, not simply net facilitation of pyramidal cells by VIP activation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Empirical reports of net facilitation under VIP optogenetic activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Fu2014,Lee2013,Krabbe2019,Myersjoseph2024` are typically obtained at moderate stimulation strengths and may probe the linear, non-paradoxical regime; reports of suppression or non-monotonic effects {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Veit2023,Bilash2023,Lenkey2025` may correspond to operation closer to the ISN boundary, or to states with elevated SST/PV recurrence.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Brain region-specific gain modulation of place cells by VIP neurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Lenkey2025 reports VIP activation causes gain modulation/amplification of place cells (facilitation via disinhibition), not suppression or non-monotonic effects.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Plotting these models in a phase diagram of VIP→SST coupling versus dendritic SST→Pyr nonlinearity (Figure 23B; Figure 24A) makes the disagreement structural rather than empirical: {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Bos2025,Veit2023` cluster in a divisive-gain corner, {cite:p}`Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b` occupy the subtractive corner, and {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020` sit on the paradoxical boundary, with {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021` displaced into a fourth quadrant where NDNF input recasts the dendritic operating point.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory stabilization and visual coding in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron subtypes
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studies both subtractive AND divisive modulation; placing it only in divisive-gain corner oversimplifies.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Plotting these models in a phase diagram of VIP→SST coupling versus dendritic SST→Pyr nonlinearity (Figure 23B; Figure 24A) makes the disagreement structural rather than empirical: {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Bos2025,Veit2023` cluster in a divisive-gain corner, {cite:p}`Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b` occupy the subtractive corner, and {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020` sit on the paradoxical boundary, with {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021` displaced into a fourth quadrant where NDNF input recasts the dendritic operating point.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 has no VIP; placing it in a VIP→SST divisive-gain corner is a mismatch.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Spiking and population models built around these regimes {cite:p}`Bos2025,Sancheztodo2026,Tahvili2025a,Pedrosa2020,Rossbroich2025` show that all three primary outcomes — net facilitation, divisive gain, paradoxical suppression — are reachable within a single 3-IN graph by retuning two or three weights, a sensitivity that any inference scheme must confront {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Dellal2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 is an analytical/rate model (E-PV-SOM), not a spiking model; also lacks VIP.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Spiking and population models built around these regimes {cite:p}`Bos2025,Sancheztodo2026,Tahvili2025a,Pedrosa2020,Rossbroich2025` show that all three primary outcomes — net facilitation, divisive gain, paradoxical suppression — are reachable within a single 3-IN graph by retuning two or three weights, a sensitivity that any inference scheme must confront {cite:p}`Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Dellal2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Fast Interneuron Dysfunction in Laminar Neural Mass Model Reproduces Alzheimer's Oscillatory Biomarkers
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is a neural mass model of PV dysfunction in AD; it does not model a 3-IN (PV+SST+VIP) circuit or demonstrate gain/paradoxical suppression regime switching.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The conflicts are therefore not "which experiment is right" but "which regime does the cortex occupy", and existing data are insufficient to localise it: VIP-activation experiments {cite:p}`Pi2013,Fu2014,Lee2013,Krabbe2019,Myersjoseph2024` consistently report pyramidal facilitation in active states, but cell-type optogenetic perturbations during behaviour reveal more complex, often paradoxical effects {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Veit2023,Bilash2023,Lenkey2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Adaptive disinhibitory gating by VIP interneurons permits associative learning
needs_fix_triples.note: Krabbe2019 is in basolateral amygdala, not cortex; claim discusses cortical regime and pyramidal facilitation in active states.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The conflicts are therefore not "which experiment is right" but "which regime does the cortex occupy", and existing data are insufficient to localise it: VIP-activation experiments {cite:p}`Pi2013,Fu2014,Lee2013,Krabbe2019,Myersjoseph2024` consistently report pyramidal facilitation in active states, but cell-type optogenetic perturbations during behaviour reveal more complex, often paradoxical effects {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Veit2023,Bilash2023,Lenkey2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Disinhibition by VIP interneurons is orthogonal to cross-modal attentional modulation in primary visual cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper found attention and VIP effects orthogonal; does not 'consistently report pyramidal facilitation' from VIP activation in active states.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The conflicts are therefore not "which experiment is right" but "which regime does the cortex occupy", and existing data are insufficient to localise it: VIP-activation experiments {cite:p}`Pi2013,Fu2014,Lee2013,Krabbe2019,Myersjoseph2024` consistently report pyramidal facilitation in active states, but cell-type optogenetic perturbations during behaviour reveal more complex, often paradoxical effects {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Veit2023,Bilash2023,Lenkey2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Brain region-specific gain modulation of place cells by VIP neurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Lenkey2025 shows VIP disinhibition amplifies place cell activity (facilitation), not paradoxical/complex suppressive effects as claimed.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Models of state-dependent attention {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Dipoppa2018,Hahn2022,Waitzmann2024,Poort2022,Wagatsuma2022` and of fear-conditioning credit assignment {cite:p}`Krabbe2019,Wagatsuma2023,Dellal2025` further suggest that the operating regime is itself dynamic, switching between subtractive and divisive operation as a function of cholinergic and noradrenergic drive {cite:p}`Fu2014,Pakan2016,Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models visual attentional modulation, not fear-conditioning credit assignment.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Models of state-dependent attention {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Dipoppa2018,Hahn2022,Waitzmann2024,Poort2022,Wagatsuma2022` and of fear-conditioning credit assignment {cite:p}`Krabbe2019,Wagatsuma2023,Dellal2025` further suggest that the operating regime is itself dynamic, switching between subtractive and divisive operation as a function of cholinergic and noradrenergic drive {cite:p}`Fu2014,Pakan2016,Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper studies VIP IN diversity/connectivity, not fear-conditioning credit assignment. Misattributed as supporting fear-conditioning models.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Iqbal2025` demonstrates that adding an NDNF channel to a 3-IN model recovers state-dependent layer-1 inhibition that 3-IN circuits attribute to SST, while {cite:t}`Hartung2024` shows that NDNF-driven volume inhibition imposes a slow envelope on VIP disinhibition that none of the rate models capture.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Biologically grounded neocortex computational primitives implemented on neuromorphic hardware improve vision transformer performance
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models LAMP5 not NDNF; abstract doesn't mention recovering state-dependent L1 inhibition attributed to SST in 3-IN models.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Iqbal2025` demonstrates that adding an NDNF channel to a 3-IN model recovers state-dependent layer-1 inhibition that 3-IN circuits attribute to SST, while {cite:t}`Hartung2024` shows that NDNF-driven volume inhibition imposes a slow envelope on VIP disinhibition that none of the rate models capture.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Layer 1 NDNF interneurons are specialized top-down master regulators of cortical circuits
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract mentions persistent firing but not explicitly 'volume inhibition' or 'slow envelope on VIP disinhibition'.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: LAMP5 and additional layer-1 cell types {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Vanderveer2021,Emmenegger2018` raise the same identifiability question for top-down inputs that NDNF raises for dendrites: a 3-IN circuit with strong VIP→SST gain and a 4-IN circuit with weaker VIP→SST gain plus NDNF inhibition can produce indistinguishable pyramidal observables {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021,Reimann2026`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Morphological and Functional Characterization of Non-fast-Spiking GABAergic Interneurons in Layer 4 Microcircuitry of Rat Barrel Cortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper characterizes L4 nFS interneurons; does not address LAMP5/layer-1 cell types, NDNF, or identifiability of top-down input circuits.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The 3-IN sufficiency proponents {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Bos2025` reply that the additional degrees of freedom are model-internal and not yet tied to compartment-specific data, while the 4-IN necessity proponents {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021,Griesius2025` reply that 3-IN fits to L1-resolved recordings require VIP→SST values inconsistent with paired-recording slopes.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Biologically grounded neocortex computational primitives implemented on neuromorphic hardware improve vision transformer performance
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract doesn't argue that 3-IN fits require VIP→SST values inconsistent with paired-recording slopes.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The 3-IN sufficiency proponents {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Bos2025` reply that the additional degrees of freedom are model-internal and not yet tied to compartment-specific data, while the 4-IN necessity proponents {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021,Griesius2025` reply that 3-IN fits to L1-resolved recordings require VIP→SST values inconsistent with paired-recording slopes.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Interplay of synaptic and backpropagating signals in neurogliaform dendrites
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models neurogliaform dendritic integration/plasticity; does not address 4-IN vs 3-IN circuit architecture debate or VIP→SST connectivity constraints.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Compartmental models that resolve apical from perisomatic inhibition {cite:p}`Jadi2012,Sancheztodo2026,Reimann2026,Morabito2024,Dorsett2021` partly relieve this degeneracy by predicting compartment-specific signatures of each architecture, but the necessary recordings — dual-compartment imaging during VIP, SST, and NDNF perturbation — are rare {cite:p}`Bilash2023,Lenkey2025,Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Fast Interneuron Dysfunction in Laminar Neural Mass Model Reproduces Alzheimer's Oscillatory Biomarkers
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper uses a laminar neural mass model, not a compartmental model resolving apical vs perisomatic inhibition with VIP/SST/NDNF.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Compartmental models that resolve apical from perisomatic inhibition {cite:p}`Jadi2012,Sancheztodo2026,Reimann2026,Morabito2024,Dorsett2021` partly relieve this degeneracy by predicting compartment-specific signatures of each architecture, but the necessary recordings — dual-compartment imaging during VIP, SST, and NDNF perturbation — are rare {cite:p}`Bilash2023,Lenkey2025,Anastasiades2021a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A dendritic substrate for temporal diversity of cortical inhibition
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models interneuron dendrites, not apical vs perisomatic inhibition onto pyramidal cells as the claim implies.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The empirical signature that would distinguish 3-IN sufficiency from 4-IN necessity is therefore concrete: 4-IN architectures predict a slow, layer-1-localised inhibition that survives complete SST silencing and tracks behavioural state on second-to-tens-of-seconds timescales {cite:p}`Hartung2024,Vighagen2021,Iqbal2025`; 3-IN architectures predict that the same slow inhibition collapses with SST silencing and emerges instead from short-term plasticity at SST→Pyr synapses {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Seay2020`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Differential Short-Term Plasticity of PV and SST Neurons Accounts for Adaptation and Facilitation of Cortical Neurons to Auditory Tones
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows STP at PV→Pyr and SST→Pyr synapses drives adaptation/facilitation, but does not argue slow inhibition collapses with SST silencing or substitutes for a 4th IN type.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In the gain-knob framing {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019,Veit2023,Bos2025,Richter2022`, VIP is a static (or slowly varying) parameter that scales pyramidal sensitivity in the service of a regression-style objective — minimising the discrepancy between bottom-up drive and a context-modulated readout.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 studies SOM-mediated modulation without VIP; citing it for VIP as gain knob is misattributed.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In the gain-knob framing {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019,Veit2023,Bos2025,Richter2022`, VIP is a static (or slowly varying) parameter that scales pyramidal sensitivity in the service of a regression-style objective — minimising the discrepancy between bottom-up drive and a context-modulated readout.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A circuit mechanism for independent modulation of excitatory and inhibitory firing rates after sensory deprivation
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper focuses on PV and SST interneurons after sensory deprivation; VIP is not modeled as a gain knob in this paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In the state/credit-signal framing {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Wilmes2019,Chevy2024`, VIP is a dynamic teaching variable that gates plasticity at upstream or apical synapses in service of a temporal-difference, prediction-error, or backpropagated-error objective.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Amplifying the redistribution of somato-dendritic inhibition by the interplay of three interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Hertäg2019 proposes VIP as switching processing modes (gate/gain), not as a dynamic teaching/credit-assignment variable.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: In the state/credit-signal framing {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Wilmes2019,Chevy2024`, VIP is a dynamic teaching variable that gates plasticity at upstream or apical synapses in service of a temporal-difference, prediction-error, or backpropagated-error objective.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models VIP as attentional modulator, not a dynamic teaching variable gating plasticity.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Under the gain-knob objective, the effect of a VIP perturbation is reversible and transient, present during the perturbation and absent immediately after; under the credit-signal objective, the same perturbation should produce a *lasting* change in subsequent responses because the perturbation alters the learning signal that updates synaptic weights {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Chevy2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models attentional modulation of oscillations, not lasting credit-signal changes from learning updates.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Models in which VIP firing is driven by long-range top-down inputs {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Wilmes2019` treat VIP as a target variable whose dynamics are imposed by upstream regions; models in which VIP participates in within-area recurrence {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Bos2025,Pedrosa2020,Rossbroich2025` treat its dynamics as emergent.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not study long-range top-down inputs driving VIP firing; it focuses on local cooperative mechanisms within IN populations.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Models in which VIP firing is driven by long-range top-down inputs {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Wilmes2019` treat VIP as a target variable whose dynamics are imposed by upstream regions; models in which VIP participates in within-area recurrence {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019,Hertag2020,Bos2025,Pedrosa2020,Rossbroich2025` treat its dynamics as emergent.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 is E-PV-SOM with no VIP; cited for models where VIP participates in within-area recurrence.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Recent work attempting to fit either formulation to multi-cell perturbation data {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Moreni2025a,Moreni2025b,Reimann2026` recovers narrow posteriors only when one of the two formulations is enforced *a priori*, suggesting that the field's apparent disagreement about VIP gain may reduce, in part, to a disagreement about which causal arrows in the connectivity graph should be treated as exogenous.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract describes empirical characterization of VIP IN subtypes, not fitting alternative parameterisations to perturbation data or identifiability analysis.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Recent work attempting to fit either formulation to multi-cell perturbation data {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Moreni2025a,Moreni2025b,Reimann2026` recovers narrow posteriors only when one of the two formulations is enforced *a priori*, suggesting that the field's apparent disagreement about VIP gain may reduce, in part, to a disagreement about which causal arrows in the connectivity graph should be treated as exogenous.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Synaptic plasticity facilitates oscillations in a V1 cortical column model with multiple interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is about STDP-driven oscillations in V1, not about fitting formulations to multi-cell perturbation data or VIP gain disagreements.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Dellal2025` make this point explicitly by fitting alternative parameterisations to the same VIP-perturbation dataset and showing that several recover the data with comparable likelihood, while {cite:t}`Reimann2026` argue from a large-scale biophysical model that the addition of dendritic compartments and conductance-based synapses *increases* rather than reduces the parameter manifold compatible with cell-type-resolved electrophysiology.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not describe fitting alternative parameterisations to the same dataset and comparing likelihoods; it characterizes VIP IN functional diversity empirically.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Constraint thus has to come from designs that pry the regimes apart: simultaneous PV, SST, and VIP perturbation with all-optical readout {cite:p}`Veit2023,Lenkey2025,Phillips2016`; compartment-resolved imaging during VIP manipulation {cite:p}`Bilash2023,Pakan2016,Anastasiades2021a`; combined imaging+modeling that exploits learning-induced changes as additional constraints {cite:p}`Poort2022,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025`; and joint fitting against connectivity priors from molecular taxonomies {cite:p}`Yao2023,Rudy2011`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Asymmetric effects of activating and inactivating cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Phillips2016 used single-unit electrophysiology, not all-optical readout; also did not do simultaneous PV/SST/VIP perturbation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Constraint thus has to come from designs that pry the regimes apart: simultaneous PV, SST, and VIP perturbation with all-optical readout {cite:p}`Veit2023,Lenkey2025,Phillips2016`; compartment-resolved imaging during VIP manipulation {cite:p}`Bilash2023,Pakan2016,Anastasiades2021a`; combined imaging+modeling that exploits learning-induced changes as additional constraints {cite:p}`Poort2022,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025`; and joint fitting against connectivity priors from molecular taxonomies {cite:p}`Yao2023,Rudy2011`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 does not combine imaging+modeling exploiting learning-induced changes; it models attentional oscillation modulation.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Recent attempts in this direction {cite:p}`Moreni2025a,Moreni2025b,Jiang2024b,Terwal2021,Huang2025a,Chou2021` couple inference frameworks to multi-cell perturbation data and recover narrower posterior regions, but the conflicts of Figure 24A persist within each posterior, and no single dataset has yet adjudicated between the {cite:p}`Hertag2019` divisive and {cite:p}`Tahvili2025b` subtractive accounts.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Synaptic plasticity facilitates oscillations in a V1 cortical column model with multiple interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models oscillations via STDP, does not couple inference frameworks to perturbation data or address divisive vs subtractive VIP accounts.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Rademacher2025` extend this by arguing that even with multi-perturbation data, the structural identifiability of cell-type-resolved models requires explicit reporting of the full posterior over parameter combinations, not a single best fit; a recommendation that, if adopted, would force the modelling community to publish posteriors over conflicting regimes rather than choose one.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons
