needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models ketamine effects on gamma oscillations; does not argue about structural identifiability or publishing full posteriors.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Hertag2020`, {cite:t}`Hertag2021`, {cite:t}`Wilmes2019`, and {cite:t}`Wagatsuma2023` show that prediction-error and credit-assignment models produce their distinctive signatures in the *temporal* structure of pyramidal responses — phase-locked to mismatch events, ramping across trials with learning, or selectively gated by behavioural context — and these are signatures that scalar readouts cannot resolve.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models attentional gamma modulation, not prediction-error signatures ramping with learning or mismatch events.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Conversely, {cite:t}`Garciadelmolino2017`, {cite:t}`Sanzeni2020`, and {cite:t}`Phillips2016` show that the gain-knob versus paradoxical-suppression distinction is most cleanly tested by perturbation-amplitude curves, in which the sign of the response to small versus large VIP perturbations diverges.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibition stabilization is a widespread property of cortical networks
needs_fix_triples.note: Sanzeni2020 tests paradoxical effects via optogenetic PV stimulation, not VIP perturbation-amplitude curves specifically.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Conversely, {cite:t}`Garciadelmolino2017`, {cite:t}`Sanzeni2020`, and {cite:t}`Phillips2016` show that the gain-knob versus paradoxical-suppression distinction is most cleanly tested by perturbation-amplitude curves, in which the sign of the response to small versus large VIP perturbations diverges.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Asymmetric effects of activating and inactivating cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows activation vs inactivation asymmetry but does not test perturbation-amplitude curves or VIP perturbations specifically.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The identifiability problem is therefore not only a problem of high-dimensional fitting; it is also a problem of designing experiments whose outputs occupy the directions in parameter space along which competing models actually differ {cite:p}`Veit2023,Lenkey2025,Dellal2025,Reimann2026,Rademacher2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not discuss identifiability of cell-type models or experiment design for distinguishing competing models.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The path forward is therefore not "the next better model" but coordinated empirical–computational design: pre-registered architectures whose distinguishing predictions are tested with cell-type-, layer-, and compartment-specific perturbations, and inference pipelines that report the full set of compatible parameterisations rather than a single best fit {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Reimann2026,Sancheztodo2026,Rademacher2025,Moreni2025a,Moreni2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not advocate full posterior reporting or pre-registered architectures; it is a ketamine modeling study.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The path forward is therefore not "the next better model" but coordinated empirical–computational design: pre-registered architectures whose distinguishing predictions are tested with cell-type-, layer-, and compartment-specific perturbations, and inference pipelines that report the full set of compatible parameterisations rather than a single best fit {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Reimann2026,Sancheztodo2026,Rademacher2025,Moreni2025a,Moreni2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Synaptic plasticity facilitates oscillations in a V1 cortical column model with multiple interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper builds a spiking network model of oscillations; does not involve inference pipelines reporting full posterior parameterisations.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The empirical signatures that would adjudicate the regimes are concrete: compartment-specific imaging during VIP perturbation to separate divisive from subtractive operation {cite:p}`Bilash2023,Lenkey2025,Reimann2026`; SST-silenced VIP perturbations to test 4-IN necessity {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021`; perturbation-amplitude curves to localise the ISN versus non-ISN regime {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Phillips2016`; and trial-resolved, learning-coupled readouts to separate gain-knob from credit-signal interpretations {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Chevy2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Asymmetric effects of activating and inactivating cortical interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows activation/inactivation asymmetry but doesn't present perturbation-amplitude curves to localise ISN vs non-ISN regime.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The empirical signatures that would adjudicate the regimes are concrete: compartment-specific imaging during VIP perturbation to separate divisive from subtractive operation {cite:p}`Bilash2023,Lenkey2025,Reimann2026`; SST-silenced VIP perturbations to test 4-IN necessity {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Vighagen2021`; perturbation-amplitude curves to localise the ISN versus non-ISN regime {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Phillips2016`; and trial-resolved, learning-coupled readouts to separate gain-knob from credit-signal interpretations {cite:p}`Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Hertag2020,Hertag2021,Chevy2024`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing
needs_fix_triples.note: Wagatsuma2023 models attentional oscillation modulation, not trial-resolved learning-coupled readouts separating gain from credit.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The next decade's progress depends less on adding cell types than on solving this identifiability problem with the cell types we already have, and on a discipline of model reporting that publishes the full set of parameterisations consistent with the data rather than a single, fragile best fit {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Reimann2026,Rademacher2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not discuss full-posterior reporting or identifiability; it is an empirical study of VIP IN diversity, not a modeling/inference paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The next decade's progress depends less on adding cell types than on solving this identifiability problem with the cell types we already have, and on a discipline of model reporting that publishes the full set of parameterisations consistent with the data rather than a single, fragile best fit {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Reimann2026,Rademacher2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
needs_fix_triples.note: Reimann2026 is an anatomy/connectivity model paper, not about full-posterior reporting or identifiability solutions.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The next decade's progress depends less on adding cell types than on solving this identifiability problem with the cell types we already have, and on a discipline of model reporting that publishes the full set of parameterisations consistent with the data rather than a single, fragile best fit {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Reimann2026,Rademacher2025`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not discuss identifiability problems or publishing full parameterisations; it fits a ketamine model.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Veit2023` and {cite:t}`Bos2025` present spiking-circuit fits that recover canonical VIP-perturbation phenomenology, but the fits use the same VIP-activation dataset that the model was conceptually motivated by, so that the agreement does not constrain the parameterisation against the alternative subtractive {cite:p}`Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b` or paradoxical {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020` regimes.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 is E-PV-SOM without VIP; claim attributes VIP-perturbation spiking fits to it.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Veit2023` and {cite:t}`Bos2025` present spiking-circuit fits that recover canonical VIP-perturbation phenomenology, but the fits use the same VIP-activation dataset that the model was conceptually motivated by, so that the agreement does not constrain the parameterisation against the alternative subtractive {cite:p}`Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b` or paradoxical {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020` regimes.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper models anatomy/connectivity, not paradoxical inhibition regimes; not about VIP-perturbation or ISN dynamics.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Moreni2025a` and {cite:t}`Moreni2025b` demonstrate that simulation-based inference applied to multi-cell perturbation recordings can recover narrower posterior regions in the VIP→SST × SST→Pyr plane than single-perturbation fits, but the recovered posteriors still straddle the divisive/subtractive boundary in several datasets.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Synaptic plasticity facilitates oscillations in a V1 cortical column model with multiple interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not apply simulation-based inference to perturbation recordings or recover posteriors in VIP→SST × SST→Pyr plane.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Jiang2024b`, {cite:t}`Huang2025a`, and {cite:t}`Terwal2021` couple perturbation experiments to spiking models and recover parameter ranges that are consistent across replicates within a behavioural paradigm but inconsistent across paradigms — a pattern that itself argues for state-dependent operating regimes.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Comprehensive characterization of oscillatory signatures in a model circuit with PV- and SOM-expressing interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper is a comprehensive motif analysis, not coupling perturbation experiments to spiking models recovering parameters consistent across replicates within behavioral paradigms.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Reimann2026` and {cite:t}`Isbister2026` integrate these data sources into anatomically constrained large-scale models that exhibit *in vivo*-like dynamics, but precisely because the resulting models inherit hundreds of free parameters, their predictions span the full divisive/subtractive/paradoxical phase space depending on which subset of parameters is fitted to data.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part II, Physiology and experimentation)
needs_fix_triples.note: Abstract describes strong validation and specific predictions, not that predictions span full phase space depending on parameter subset fitting.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Recent work in this direction {cite:p}`Sancheztodo2026,Rademacher2025,Moreni2025a,Moreni2025b` is beginning to publish posterior-resolved fits, suggesting that the discipline is correctable; but the publication norm of reporting a single best-fit parameter set still dominates and continues to obscure the conflicts the field is attempting to resolve.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not publish posterior-resolved fits or discuss publication norms for parameter reporting.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Recent work in this direction {cite:p}`Sancheztodo2026,Rademacher2025,Moreni2025a,Moreni2025b` is beginning to publish posterior-resolved fits, suggesting that the discipline is correctable; but the publication norm of reporting a single best-fit parameter set still dominates and continues to obscure the conflicts the field is attempting to resolve.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Synaptic plasticity facilitates oscillations in a V1 cortical column model with multiple interneuron types
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not publish posterior-resolved fits; it is a mechanistic spiking model study of plasticity-driven oscillations.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Until paired-recording datasets supply tighter priors, or until inference frameworks adopt the full-posterior reporting standard {cite:p}`Rademacher2025,Dellal2025,Reimann2026`, the field's consensus on VIP function will remain a consensus on connectivity topology, not on circuit operation.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not advocate full-posterior reporting standards; unrelated ketamine gamma-oscillation modeling study.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Until paired-recording datasets supply tighter priors, or until inference frameworks adopt the full-posterior reporting standard {cite:p}`Rademacher2025,Dellal2025,Reimann2026`, the field's consensus on VIP function will remain a consensus on connectivity topology, not on circuit operation.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory and disinhibitory VIP IN-mediated circuits in neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not propose or adopt a full-posterior reporting standard; it is an empirical characterization study.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Until paired-recording datasets supply tighter priors, or until inference frameworks adopt the full-posterior reporting standard {cite:p}`Rademacher2025,Dellal2025,Reimann2026`, the field's consensus on VIP function will remain a consensus on connectivity topology, not on circuit operation.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper does not advocate full-posterior reporting standard; it is an anatomy model paper.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: **Empirical conflicts mapped to model parameter regimes.** **(A)** Conflict pairs Hertäg 2019 vs Tahvili 2025b (3-IN divisive vs 2-IN subtractive) and Pi 2013 vs García del Molino 2017 (net disinhibition vs paradoxical inhibition) sit in distinct regions of the same parameter plane; NDNF-channel models {cite:p}`Iqbal2025,Hartung2024` displace the operating point further. **(B)** Architectural-choice tree: which conflicts each architecture (2-IN, 3-IN, 4-IN; rate, spiking, predictive-coding) can in principle address. **(C)** Identifiability problem — three different parameterisations of the same observable: pyramidal firing-rate response to VIP optogenetic activation can be reproduced by strong VIP→SST + weak SST→Pyr, weak VIP→SST + strong SST→Pyr, or a 3-IN+NDNF circuit, illustrating why VIP-perturbation experiments alone cannot constrain architecture {cite:p}`Dellal2025,Reimann2026`. :::
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Biologically grounded neocortex computational primitives implemented on neuromorphic hardware improve vision transformer performance
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper uses LAMP5 interneurons (not NDNF-channel models specifically) and focuses on sWTA dynamics, not subtractive/divisive normalization parameter regimes.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The disinhibitory reading of VIP function — VIP cells, recruited by behavioural state, suppress SST cells and so release pyramidal dendrites from inhibition — is supported as a population-average operating mode in primary visual, auditory, somatosensory, prefrontal, and cingulate cortex {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Pfeffer2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Veit2023`, but it is one operating mode among several, and the literature provides equally well-documented preparations in which VIP recruitment activates SST cells, inverts pyramidal modulation, or fails to modulate pyramidal output at all {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Garrett2020,Yavorska2021,Bigelow2019,Anastasiades2021a,Bastos2023a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
needs_fix_triples.note: Paper shows VIP INs recruit other VIP cells cooperatively and SOM INs co-activate as populations; it does not demonstrate VIP suppressing SST as a disinhibitory motif across cortical areas.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The subtractive-versus-divisive gain ambiguity reduces to operating regime: VIP perturbation is divisive when SST recruitment is concurrent and dendritic inhibition is dominant, subtractive when the disinhibitory release falls on perisomatic compartments, and approximately mixed when both are engaged, with the rate-model literature reproducing each of these outcomes from the same wiring under different parameter draws {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017,Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
needs_fix_triples.note: Bos2025 has no VIP component; claim is about VIP perturbation gain regimes.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Within that triple, primary visual cortex VIP cells implement contrast-dependent, locomotion-gated gain enhancement {cite:p}`Fu2014,Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016,Millman2020`; auditory cortex deploys them for reinforcement-linked disinhibition and critical-period plasticity, with a polarity puzzle around movement-driven SST activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Krabbe2019,Takesian2018,Yavorska2021,Bigelow2019`; somatosensory cortex recruits them via motor copy during active sensing {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019,Kuchibhotla2017`; medial prefrontal and anterior cingulate cortex implement top-down control of attention, working memory, and pain {cite:p}`Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Kamigaki2017,Li2022a`; and hippocampal IS-3 cells assemble a circuit motif that targets other interneurons rather than principal cells, breaking the cortical disinhibitory assumption at the wiring level {cite:p}`Gulyas1996,Tyan2014,Tricoire2011,Kawaguchi1996,Francavilla2018a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Adaptive disinhibitory gating by VIP interneurons permits associative learning
needs_fix_triples.note: Krabbe2019 is in basolateral amygdala, not auditory cortex; citing it under 'auditory cortex' reinforcement-linked disinhibition is a region error.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Within that triple, primary visual cortex VIP cells implement contrast-dependent, locomotion-gated gain enhancement {cite:p}`Fu2014,Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016,Millman2020`; auditory cortex deploys them for reinforcement-linked disinhibition and critical-period plasticity, with a polarity puzzle around movement-driven SST activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Krabbe2019,Takesian2018,Yavorska2021,Bigelow2019`; somatosensory cortex recruits them via motor copy during active sensing {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019,Kuchibhotla2017`; medial prefrontal and anterior cingulate cortex implement top-down control of attention, working memory, and pain {cite:p}`Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Kamigaki2017,Li2022a`; and hippocampal IS-3 cells assemble a circuit motif that targets other interneurons rather than principal cells, breaking the cortical disinhibitory assumption at the wiring level {cite:p}`Gulyas1996,Tyan2014,Tricoire2011,Kawaguchi1996,Francavilla2018a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory circuit gating of auditory critical-period plasticity
needs_fix_triples.note: Takesian2018 studied L1 interneurons (not VIP cells) for critical-period plasticity; silencing VIP cells did NOT abolish plasticity.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: Within that triple, primary visual cortex VIP cells implement contrast-dependent, locomotion-gated gain enhancement {cite:p}`Fu2014,Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016,Millman2020`; auditory cortex deploys them for reinforcement-linked disinhibition and critical-period plasticity, with a polarity puzzle around movement-driven SST activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Krabbe2019,Takesian2018,Yavorska2021,Bigelow2019`; somatosensory cortex recruits them via motor copy during active sensing {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019,Kuchibhotla2017`; medial prefrontal and anterior cingulate cortex implement top-down control of attention, working memory, and pain {cite:p}`Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Kamigaki2017,Li2022a`; and hippocampal IS-3 cells assemble a circuit motif that targets other interneurons rather than principal cells, breaking the cortical disinhibitory assumption at the wiring level {cite:p}`Gulyas1996,Tyan2014,Tricoire2011,Kawaguchi1996,Francavilla2018a`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
needs_fix_triples.note: Kuchibhotla2017 studies auditory cortex context-dependent behavior, not somatosensory cortex active sensing.

needs_fix_triples.claim_text: The same triple maps onto the disease literature reviewed in {numref}`sec-disease-translational`: VIP-IN dysfunction has been documented in autism, Rett, Dravet, schizophrenia, and Alzheimer-related models, with the sign of the effect depending on perturbation polarity, brain area, and disease model rather than on a uniform vulnerability of the subclass {cite:p}`Mossner2020,Goff2023,Hu2024,Mcfarlan2024a,Bhandari2024,Hall2015,Goel2025,Kranz2025,Goff2019`.
needs_fix_triples.paper_title: Characterization of GABAergic marker expression in prefrontal cortex in dexamethasone induced depression/anxiety model
needs_fix_triples.note: Hu2024 is a depression/anxiety DEX model, not autism/Rett/Dravet/schizophrenia/Alzheimer; VIP-IN dysfunction per se is not the focus.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Tremblay2016` flagged this as the most salient feature of cortical *Vip* neurons — higher than most cortical neurons and a substrate for sensitivity to weak excitatory input.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: GABAergic Interneurons in the Neocortex: From Cellular Properties to Circuits
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract discusses interneuron diversity and computational roles broadly but does not specifically mention high input resistance as the most salient VIP feature; this level of detail likely appears in the body text but cannot be confirmed from the abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory stabilization and visual coding in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: The paper studies dynamics of multi-interneuron networks and their roles in visual coding, but the abstract does not clearly address 'gain-regime conflicts' or 'parameter identifiability' as a bottleneck, so its role as contradicting that specific claim cannot be confirmed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The same generalisation, however, has surfaced empirical and theoretical conflicts that the field has not resolved: in several preparations VIP recruitment does not produce the expected SST suppression, and in several models the same connectivity supports either gain enhancement or paradoxical pyramidal suppression depending on weakly constrained parameters {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Yavorska2021,Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Reimann2026`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition stabilization is a widespread property of cortical networks
insufficient_triples.note: The paper demonstrates ISN operation broadly but does not specifically address VIP→SST suppression failures or gain-regime conflicts in the manner claimed; the abstract focuses on paradoxical PV effects, not VIP/SST disinhibition conflicts.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The same generalisation, however, has surfaced empirical and theoretical conflicts that the field has not resolved: in several preparations VIP recruitment does not produce the expected SST suppression, and in several models the same connectivity supports either gain enhancement or paradoxical pyramidal suppression depending on weakly constrained parameters {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Yavorska2021,Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Reimann2026`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
insufficient_triples.note: The paper models neocortical anatomy and connectivity but the abstract does not discuss VIP-SST disinhibition dynamics, gain enhancement, or paradoxical pyramidal suppression conflicts.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
insufficient_triples.note: The paper is an anatomical connectivity model; the abstract does not discuss gain-regime conflicts or parameter identifiability in rate/spiking models.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The third is the **net-disinhibition versus paradoxical-effect** conflict: the same VIP→SST→Pyr connectivity, parameterised inside or outside the inhibition-stabilised regime, supports either pyramidal facilitation or pyramidal suppression on VIP activation, so that "disinhibition" is a property of operating point and not of wiring alone {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Reimann2026,Hertag2019,Tahvili2025a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes anatomical modeling and does not address disinhibition versus paradoxical effects or inhibition-stabilised regime dynamics.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The fourth is the **identifiability of the VIP→SST weight**: in the four-population rate equations, VIP→SST and VIP→PV cross-couplings trade off against SST→Pyr and PV→Pyr terms in a way that connectomic priors alone do not constrain, and several published models with incompatible VIP→SST weights fit the same in-vivo data {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Reimann2026,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not discuss VIP→SST weight identifiability or rate-equation parameter trade-offs; it focuses on anatomical connectivity reconstruction.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Forward and backward cross-references between {numref}`sec-molecular-identity` through {numref}`sec-computational-models` are dense and intentional: a claim about VIP firing in {numref}`sec-in-vivo-behavior` is only as strong as the molecular and morphological identity assigned to the recorded cells in {numref}`sec-molecular-identity` and {numref}`sec-morphology` {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Gouwens2020a,Pronneke2015,Schneidermizell2025`, and a claim about disinhibitory gain in {numref}`sec-disinhibition-framework` is only as strong as the synaptic-weight and sign of the model in {numref}`sec-computational-models` {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Reimann2026`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
insufficient_triples.note: The paper addresses anatomical connectivity and morphological placement, not disinhibitory gain or synaptic-weight/sign constraints in computational models of VIP circuits.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synthesis the review aims to deliver, summarised in {ref}`sec-conclusion`, is therefore not a unified theory of VIP function but a calibrated map of where the disinhibitory reading holds, where it does not, and what experiments and analyses would discriminate the remaining cases {cite:p}`Pi2013,Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Garciadelmolino2017,Reimann2026`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy)
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes an anatomical model of somatosensory cortex and does not discuss where the disinhibitory reading holds or fails.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The Allen Institute Patch-seq atlas {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019,Gliko2024,Gouwens2020b` partitioned the VISp Vip subclass into five morpho-electric-transcriptomic types (Vip-MET-1 through Vip-MET-5) all resembling bipolar or bitufted cells but differing in laminar axon innervation, with Vip-MET-5 (Vip Crispld2) the principal L1-projecting type and Vip-MET-1/-2 occupying L2/3–L4 with distinct axonal biases.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Classification of electrophysiological and morphological neuron types in the mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Gouwens2019 describes 17 e-types, 38 m-types, and 46 me-types but does not describe Vip-MET-1 through Vip-MET-5 partitions; those are from Gouwens2020a. The claim uses 'contradicts' role but Gouwens2019 simply doesn't contain these specific MET-type details.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Gouwens2020a` reported that all five Vip-MET-types resemble bipolar or bitufted cells but differ in laminar axon innervation: Vip-MET-5 (containing Vip Crispld2 t-types) is the principal L1-projecting VIP met-type, innervates all cortical layers including L1, and shows elevated hyperpolarisation-evoked sag relative to the other Vip met-types {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019,Chartrand2023,Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Classification of electrophysiological and morphological neuron types in the mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Gouwens2019 does not describe Vip-MET-types or Vip-MET-5/Crispld2; these details come from Gouwens2020a. The abstract only mentions 17 e-types, 38 m-types, 46 me-types and correspondence to transcriptomic subclasses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Vip-MET-1 occupies L2/3–L4 with bitufted dendrites extending into L1 and a concentrated axon plexus in lower L2/3, while Vip-MET-2 shares the laminar position of Vip-MET-1 but exhibits a deep-biased and broader axon distribution — making the laminar axon profile the defining within-VIP discriminator rather than dendritic geometry alone {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019,Schneidermizell2025,Gamlin2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Classification of electrophysiological and morphological neuron types in the mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Gouwens2019 does not describe Vip-MET-1 or Vip-MET-2 clusters; those specific MET partitions are from Gouwens2020a.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Wu2022` showed that upper- and deeper-layer cortical VIP+ interneurons are transcriptionally distinguishable; {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` independently showed laminar differences in morphology and intrinsic properties; and the Patch-seq atlases of {cite:t}`Gouwens2020a` and {cite:t}`Scala2021` show that the same *Vip* t-types are not equally sampled across cortical areas.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Integrated Morphoelectric and Transcriptomic Classification of Cortical GABAergic Cells
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not discuss whether the same Vip t-types are unequally sampled across cortical areas; the study is restricted to mouse visual cortex.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-synaptic-properties` inventories afferent and efferent connectivity — long-range cortico-cortical and thalamic input, neuromodulatory drive, and the asymmetric VIP→SST > VIP→PV/Pyr output that is the structural basis for the disinhibitory reading — and flags the layer- and area-specific exceptions to that asymmetry {cite:p}`Pi2013,Pfeffer2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Wall2016,Stachniak2021,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Postmitotic Prox1 Expression Controls the Final Specification of Cortical VIP Interneuron Subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: The paper focuses on Prox1's role in VIP interneuron subtype specification and synaptic properties during development, not on inventorying afferent/efferent connectivity patterns or the VIP→SST > VIP→PV/Pyr output asymmetry.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In hippocampus, the analogous role is played by interneuron-selective IS3 cells with somata in stratum oriens that target oriens-lacunosum-moleculare cells {cite:p}`Gulyas1996,Cauli2014,Stachniak2021,Lee2013` — providing a cross-region template for the structural logic of selective interneuron targeting.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Postmitotic Prox1 Expression Controls the Final Specification of Cortical VIP Interneuron Subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: The paper focuses on cortical VIP interneuron subtype specification via Prox1/Elfn1; it does not address hippocampal IS3 cells or their targeting of oriens-lacunosum-moleculare interneurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The same study reported a striking layer-dependent shift: in L6, ~40% of VIP boutons synapsed onto somata, contrasting with 8% in L2/3 and 8% in L5 — indicating that the canonical "perisomatic basket cell" function is not absent from the VIP class but compartmentalised into deep layers, and aligning with the layer-specific axon innervation that defines Patch-seq met-types {cite:p}`Zhou2017,Gouwens2020a,Schneidermizell2025,Gamlin2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes inhibitory specificity and motif groups but does not discuss layer-dependent shifts in VIP bouton somatic targeting (e.g., L6 ~40% somatic boutons); the specific claim about contradicting canonical perisomatic function compartmentalised into deep layers cannot be confirmed or refuted from the abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Vip-MET-1 occupies L2/3–L4 with bitufted dendrites extending into L1 and a concentrated axon plexus in lower L2/3, while Vip-MET-2 shares the laminar position of Vip-MET-1 but exhibits a deep-biased and broader axon distribution — making the laminar axon profile the defining within-VIP discriminator rather than dendritic geometry alone {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019,Schneidermizell2025,Gamlin2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not mention Vip-MET-1/MET-2 subtypes or laminar axon profile differences; this level of morphological detail cannot be confirmed or refuted from the abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-in-vivo-behavior` reviews calcium-imaging and electrophysiological recordings of VIP cells during locomotion, reward, punishment, and stimulus encoding, and assembles the cases in which the disinhibitory mechanism fails or runs in reverse {cite:p}`Fu2014,Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Krabbe2019,Kamigaki2017,Yavorska2021,Bigelow2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Adaptive disinhibitory gating by VIP interneurons permits associative learning
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes VIP interneurons providing a mandatory disinhibitory signal for associative learning in the basolateral amygdala; it does not discuss cases where the disinhibitory mechanism fails or runs in reverse, so it is unclear whether the paper is appropriately cited as a case where disinhibition breaks down.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-cross-areas` lays out the cross-area heterogeneity directly: visual, auditory, somatosensory, frontal, and hippocampal VIP populations differ in afferent profile, output specificity, and behavioural recruitment {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Dipoppa2018,Millman2020,Sermet2019,Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Tyan2014,Tricoire2011,Gulyas1996,Kawaguchi1996`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Interneurons Containing Calretinin Are Specialized to Control Other Interneurons in the Rat Hippocampus
insufficient_triples.note: The paper focuses on calretinin interneurons in rat hippocampus targeting other interneurons; it does not directly address cross-area heterogeneity of VIP populations across visual, auditory, somatosensory, and frontal cortices.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Lee2013` extended this to neocortex with channelrhodopsin-assisted circuit mapping in mouse barrel cortex, showing that VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST-expressing interneurons during whisking {cite:p}`Lee2013,Gulyas1996,Schneidermizell2025,Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Interneurons Containing Calretinin Are Specialized to Control Other Interneurons in the Rat Hippocampus
insufficient_triples.note: This paper is about calretinin interneurons in hippocampus, not about VIP cells inhibiting SST neurons in neocortex during whisking; the claim is attributed to Lee2013, and Gulyas1996 is cited as supporting context but does not directly evidence the neocortical VIP→SST finding.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-cross-areas` lays out the cross-area heterogeneity directly: visual, auditory, somatosensory, frontal, and hippocampal VIP populations differ in afferent profile, output specificity, and behavioural recruitment {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Dipoppa2018,Millman2020,Sermet2019,Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Tyan2014,Tricoire2011,Gulyas1996,Kawaguchi1996`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Physiological and morphological identification of somatostatin- or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-containing cells among GABAergic cell subtypes in rat frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper characterizes VIP cells in rat frontal cortex only; it does not address cross-area heterogeneity across visual, auditory, somatosensory, or hippocampal regions, so the 'contradicts' role cannot be confirmed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015`, using VIP-Cre × tdTomato barrel cortex slices, reported a substantially broader repertoire than the classical bipolar form — including bipolar/bitufted, tufted, and multipolar somatodendritic configurations distributed differently across layers — and noted that the major axon often emerged not from the soma but from a basal dendrite directed toward the white matter, a feature also flagged by {cite:t}`Apicella2022` {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Apicella2022,Kawaguchi1996,Cauli2014`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Physiological and morphological identification of somatostatin- or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-containing cells among GABAergic cell subtypes in rat frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Kawaguchi1996 described VIP morphologies but did not specifically discuss axon emergence from basal dendrites; the 'contradicts' role regarding the broader Pronneke finding cannot be confirmed or denied from this abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` reported that the majority of VIP barrel-cortex cells emit their main axon not from the soma but from a basal dendrite directed toward the white matter — a feature shared across the dataset and corroborated by {cite:t}`Apicella2022` and consistent with reconstructions reviewed by {cite:t}`Cauli2014` {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Apicella2022,Cauli2014,Kawaguchi1996`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Physiological and morphological identification of somatostatin- or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-containing cells among GABAergic cell subtypes in rat frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Kawaguchi1996 described VIP cell morphologies but the abstract does not specifically address axon emergence from basal dendrites; a 'contradicts' role regarding this feature cannot be confirmed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Modern scRNA-seq taxonomies recapitulate this division at the transcriptomic level: in mouse cortex, CGE origin maps onto the *Vip*, *Lamp5*, *Sncg* and *Serpinf1* subclasses {cite:p}`Tasic2018`, while in human middle temporal gyrus the CGE branch is identified by *ADARB2* expression and accounts for ~50% of cortical interneurons against ~44% for the *LHX6*+ MGE branch {cite:p}`Hodge2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Conserved cell types with divergent features in human versus mouse cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not report specific CGE/MGE proportions (50% ADARB2+ vs 44% LHX6+); these details are likely in the paper body but cannot be confirmed from the abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Postmitotic *Prox1* ablation in early postnatal VIP cells then reveals an additional, subtype-selective role: it reduces *Elfn1*-dependent short-term plasticity of excitatory inputs onto multipolar but not bipolar VIP cells {cite:p}`Stachniak2021`, in line with adult VIP-subclass transcriptomic preservation of *Prox1* expression {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Hodge2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Conserved cell types with divergent features in human versus mouse cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not specifically address Prox1 expression preservation in the VIP subclass; this is a detailed gene-level claim likely in the body but not verifiable from the abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Boldog2018` described a novel rosehip interneuron in human L1 — with compact bushy axonal arborisations and unusually large boutons confined to L1 — at ~13% (10/76) of L1 interneurons sampled, providing a human-specific morphology with no clear mouse counterpart and adjacent to the VIP/Lamp5 family {cite:p}`Boldog2018,Hodge2019,Chartrand2023,Lee2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Conserved cell types with divergent features in human versus mouse cortex
insufficient_triples.note: LLM did not return verdict for this triple

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Hodge2019` reported that in adult human middle temporal gyrus the VIP subclass is the most diverse among inhibitory subclasses, partitioning into 21 transcriptomic types most of which are enriched in upper cortical layers {cite:p}`Hodge2019,Boldog2018,Lee2023a,Chartrand2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and morphophysiological evidence for a specialized human cortical GABAergic cell type
insufficient_triples.note: Boldog2018 describes rosehip cells in L1 with a GAD1+CCK+ profile; the abstract does not discuss VIP subclass diversity or its partitioning into 21 types, so it cannot support the specific claim about VIP being the most diverse inhibitory subclass.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Gouwens2020a` reported that all five Vip-MET-types resemble bipolar or bitufted cells but differ in laminar axon innervation: Vip-MET-5 (containing Vip Crispld2 t-types) is the principal L1-projecting VIP met-type, innervates all cortical layers including L1, and shows elevated hyperpolarisation-evoked sag relative to the other Vip met-types {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019,Chartrand2023,Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphoelectric and transcriptomic divergence of the layer 1 interneuron repertoire in human versus mouse neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract discusses L1 interneuron subclasses and morphoelectric variability in human vs mouse but does not specifically mention Vip-MET-5, Vip Crispld2, or sag differences among Vip met-types; these details likely reside in the paper body but cannot be confirmed from the abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-disease-translational` works out cross-species conservation and divergence, primate expansion of CGE-derived diversity, and VIP-IN involvement in autism, Rett, Dravet, schizophrenia, and Alzheimer-related pathology {cite:p}`Hodge2019,Boldog2018,Chartrand2023,Bakken2021a,Bakken2021b,Krienen2020,Mossner2020,Goff2023,Hu2024,Mcfarlan2024a,Bhandari2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: The spike-timing-dependent plasticity of VIP interneurons in motor cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper discusses VIP IN plasticity and mentions potential relevance to epilepsy, but does not address autism, Rett, Dravet, schizophrenia, or Alzheimer-related pathology, nor cross-species conservation/divergence or CGE-derived diversity expansion.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Untangling stability and gain modulation in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron classes
insufficient_triples.note: The paper analyzes E-PV-SOM circuit models focusing on gain modulation and stability, but the abstract does not address parameter identifiability as a bottleneck, making it unclear whether the paper contradicts this specific claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.

insufficient_triples.note: The claim references a broad set of papers to support a nuanced argument about gain-regime conflicts and parameter identifiability as the bottleneck; the paper's own abstract does not directly address or contradict this specific framing, and many of the cited works cannot be individually verified from the abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Layer 1 NDNF interneurons are specialized top-down master regulators of cortical circuits
insufficient_triples.note: This paper focuses on NDNF L1 interneuron connectivity and physiology; its abstract does not address computational models, gain regimes, or parameter identifiability, making it impossible to confirm or refute the claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: PV and SOM cells play distinct causal roles in controlling network oscillations and stability
insufficient_triples.note: The paper focuses on PV and SOM roles in oscillations and stability, not on VIP-related gain-regime conflicts or parameter identifiability debates in rate/spiking/learning models.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The second is the **gain-regime ambiguity**: optogenetic VIP activation has been read as subtractive, divisive, or mixed gain depending on area, layer, and analysis convention, and the rate models that fit the data span the same range {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017,Hertag2019,Garciadelmolino2017,Litwinkumar2016,Veit2023,Bos2025,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: PV and SOM cells play distinct causal roles in controlling network oscillations and stability
insufficient_triples.note: The paper does not address VIP activation, gain modulation (subtractive/divisive/mixed), or the gain-regime ambiguity described in the claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The third is the **net-disinhibition versus paradoxical-effect** conflict: the same VIP→SST→Pyr connectivity, parameterised inside or outside the inhibition-stabilised regime, supports either pyramidal facilitation or pyramidal suppression on VIP activation, so that "disinhibition" is a property of operating point and not of wiring alone {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Sanzeni2020,Reimann2026,Hertag2019,Tahvili2025a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: PV and SOM cells play distinct causal roles in controlling network oscillations and stability
insufficient_triples.note: The paper does not discuss VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition, paradoxical effects, or inhibition-stabilised regime operating points relevant to this claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The second is the **gain-regime ambiguity**: optogenetic VIP activation has been read as subtractive, divisive, or mixed gain depending on area, layer, and analysis convention, and the rate models that fit the data span the same range {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017,Hertag2019,Garciadelmolino2017,Litwinkumar2016,Veit2023,Bos2025,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b`.

insufficient_triples.note: The paper's abstract focuses on oscillatory dynamics in E-PV-SOM circuits and does not discuss VIP activation, gain regimes (subtractive/divisive/mixed), or the ambiguity surrounding optogenetic VIP perturbations.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.

insufficient_triples.note: The claim references a large set of papers in a broad architectural review context; without access to each cited paper's abstract individually, and given the manuscript's own abstract does not discuss 'gain-regime conflicts' or 'parameter identifiability' as a bottleneck, there is insufficient evidence to verify or refute this composite claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {numref}`sec-computational-models` audits the rate-, spiking-, and learning-model literature, exposes the gain-regime conflicts the architectures generate, and argues that the present bottleneck is parameter identifiability rather than missing biology {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Bos2025,Iqbal2025,Hartung2024,Tahvili2025a,Tahvili2025b,Reimann2026,Sanzeni2020,Wagatsuma2023,Lee2025,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity
insufficient_triples.note: This paper is an experimental study on VIP interneuron modulation of cortical activity by behavioral state; it does not present computational/rate/spiking/learning models or discuss parameter identifiability or gain-regime conflicts.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The fourth is the **identifiability of the VIP→SST weight**: in the four-population rate equations, VIP→SST and VIP→PV cross-couplings trade off against SST→Pyr and PV→Pyr terms in a way that connectomic priors alone do not constrain, and several published models with incompatible VIP→SST weights fit the same in-vivo data {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Litwinkumar2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Veit2023,Reimann2026,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity
insufficient_triples.note: This paper is an experimental study on VIP interneurons and behavioral state modulation; it does not present rate-equation models or discuss VIP→SST weight identifiability or cross-coupling trade-offs.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The most widely used genetic-access tool, the VIP-Cre or VIP-IRES-Cre line {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Paul2017`, captures most but not all *Vip* subclass cells, and may also label transiently *Vip*-expressing cells from neighbouring CGE subclasses; the off-target risk is most acute at the boundary with *Sncg* and *Lamp5* {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Yao2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Shared and distinct transcriptomic cell types across neocortical areas
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not discuss VIP-Cre driver lines, off-target labeling, or Sncg/Lamp5 boundary issues; the paper focuses on transcriptomic cell types, not Cre-line specificity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The most widely used genetic-access tool, the VIP-Cre or VIP-IRES-Cre line {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Paul2017`, captures most but not all *Vip* subclass cells, and may also label transiently *Vip*-expressing cells from neighbouring CGE subclasses; the off-target risk is most acute at the boundary with *Sncg* and *Lamp5* {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Yao2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Shared and distinct transcriptomic cell types across neocortical areas
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not address VIP-Cre driver specificity or off-target labeling at CGE subclass boundaries.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The marker-defined subdivisions VIP/ChAT, VIP/CR, VIP/CCK, and VIP/CALB2 partition the *Vip* subclass with overlapping rather than disjoint boundaries {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Paul2017,Granger2020`. **(B)** Schematic Venn comparison of populations captured by VIP-Cre, VIP-IRES-Cre, and anti-VIP immunolabel: Cre-driver populations include most but not all transcriptomically defined *Vip* cells and may capture transiently *Vip*-expressing neighbours from *Sncg* and *Lamp5* subclasses; immunolabel captures cells with detectable VIP peptide, biasing toward higher-expressing subsets {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Yao2021a,Pronneke2015`. **(C)** Schematic of the immunolabel-to-t-type mapping: VIP/CR maps largely one-to-many onto multiple bipolar *Vip* t-types and onto human In1d/VIP-CALB2-TAC3 nuclei {cite:p}`Lake2018,Tremblay2016`; VIP/CCK maps many-to-many onto *Vip* t-types overlapping with *Sncg* CCK-basket-related types {cite:p}`Miczan2021,Paul2017`; VIP/ChAT maps approximately one-to-one onto a small bipolar *Vip-Chat* type that is identified at the t-type level by {cite:p}`Tasic2016,Granger2020` but is not formally labelled cholinergic at the cluster level in larger atlases {cite:p}`Yao2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Shared and distinct transcriptomic cell types across neocortical areas
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not discuss Cre-driver specificity or immunolabel-to-t-type mapping for VIP subtypes; the claim's 'contradicts' role regarding Cre off-target capture cannot be confirmed from the abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Postmitotic *Prox1* ablation in early postnatal VIP cells then reveals an additional, subtype-selective role: it reduces *Elfn1*-dependent short-term plasticity of excitatory inputs onto multipolar but not bipolar VIP cells {cite:p}`Stachniak2021`, in line with adult VIP-subclass transcriptomic preservation of *Prox1* expression {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Hodge2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Shared and distinct transcriptomic cell types across neocortical areas
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not discuss Prox1 expression preservation in adult VIP subclass cells; the paper focuses on defining transcriptomic types, not specific gene-level expression details for Prox1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Chartrand2023` reported that L1 VIP interneurons in both human and mouse exhibit characteristic descending axon collaterals, but that mouse L1-VIP cells show greater morphological variability than the more uniform human L1-VIP TSPAN12 cells; the human L1 VIP TSPAN12 cells, in particular, display stellate-like dendrites combined with descending axon collaterals and high voltage sag — a morphoelectric profile distinct from the mouse counterpart {cite:p}`Chartrand2023,Boldog2018,Hodge2019,Lee2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Signature morphoelectric properties of diverse GABAergic interneurons in the human neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract mentions cellular differences between mouse and human GABAergic types and morphological heterogeneity, including primate-specialized double bouquet cells, but does not specifically address L1 VIP TSPAN12 stellate dendrites, voltage sag, or comparative mouse L1-VIP morphological variability; Chartrand2023 is a different paper from Lee2023a.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Boldog2018` described, by Patch-seq and snRNA-seq, a novel rosehip interneuron in human L1 with compact bushy axonal arborisations, unusually large axonal boutons, and L1-confined arborisation, accounting for ~13% (10/76) of L1 interneurons sampled and lacking a clear mouse counterpart {cite:p}`Boldog2018,Hodge2019,Chartrand2023,Lee2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Signature morphoelectric properties of diverse GABAergic interneurons in the human neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2023a's abstract discusses cross-species GABAergic differences and primate-specialized morphologies (double bouquet cells) but does not specifically mention rosehip interneurons or their properties.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The L1-VIP morpho-electric profile reported in human by {cite:t}`Chartrand2023` — stellate-like dendrites combined with descending axon collaterals and high voltage sag in TSPAN12 cells — should therefore be read against the rosehip and LAMP5/PAX6 backdrop rather than as a pure VIP-class statement {cite:p}`Chartrand2023,Boldog2018,Hodge2019,Lee2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Signature morphoelectric properties of diverse GABAergic interneurons in the human neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2023a's abstract does not mention rosehip cells, LAMP5/PAX6 backdrop, or L1-VIP TSPAN12 morpho-electric profiles specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The contemporary picture, drawn from Patch-seq atlases, large-volume connectomics, and decades of biocytin-based reconstructions, is consistent on the gross descriptors but contested on the boundaries: cortical VIP cells span a recognisable but graded morphological space dominated by bipolar/bitufted somatodendritic profiles with descending axonal arbors {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1996,Cauli2014,Pronneke2015,Apicella2022`, with smaller proportions of small-basket and arcade-like variants {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1996,Rudy2011,Stachniak2021,Yayon2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Three groups of interneurons account for nearly 100 of neocortical GABAergic neurons
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract mentions the 5HT3aR group includes VIP-expressing neurons but does not specifically discuss small-basket or arcade-like VIP variants.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: By the end of the first postnatal week, CGE-derived cells have completed their characteristic preferential occupancy of layers II/III (~75%) {cite:p}`Miyoshi2010`, although in mouse this enrichment is graded rather than absolute and a substantial CGE-derived contingent settles in deeper layers and in layer 1 {cite:p}`Lee2010`. *Larimer2016* used heterochronic CGE transplants into neonatal mouse V1 to show that CGE-derived precursors disperse, acquire host-like laminar position, marker expression, intrinsic properties and visual response — but, unlike MGE transplants, they do not reactivate ocular dominance plasticity, with the residual ODP induction observed in CGE transplants attributable to a small contaminating fraction of MGE-lineage cells {cite:p}`Larimer2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: The Largest Group of Superficial Neocortical GABAergic Interneurons Expresses Ionotropic Serotonin Receptors
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract states 5-HT3AR+ neurons are the largest group in superficial layers but does not specifically discuss CGE-derived contingents settling in deeper layers or layer 1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The marker-defined subdivisions VIP/ChAT, VIP/CR, VIP/CCK, and VIP/CALB2 partition the *Vip* subclass with overlapping rather than disjoint boundaries {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Paul2017,Granger2020`. **(B)** Schematic Venn comparison of populations captured by VIP-Cre, VIP-IRES-Cre, and anti-VIP immunolabel: Cre-driver populations include most but not all transcriptomically defined *Vip* cells and may capture transiently *Vip*-expressing neighbours from *Sncg* and *Lamp5* subclasses; immunolabel captures cells with detectable VIP peptide, biasing toward higher-expressing subsets {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Yao2021a,Pronneke2015`. **(C)** Schematic of the immunolabel-to-t-type mapping: VIP/CR maps largely one-to-many onto multiple bipolar *Vip* t-types and onto human In1d/VIP-CALB2-TAC3 nuclei {cite:p}`Lake2018,Tremblay2016`; VIP/CCK maps many-to-many onto *Vip* t-types overlapping with *Sncg* CCK-basket-related types {cite:p}`Miczan2021,Paul2017`; VIP/ChAT maps approximately one-to-one onto a small bipolar *Vip-Chat* type that is identified at the t-type level by {cite:p}`Tasic2016,Granger2020` but is not formally labelled cholinergic at the cluster level in larger atlases {cite:p}`Yao2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A high-resolution transcriptomic and spatial atlas of cell types in the whole mouse brain
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes neurotransmitter/neuropeptide diversity and co-expression patterns but does not specifically address whether Vip-Chat clusters were or were not formally labelled cholinergic; the specific claim about sub-threshold Slc18a3/Chat expression in Vip clusters cannot be confirmed or refuted from the abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Yao2023` reported that *Slc18a3* (the vesicular acetylcholine transporter) and *Chat* mRNAs are detected in several clusters within the *Vip* GABAergic subclass of isocortex, but expression at the cluster level did not cross thresholds for formal labelling as cholinergic, and the whole-brain atlas does not therefore name a "VIP/ChAT" cluster.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A high-resolution transcriptomic and spatial atlas of cell types in the whole mouse brain
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract mentions extraordinary diversity in neurotransmitter and neuropeptide expression but does not detail whether Slc18a3/Chat in Vip clusters crossed formal labelling thresholds; the specific claim about absence of a 'VIP/ChAT' cluster label cannot be verified from the abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Yao2023` reported that *Slc18a3*/*Chat* are detected in several clusters within the *Vip* subclass of mouse isocortex but at sub-threshold cluster-level expression, so the whole-brain atlas does not formally label any *Vip* cluster cholinergic.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A high-resolution transcriptomic and spatial atlas of cell types in the whole mouse brain
insufficient_triples.note: Same issue as above: the abstract discusses neurotransmitter co-expression patterns broadly but does not specify whether any Vip cluster was or was not formally labelled cholinergic.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The most widely used genetic-access tool, the VIP-Cre or VIP-IRES-Cre line {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Paul2017`, captures most but not all *Vip* subclass cells, and may also label transiently *Vip*-expressing cells from neighbouring CGE subclasses; the off-target risk is most acute at the boundary with *Sncg* and *Lamp5* {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Yao2021a`.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The marker-defined subdivisions VIP/ChAT, VIP/CR, VIP/CCK, and VIP/CALB2 partition the *Vip* subclass with overlapping rather than disjoint boundaries {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Paul2017,Granger2020`. **(B)** Schematic Venn comparison of populations captured by VIP-Cre, VIP-IRES-Cre, and anti-VIP immunolabel: Cre-driver populations include most but not all transcriptomically defined *Vip* cells and may capture transiently *Vip*-expressing neighbours from *Sncg* and *Lamp5* subclasses; immunolabel captures cells with detectable VIP peptide, biasing toward higher-expressing subsets {cite:p}`Tasic2018,Yao2021a,Pronneke2015`. **(C)** Schematic of the immunolabel-to-t-type mapping: VIP/CR maps largely one-to-many onto multiple bipolar *Vip* t-types and onto human In1d/VIP-CALB2-TAC3 nuclei {cite:p}`Lake2018,Tremblay2016`; VIP/CCK maps many-to-many onto *Vip* t-types overlapping with *Sncg* CCK-basket-related types {cite:p}`Miczan2021,Paul2017`; VIP/ChAT maps approximately one-to-one onto a small bipolar *Vip-Chat* type that is identified at the t-type level by {cite:p}`Tasic2016,Granger2020` but is not formally labelled cholinergic at the cluster level in larger atlases {cite:p}`Yao2023`.
