insufficient_triples.note: The claim cites Paul2017 for VIP/CCK mapping many-to-many onto Vip t-types overlapping with Sncg CCK-basket types; the abstract discusses combinatorial molecular profiles of GABAergic neurons but does not specifically address VIP/CCK-to-t-type mapping or Sncg overlap.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Tremblay2016` and {cite:t}`Paul2017` accordingly treated VIP/ChAT cells as a distinct subset of the VIP class, reporting them as a small fraction of cortical VIP cells.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Transcriptional Architecture of Synaptic Communication Delineates GABAergic Neuron Identity
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract discusses transcriptional profiling of GABAergic neurons and combinatorial gene expression but does not specifically mention VIP/ChAT cells as a distinct subset or their fraction among VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The classical estimate that approximately 40% of cortical VIP interneurons co-express calretinin {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Cauli2014,Cauli1997,Demarcogarcia2011` was challenged by region-specific counts in mouse V1 layers 4 and 5 reporting ~52% VIP/CR overlap {cite:p}`Xu2010a,Gonchar2008,Rudy2011,Cauli2014`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Neuronal activity is required for the development of specific cortical interneuron subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract focuses on activity-dependent migration and morphological development of CGE interneuron subtypes, not on quantitative VIP/calretinin co-expression percentages.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Rudy2011` summarised the rodent literature as reporting approximately 40% of cortical VIP-positive interneurons co-expressing calretinin {cite:p}`Rudy2011`, while {cite:t}`Xu2010a` reported a significantly higher VIP/CR overlap (~52%) restricted to layers 4 and 5 of mouse V1 {cite:p}`Xu2010a`. **Resolution:** The two estimates are likely not contradictory: co-expression depends on cortical region, laminar position, and developmental stage, and both reports remain valid for their respective contexts {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Xu2010a,Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021`. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Neuronal activity is required for the development of specific cortical interneuron subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not address VIP/CR co-expression percentages; it focuses on activity-dependent development of CGE interneuron subtypes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: PROX1 is expressed in 84% of fate-mapped CGE/LGE/POA-derived interneurons and in 100% of VIP+ cortical interneurons in P30 mouse cortex {cite:p}`Rubin2013`, and is selectively maintained in postmitotic CGE-derived interneuron precursors as the first identified CGE-specific postmitotic TF {cite:p}`Miyoshi2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: PROX1: A Lineage Tracer for Cortical Interneurons Originating in the Lateral/Caudal Ganglionic Eminence and Preoptic Area
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract confirms PROX1 marks nearly all LGE/CGE- and subsets of POA-derived cortical interneurons and lists VIP as an expressed marker, but does not provide the specific quantitative figures (84%, 100%) cited in the claim; these details may be in the paper body but cannot be confirmed from the abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Karayannis2012` extended these findings using the same Kir2.1 paradigm, reporting that cell-autonomous activity reduction lowered maximum AP discharge from 93±10 Hz to 44±8 Hz and broadened AP half-width from 1.3±0.15 ms to 3.0±0.32 ms in CGE-derived cells (P12–P15 cortex), and produced subtype-specific laminar mispositioning in Re+ and Cr+ but not VIP+ cells {cite:p}`Karayannis2012`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Functional adaptation of cortical interneurons to attenuated activity is subtype-specific
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract confirms Kir2.1 was used to reduce activity in CGE-derived interneurons and that developmental changes in intrinsic electrophysiological properties occurred, but the specific quantitative values (93±10 Hz to 44±8 Hz, 1.3±0.15 ms to 3.0±0.32 ms) and the subtype-specific laminar mispositioning details (Re+ and Cr+ but not VIP+) are not mentioned in the abstract and cannot be confirmed or refuted from it alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Karayannis2012` extended these findings using the same Kir2.1 paradigm, reporting that cell-autonomous activity reduction lowered maximum AP discharge from 93±10 Hz to 44±8 Hz and broadened AP half-width from 1.3±0.15 ms to 3.0±0.32 ms in CGE-derived cells (P12–P15 cortex), and produced subtype-specific laminar mispositioning in Re+ and Cr+ but not VIP+ cells {cite:p}`Karayannis2012`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Functional adaptation of cortical interneurons to attenuated activity is subtype-specific
insufficient_triples.note: Same claim as T_604c0e03bea2; the abstract confirms the general paradigm (Kir2.1, CGE interneurons, intrinsic property changes) but does not provide the specific quantitative values or laminar mispositioning details cited.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Bandler2017` had previously argued that cortical interneuron specification is governed by the joint contributions of spatial origin, birthdate, lineage relationships and mode of cell division — a framework that the more recent CGE-resolved data {cite:p}`Garg2025,Bright2025` now operationalise.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mature interneuron subtypes arise from distinct spatial and temporal subdomains within the caudal ganglionic eminence
insufficient_triples.note: Bandler et al. 2017 is not the paper under review; its abstract is not available to verify the specific claim about spatial origin, birthdate, lineage, and mode of cell division.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Wong2022` reported, using immunohistochemistry of CGE-derived Htr3a-positive cells, that VIP is expressed in two morphologically distinct subtypes — most CR-positive bipolar cells and approximately half of CR-/Reln-negative basket cells — providing a molecular link between the bipolar majority and the smaller VIP-basket fraction {cite:p}`Wong2022,Stachniak2021,Rudy2011,Yayon2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: High-throughput morphometric and transcriptomic profiling uncovers composition of naive and sensory-deprived cortical cholinergic VIP/CHAT neurons
insufficient_triples.note: Wong2022 is not the paper under review and no abstract is provided for it; cannot confirm the specific claim about CR-positive bipolar cells and CR-/Reln-negative basket cells from the Yayon2023 abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Gouwens2019` had earlier shown across the whole inhibitory inventory that the axon, not the dendrite, is the dominant feature distinguishing inhibitory m-types in mouse visual cortex; the same paper noted that L2/3 Chat-Cre-labelled neurons (a known subset of Vip cells) map predominantly to a small number of related me-types associated with the Vip-Ptprt-Pkp2 transcriptomic type {cite:p}`Gouwens2019,Gouwens2020a,Yayon2023,Wong2022`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: High-throughput morphometric and transcriptomic profiling uncovers composition of naive and sensory-deprived cortical cholinergic VIP/CHAT neurons
insufficient_triples.note: The Yayon2023 abstract does not discuss axon vs dendrite as discriminating features or mention Vip-Ptprt-Pkp2 transcriptomic types; this claim primarily concerns Gouwens2019 findings.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Stachniak2021` further showed in mouse cortex that VIP cells split during development into a CR-positive bipolar subtype and a CCK-positive multipolar subtype with distinct dendritic geometries, and that genetic perturbations differentially affect the two arms {cite:p}`Stachniak2021,Demarcogarcia2011,Wong2022,Yayon2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: High-throughput morphometric and transcriptomic profiling uncovers composition of naive and sensory-deprived cortical cholinergic VIP/CHAT neurons
insufficient_triples.note: The claim attributes CR-positive bipolar vs CCK-positive multipolar splitting and genetic perturbation effects to Stachniak2021; the Yayon2023 abstract alone cannot confirm or contradict those specific findings.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The Allen Institute Patch-seq atlas {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019,Gliko2024,Gouwens2020b` partitioned the VISp Vip subclass into five morpho-electric-transcriptomic types (Vip-MET-1 through Vip-MET-5) all resembling bipolar or bitufted cells but differing in laminar axon innervation, with Vip-MET-5 (Vip Crispld2) the principal L1-projecting type and Vip-MET-1/-2 occupying L2/3–L4 with distinct axonal biases.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: High-throughput analysis of dendrite and axonal arbors reveals transcriptomic correlates of neuroanatomy
insufficient_triples.note: The paper (Gliko2024) discusses 813 inhibitory neurons and gene expression correlates of laminar innervation in Martinotti cells, but the abstract does not detail five Vip-MET types or their specific laminar axon innervation patterns; this level of detail likely comes from Gouwens2020a rather than this paper.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Gliko2024` then enlarged the reconstructed inhibitory dataset to 813 cells and showed that arbor-density representations alone classify subclass-level identity (Sst/Pvalb/Vip/Lamp5) with high accuracy while within-VIP boundaries remain less crisp {cite:p}`Gliko2024,Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2020b,Elabbady2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Perisomatic ultrastructure efficiently classifies cells in mouse cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes perisomatic ultrastructural classification of cells in mouse cortex but does not mention arbor-density representations, subclass-level classification of Sst/Pvalb/Vip/Lamp5, or within-VIP boundaries specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The Allen Institute Patch-seq atlas {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019,Gliko2024,Gouwens2020b` partitioned the VISp Vip subclass into five morpho-electric-transcriptomic types (Vip-MET-1 through Vip-MET-5) all resembling bipolar or bitufted cells but differing in laminar axon innervation, with Vip-MET-5 (Vip Crispld2) the principal L1-projecting type and Vip-MET-1/-2 occupying L2/3–L4 with distinct axonal biases.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Toward an integrated classification of neuronal cell types: morphoelectric and transcriptomic characterization of individual GABAergic cortical neurons
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract mentions ~20 interneuron met-types with congruent properties and different Sst met-types innervating different layers, but does not specifically describe five Vip-MET types or their laminar axon innervation details; this is a preprint companion to the peer-reviewed Gouwens2020a which likely contains those details.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Vucurovic2010` then demonstrated, using 5-HT3aR-BAC transgenics combined with in situ hybridisation, that the cortical 5-HT3AR-positive lineage partitions into a VIP-positive subset with bipolar/bitufted morphology and a VIP-negative subset with multipolar morphology — embedding the bipolar/bitufted descriptor in the broader CGE taxonomy {cite:p}`Vucurovic2010,Rudy2011,Cauli2014,Fishell2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Interneuron Types as Attractors and Controllers
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract discusses interneuron classification, developmental trajectories, and evolutionary conservation but does not specifically mention 5-HT3AR, VIP subsets, or bipolar/bitufted morphology in CGE taxonomy.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Demarcogarcia2011` separated VIP-positive multipolar cells from CR-positive bipolar/multipolar siblings in early postnatal cortex, with the two showing distinct activity-dependence of axonal arbor length under in utero electroporation {cite:p}`Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021,Wong2022,Fishell2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Interneuron Types as Attractors and Controllers
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not mention VIP-positive multipolar cells, CR-positive bipolar cells, or activity-dependent axonal arbor length; it discusses interneuron classification at a high level.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Demarcogarcia2011` reported that VIP-positive multipolar cells, in contrast to CR-positive multipolar and bipolar siblings, do not reduce their axonal arbor length under activity-suppression in early postnatal cortex — making axonal length development a class-specific rather than CGE-generic trait {cite:p}`Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021,Wong2022,Fishell2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Interneuron Types as Attractors and Controllers
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not address activity-suppression effects on axonal arbor length or distinguish VIP-positive vs CR-positive cell responses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: First, all of the major rodent reconstructed datasets — {cite:t}`Pronneke2015`, {cite:t}`Gouwens2019`, {cite:t}`Gouwens2020a`, {cite:t}`Gliko2024`, and {cite:t}`Schneidermizell2025` — rely on VIP-Cre genetic labelling, which captures the *Vip*-expressing transcriptomic subclass but does not perfectly resolve VIP-peptide expression on a cell-by-cell basis; the difference matters most for rodent-to-primate translation, where the calretinin-positive analogue is the operational handle {cite:p}`Cauli2014,Medalla2023,Hodge2019,Fishell2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Interneuron Types as Attractors and Controllers
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract discusses interneuron classification and developmental specification broadly but does not address VIP-Cre labelling specificity or rodent-to-primate translation issues.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two intersectional studies further partitioned the class along molecular axes: {cite:t}`Stachniak2021` separated CR-positive bipolar and CCK-positive multipolar VIP subtypes during cortical development, and {cite:t}`Yayon2023` resolved the cholinergic subset (VChIs) of VIP barrel-cortex cells into bipolar (two stem dendrites) and multipolar (≥3 stem dendrites) morphologies {cite:p}`Stachniak2021,Yayon2023,Wong2022,Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival in the developing cerebral cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper focuses on serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival during programmed cell death, not on partitioning VIP subtypes along molecular axes or distinguishing CR-positive bipolar from CCK-positive multipolar subtypes during development.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Demarcogarcia2011` separated VIP-positive multipolar cells from CR-positive bipolar/multipolar siblings in early postnatal cortex, with the two showing distinct activity-dependence of axonal arbor length under in utero electroporation {cite:p}`Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021,Wong2022,Fishell2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival in the developing cerebral cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper studies survival regulation of CGE-derived interneuron subclasses, not the activity-dependence of axonal arbor length under in utero electroporation in VIP-positive multipolar vs CR-positive bipolar cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Gouwens2019` had earlier shown across the whole inhibitory inventory that the axon, not the dendrite, is the dominant feature distinguishing inhibitory m-types in mouse visual cortex; the same paper noted that L2/3 Chat-Cre-labelled neurons (a known subset of Vip cells) map predominantly to a small number of related me-types associated with the Vip-Ptprt-Pkp2 transcriptomic type {cite:p}`Gouwens2019,Gouwens2020a,Yayon2023,Wong2022`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival in the developing cerebral cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper studies programmed cell death of CGE-derived interneurons regulated by serotonin; it does not address morphological classification of inhibitory m-types or Chat-Cre-labelled neuron mapping to transcriptomic types.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Demarcogarcia2011` reported that VIP-positive multipolar cells, in contrast to CR-positive multipolar and bipolar siblings, do not reduce their axonal arbor length under activity-suppression in early postnatal cortex — making axonal length development a class-specific rather than CGE-generic trait {cite:p}`Demarcogarcia2011,Stachniak2021,Wong2022,Fishell2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival in the developing cerebral cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper addresses survival/cell death of CGE-derived interneurons, not axonal arbor length changes under activity suppression in specific VIP subtypes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Stachniak2021` further showed in mouse cortex that VIP cells split during development into a CR-positive bipolar subtype and a CCK-positive multipolar subtype with distinct dendritic geometries, and that genetic perturbations differentially affect the two arms {cite:p}`Stachniak2021,Demarcogarcia2011,Wong2022,Yayon2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival in the developing cerebral cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper focuses on serotonergic modulation of bipolar cell survival during programmed cell death; it does not specifically address the developmental split of VIP cells into CR-positive bipolar vs CCK-positive multipolar subtypes in a way that would contradict the claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Fishell2020` ties these observations together with a transcription-factor framework: embryonic loss of Prox1 abolishes calretinin expression in the bipolar VIP arm and truncates dendrites, establishing Prox1 as a central developmental regulator of the bipolar VIP morphology {cite:p}`Fishell2020,Stachniak2021,Demarcogarcia2011,Wong2022`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival in the developing cerebral cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper does not address Prox1 transcription-factor regulation of calretinin expression or dendritic morphology in VIP bipolar cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Fishell2020` argued that Prox1 is required for the bipolar VIP fate (Prox1 loss abolishes calretinin expression and truncates dendrites), tying the dominant morphology to a specific transcription-factor programme {cite:p}`Fishell2020,Stachniak2021,Wong2022,Demarcogarcia2011`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Serotonergic regulation of bipolar cell survival in the developing cerebral cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The paper does not address Prox1's role in bipolar VIP fate, calretinin expression, or dendritic truncation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Schneidermizell2025` identified, in the MICrONS cubic-millimetre EM volume of mouse VISp, an "inhibition-targeting" cell class (InhTCs) that aligns with the disinhibitory VIP cardinal class and that subdivides into two connectomic subtypes — InhTC_dist (targeting distal-dendrite SST-like cells) and InhTC_peri (targeting perisomatic PV-like basket cells) — refining the long-standing view of a single VIP→SST→Pyr motif {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025,Lee2013,Gamlin2025,Cauli2014`.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The same study reported a striking layer-dependent shift: in L6, ~40% of VIP boutons synapsed onto somata, contrasting with 8% in L2/3 and 8% in L5 — indicating that the canonical "perisomatic basket cell" function is not absent from the VIP class but compartmentalised into deep layers, and aligning with the layer-specific axon innervation that defines Patch-seq met-types {cite:p}`Zhou2017,Gouwens2020a,Schneidermizell2025,Gamlin2025`.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: VIP interneurons are not a single firing class. They are a transcriptomically coherent CGE-derived subclass that constitutes ~12% of neocortical interneurons and is concentrated in superficial layers {cite:p}`Lim2018,Tasic2018,Yao2021a,Machold2023` whose intrinsic-electrophysiological repertoire is dominated by high input resistance, modest sag, fast-rising action potentials, and a heterogeneous mix of irregular-spiking, continuous-adapting, and (in upper layers) bursting firing patterns {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Pronneke2015,Cauli1997`.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Patch-seq has, over the last decade, transformed how this repertoire is parsed: the four GABAergic subclasses (PV, SST, VIP, Lamp5/Sncg) separate cleanly along intrinsic-feature axes both in mouse {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Gouwens2019` and in human cortex {cite:p}`Lee2023a,Kim2023`, but *within* the VIP class, electrophysiological types form a continuum onto which transcriptomic types map only partially {cite:p}`Scala2021,Gouwens2020a`.

The abstract does not provide evidence to contradict, support, or qualify the specific VIP-related claim.

insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP+ cells in L1b but does not report specific firing-type breakdowns (NFS/IS/fast-adapting counts).

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Anastasiades2021a` recorded VIP-Cre × Ai14+ cells in mouse mPFC L1b and reported NFS = 9/17 (53%), IS = 5/17 (29%), and fast-adapting = 3/17 (18%) (n=17 VIP+ cells, 8 mice) {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP+ cells in L1b but does not report specific firing-type breakdowns (NFS/IS/fast-adapting counts).

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Per-entry denominators are annotated on the bars per Phase-6 audit requirement; entries: {cite:p}`Cauli1997` (15 IS cells / 97 nonpyramidal interneurons sampled = 15.5% of nonpyramidal pool; the 15 IS cells co-expressed CR+VIP, rat S1, single-cell RT-PCR + patch); {cite:p}`Cauli2000` (16/60 = 26.7% IS-VIP cluster, rat frontal cortex L2–3, fusiform interneurons clustered by RT-PCR + patch); {cite:p}`Pronneke2015` (14.5% of whole-population VIP-Cre/tdTomato barrel cortex); {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a` (5/17 = 29.4% of mPFC L1b VIP-Cre × Ai14+ cells, mouse). *Caveat (verbatim from Phase-6 audit):* Denominators differ across entries; per-entry denominators MUST be shown in the figure (cannot be presented as a single comparable percentage without that annotation). | Rat (Cauli) vs mouse (Anastasiades) species mismatch. | Different cortical areas (S1, frontal cortex, mPFC L1b) and slightly different firing-class taxonomies. *Panel D:* Single-row entry showing membrane time constant τ_{m} (ms) for the only entry retained after Phase-6 audit removed {cite:t}`Rhomberg2018` from the τ_{m} sub-comparison ({cite:p}`Michaud2024` reports τ_{m} = 36.5 ± 3.2 ms, n=7 VIP/CR+ I-S3 cells, mouse CA1 stratum radiatum); panel reduced to a single-row entry per the iter3 audit.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report IS fraction or firing-type breakdown for VIP+ cells in L1b.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The widespread textbook claim that "VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons" is contested {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a` against a robust but quantitatively heterogeneous canonical motif {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a`, and represents the first major divergence between canonical schematic and contemporary data.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not address VIP output targeting specificity or contest the 'VIP inhibits only interneurons' claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In mouse barrel cortex, vibrissa motor cortex (vM1) projects more strongly onto VIP cells than onto neighbouring pyramidal neurons, with input ratios of roughly threefold {cite:p}`Lee2013, Williams2019, Audette2018`; analogous over-targeting of VIP by long-range cortical fibres has been most cleanly demonstrated for the cingulate→V1 projection {cite:p}`Zhang2014`, with related VIP-preferential top-down recruitment reported in mPFC and frontal-feedback contexts {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a, Wall2016`; orbital→sensory and other higher-order routes are not yet independently mapped at the same resolution and we treat them here as extrapolations from the cingulate→V1 result.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper studies thalamo-prefrontal pathways, not vM1→barrel cortex VIP input ratios.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A monosynaptic-rabies survey by {cite:t}`Wall2016` formalised this point at scale: VIP cells in mouse V1 receive disproportionately more long-range cortical input — relative to local pyramidal input — than do SST or PV cells in the same preparation, an asymmetry that has since been reproduced across primary sensory cortices and prefrontal cortex {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is not a rabies survey; it uses optogenetics/tracing in PFC. Claim attributes rabies survey to wrong paper.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Higher-order thalamic nuclei — POm in the somatosensory pathway and pulvinar/LP in the visual stream — innervate VIP cells robustly, often with response probabilities and amplitudes comparable to those onto pyramidal targets {cite:p}`Williams2019, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a, Ramamurthy2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper studies MD and VM thalamus→PFC, not POm or pulvinar/LP→VIP in somatosensory/visual pathways.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: By contrast, primary thalamic relays (VPM, LGN, MGv) excite VIP cells more weakly, and several reports place VPM-driven excitation of PV cells above that of VIP {cite:p}`Cruikshank2012, Ji2016, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper does not study VPM, LGN, or MGv inputs; it examines MD and VM thalamus to PFC.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, the rabies-tracing asymmetry of {cite:t}`Wall2016` has been recapitulated across cortical areas with both pseudo-typed rabies and CTB injection assays, all converging on the same direction of effect even when absolute counts differ {cite:p}`Wall2016, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a, Ramamurthy2023, Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper does not use rabies tracing or CTB injection assays to assess long-range input asymmetry.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, although Cg→V1 robustly recruits VIP {cite:p}`Zhang2014`, A1→V1 by contrast *suppresses* VIP cells {cite:p}`Ibrahim2016`, and direct optogenetic VIP perturbations do not always replicate the modulatory effects ascribed to attentional state in earlier work {cite:p}`Ibrahim2016, Sabri2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is about mPFC thalamic circuits, not about Cg→V1 VIP recruitment or A1→V1 VIP suppression.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The dominant cortical schematic — VIP cells inhibit other interneurons, principally SST — is supported in superficial layers but does not survive a deeper-layer or in vivo cell-attached test {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper discusses L1 circuits in PFC but doesn't directly test whether VIP→SST dominance fails in deeper layers or in vivo cell-attached.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In a complementary slice study, {cite:t}`Zhou2017` reported direct VIP→pyramidal connectivity in mPFC L5 with paired recordings, and {cite:t}`Anastasiades2021a` confirmed that this projection is dense enough to be physiologically consequential during MD-thalamic recruitment of VIP cells.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions MD recruits VIP which targets SOM, but does not mention direct VIP→pyramidal connectivity or paired recordings in L5.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: These layer- and area-specific direct VIP→Pyr and VIP→PV connections are not at odds with {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` once the comparison is read carefully: the original measurements were made in V1 L2/3 paired recordings (where superficial pyramids receive only sparse VIP input) and the resolution-status entry in the conflicts table is layer-dependent, with L2/3 canonical disinhibition coexisting with L5/6 direct VIP→PV/Pyr connectivity {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss layer-dependent VIP→Pyr or VIP→PV connectivity comparisons between L2/3 and L5/6.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Independent reports of meaningful VIP→PV connection rates in barrel and frontal cortices {cite:p}`Walker2016, Anastasiades2021a, Pi2013` and of VIP synapses onto NDNF/L1 interneurons {cite:p}`Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Abs2018` further enlarge the output set.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention VIP synapses onto NDNF/L1 interneurons; it describes NDNF and VIP as separate populations.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Long-range thalamic and cortico-cortical inputs onto VIP cells are likewise dominated by facilitation: anterior thalamic input onto VIP cells in mouse presubiculum is overtly facilitating across paired-pulse ratios {cite:p}`Nassar2025`, and POm input onto S1 VIP cells supports sustained activation rather than transient bursts {cite:p}`Williams2019, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss short-term plasticity dynamics (facilitation/depression) of thalamic inputs onto VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The convergence of three independent methodological lines — paired recording, optogenetic dissection and EM ultrastructure — on the same compartment-specific output rule strengthens the case that the canonical schematic is correct *as far as it goes*, while leaving open the layer- and area-specific exceptions documented in earlier subsections {cite:p}`Hajos1996, Defelipe1999, Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss compartment-specific output rules, paired recording/EM convergence on subcellular targeting of VIP outputs.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Direct optogenetic VIP perturbations during cross-modal attention do not reliably replicate the modulation reported in earlier indirect manipulations {cite:p}`Ibrahim2016, Anastasiades2021a, Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper does not discuss cross-modal attention or compare optogenetic VIP perturbations with indirect manipulations.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **(iv) Direct VIP→pyramid connectivity.** The schematic claim that VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons is contradicted by Yu 2019, Zhou 2017 and several intersectional in vivo dissections {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper discusses VIP cells in L1b and their targeting of SOM+ interneurons but does not address direct VIP→pyramid connectivity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The resolution is layer- and area-specific: in V1 L2/3 the canonical schematic remains a defensible first approximation, but in deep layers and in mPFC the direct VIP→Pyr path is part of the wiring and ignoring it produces predictions that fail in vivo {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016, Pfeffer2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss direct VIP→Pyr pathway or its layer/area specificity; focuses on thalamic input to L1 interneurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP→SOM targeting but does not discuss VIP inhibiting PV in deeper layers, NDNF cells, pyramidal cells, or OLM cells directly.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pharmacological and optogenetic isolation of cholinergic transmission has since shown that VIP cells across V1, A1, S1, and PFC respond rapidly to ACh release through α4β2 and α7 nicotinic receptors {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019,Anastasiades2021a,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b`, with response amplitudes that can rival those produced by direct optogenetic stimulation of long-range glutamatergic axons.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention cholinergic/nicotinic receptor responses of VIP cells; focuses on thalamic glutamatergic inputs to L1 interneurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In PFC, attention and working-memory tasks recruit VIP cells whose silencing impairs performance {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention VIP silencing impairing attention or working-memory task performance; focuses on circuit anatomy and physiology.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In PFC, the motif is engaged in attention and working-memory tasks {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Pinto2015,Anastasiades2021a`, but PFC VIP cells receive a richer mix of long-range inputs and project to a broader set of postsynaptic targets, including direct VIP→pyramidal connections that may be more prominent than in sensory cortex {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows MD→VIP and VM→NDNF L1 circuits but does not discuss VIP→pyramidal connections or broader postsynaptic target sets vs sensory cortex.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In PFC, the motif is engaged in attention and working-memory tasks {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Pinto2015,Anastasiades2021a`, but PFC VIP cells receive a richer mix of long-range inputs and project to a broader set of postsynaptic targets, including direct VIP→pyramidal connections that may be more prominent than in sensory cortex {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not address direct VIP→pyramidal connections or comparisons of VIP postsynaptic target breadth between PFC and sensory cortex.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In addition to these four families, a number of motif fragments are documented but less developed in the literature: VIP→Martinotti subtype-specific connections in deep layers {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`, VIP→long-range projecting interneuron contacts, and VIP-cell mediated inhibition of other VIP cells through CCK-positive subtypes {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Garrett2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP→Martinotti deep-layer connections or VIP→VIP inhibition via CCK subtypes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` reported that a ChAT-expressing subset of cortical VIP cells *excites* (rather than inhibits) neighbouring pyramidal and interneurons through fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission {cite:p}`Obermayer2019`. These observations do not overturn the basic intrinsic-feature consensus, but they establish that VIP intrinsic biophysics is plastic, conditional, and partially uncoupled from the canonical "disinhibitor" framing {cite:p}`Pi2013,Pfeffer2013,Karnani2016a` — a point that {ref}`sec-disinhibition-framework` and the concluding synthesis revisit.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Pi2013 describes canonical disinhibitory framing; abstract says nothing about ChAT+ VIP subset or cholinergic co-transmission.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The widespread textbook claim that "VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons" is contested {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a` against a robust but quantitatively heterogeneous canonical motif {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a`, and represents the first major divergence between canonical schematic and contemporary data.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Pi2013 shows VIP primarily inhibits SST/PV interneurons but abstract doesn't directly contest the 'only interneurons' claim in those terms.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: First, the vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio of ~2.9× reported by {cite:t}`Lee2013` is independently reproduced in similar form for vS1→Cg, ACC→V1 and OFC→sensory pathways {cite:p}`Zhang2014, Bilash2023, Stachniak2023, Pi2013, Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Pi2013 abstract does not report a vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio of ~2.9×; that specific quantitative detail is not mentioned.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Independent reports of meaningful VIP→PV connection rates in barrel and frontal cortices {cite:p}`Walker2016, Anastasiades2021a, Pi2013` and of VIP synapses onto NDNF/L1 interneurons {cite:p}`Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Abs2018` further enlarge the output set.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Pi2013 mentions VIP suppresses primarily SST and a fraction of PV; does not report VIP→PV connection rates in barrel/frontal cortex specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: VIP cells therefore inhibit pyramidal neurons less than they inhibit SST cells, but their direct pyramidal output is not zero, and the connection probability scales with cortical depth and source area in a way that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016, Pi2013, Schuman2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Pi2013 does not discuss layer-dependent connection probability scaling or direct pyramidal output quantification in that manner.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The resolution from independent replications {cite:p}`Walker2016, Pi2013, Anastasiades2021a` is that VIP→PV connectivity is layer-dependent: superficial L2/3 microcircuits cleanly support the canonical interpretation, but L5/6 microcircuits — and especially in non-V1 cortices — recruit a direct VIP→PV path that the canonical schematic misses {cite:p}`Yu2019, Walker2016, Anastasiades2021a, Pi2013, Zhou2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Pi2013 reports VIP suppresses primarily SST and fraction of PV but does not address layer-dependent VIP→PV connectivity resolution.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The resolution from independent replications {cite:p}`Walker2016, Pi2013, Anastasiades2021a` is that VIP→PV connectivity is layer-dependent: superficial L2/3 microcircuits cleanly support the canonical interpretation, but L5/6 microcircuits — and especially in non-V1 cortices — recruit a direct VIP→PV path that the canonical schematic misses {cite:p}`Yu2019, Walker2016, Anastasiades2021a, Pi2013, Zhou2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; Pi2013 abstract does not discuss layer-dependent VIP→PV connectivity differences.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The Pfeffer 2013 paired-recording survey reported negligible *functional* VIP→PV connection probability in V1 L2/3 {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013`, whereas anatomical / synaptic-counting work — including the Pi 2013 framing of VIP cells as canonical disinhibitors of multiple interneuron classes — implies that VIP+ axons do form symmetric synapses onto PV soma and proximal dendrites at non-trivial incidence {cite:p}`Pi2013, Walker2016, Karnani2016a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract says VIP suppresses SST and 'a fraction of PV' interneurons but doesn't discuss anatomical synapse counts or VIP→PV connection probability specifics.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Once one accepts that VIP outputs include direct PV and pyramidal inhibition in some layers and areas, that long-range afferents are a structured asymmetry rather than a uniform "top-down→VIP" rule, and that neuromodulator pharmacology is mostly but not entirely excitatory, the apparent disagreements collapse to a smaller set of well-characterised quantitative gradients along axis variables (layer, area, source nucleus, behavioural state) that future work could in principle saturate {cite:p}`Wall2016, Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Sabri2024, Anastasiades2021a, Pronneke2019, Tasic2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: The claim synthesizes findings across 467 papers; the abstract doesn't address most specific details cited (layers, NDNF, noradrenergic input, etc.).

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Independently, {cite:t}`Pi2013` showed in mouse auditory cortex that VIP cells were activated by foot-shock and noise via a long-range projection from cingulate areas and that VIP-cell activation released pyramidal neurons from SST-mediated inhibition; deleting GABA release from VIP cells abolished the disinhibitory effect of cingulate input.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't mention cingulate projection, foot-shock specifics, or GABA-release deletion from VIP cells. Details exceed what abstract provides.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013` reported that long-range cingulate input was the dominant recruiter in auditory cortex during reinforcement, while {cite:t}`Hangya2015` demonstrated that basal-forebrain cholinergic neurons fire phasically to behaviourally relevant cues in a manner consistent with recruiting VIP cells through nicotinic receptors.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't mention cingulate input or long-range cortical projections as dominant recruiters. Reinforcement signals activated VIP, but source not specified.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The downstream consequence is that the canonical motif is engaged most strongly when external salience and internal arousal coincide, and only weakly engaged by either alone. This reconciles the apparent disagreement between {cite:t}`Pi2013` and {cite:t}`Fu2014` over which input is ‘primary’: in their respective preparations and behavioural epochs, each was the dominant driver, but neither study tested the alternate input under the other's task structure.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss which input is 'primary' or compare glutamatergic vs cholinergic recruitment; doesn't address alternate input testing.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013` likewise emphasised long-range cortical drive in auditory cortex, while {cite:t}`Fu2014` reported that nicotinic cholinergic input from basal forebrain was sufficient to drive locomotion-evoked VIP recruitment in the same area. **Resolution:** partial.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't mention long-range cortical drive as dominant route or compare it to cholinergic input in auditory cortex.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013` framed VIP-mediated disinhibition as the principal gating mechanism for cingulate-driven cortical gain in auditory cortex.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes reinforcement-driven VIP recruitment and gain modulation but doesn't specifically mention cingulate-driven cortical gain as principal mechanism.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: :::{admonition} Conflict :class: conflict **Is VIP disinhibition *the* privileged channel for behavioural-state gain?** {cite:t}`Pi2013` framed VIP→SST disinhibition as the principal gating mechanism through which cingulate input enhances cortical responses.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't frame VIP→SST disinhibition as specifically gated by cingulate input; reinforcement signals noted but source unspecified.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` reported pooled connection probabilities in paired recordings, in which VIP→PV pairs do exist but are rare; {cite:t}`Pi2013` reported within-cell IPSC waveforms in cells that received any VIP input, normalising away the rarity.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't specify paired recordings or pooled connection probabilities as the methodology for circuit analysis.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013`, in mouse auditory cortex, reported that VIP cells inhibit both SST and PV cells with kinetically distinct IPSCs (decay τ ≈ 18 ms onto SST, ≈ 6 ms onto PV; Pi 2013, error metric ‘± as printed’, n not reported in the cited row). **Resolution:** the metrics differ — connection probability versus within-pair IPSC waveform — and both can hold simultaneously {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms VIP inhibits SST and PV in auditory cortex but does not report specific IPSC decay time constants.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013` reported, in auditory cortex, that VIP-mediated inhibition of pyramidal cells was substantial enough that direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition needed to be considered alongside disinhibition.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract emphasizes disinhibition via SST/PV suppression; does not mention substantial direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition needing consideration.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The variability is attributable to (i) cortical area and layer, (ii) opsin dose and behavioural state during the perturbation, (iii) co-recruitment of VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b,Lee2013,Pi2013`, and (iv) co-recruitment of NDNF/Layer-1 interneurons whose top-down drive partially overlaps with that of VIP cells {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss co-recruitment of VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal cells as a source of variability.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition.** A subset of VIP cells, most prominently the calretinin-positive, bipolar morphological type, contact pyramidal somata and proximal dendrites and produce direct inhibitory currents large enough to suppress pyramidal firing under volley activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't mention calretinin-positive bipolar VIP cells contacting pyramidal somata or direct inhibitory currents on pyramidal cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Panel A places the VIP→SST IPSC measurements of {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013`, {cite:t}`Lee2013`, and {cite:t}`Pi2013` on a single axis.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't report specific VIP→SST IPSC amplitude measurements suitable for cross-study comparison on a single axis.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: VIP cells fire fast, narrow APs (half-width ≈ 0.95 ms in juvenile S1 {cite:p}`Vucurovic2010`; AP amplitude 65.1 ± 9.1 mV in barrel cortex {cite:p}`Pronneke2015`) — narrower than pyramidal neurons but broader than PV fast-spiking cells, again consistent with the broader cortical interneuron taxonomy {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Karnani2016a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report AP half-width, amplitude, or intrinsic electrophysiological parameters for VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Across these comparisons, the rule that survives is structural rather than numeric: the VIP/CGE subclass occupies a high-R_{in}, low-rheobase, narrow-AP corner of the cortical-interneuron biophysical manifold, with within-class scatter that is real but bounded {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Pronneke2015,Vucurovic2010,Wierenga2010,Karnani2016a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss R_in, rheobase, AP width, or biophysical manifold positioning of VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two further details place the intrinsic profile in its developmental and circuit context: all cortical VIP cells originate from the CGE {cite:p}`Lim2018,Cauli2014`, segregating sharply from the MGE-derived PV/SST classes that account for the bulk of the rest of the inhibitory taxonomy {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Karnani2016a,Pfeffer2013`; and a small ChAT-expressing VIP subpopulation directly *excites* (rather than inhibits) neighbouring pyramidal and interneurons through fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission {cite:p}`Obermayer2019` — a counter-example to the strict-disinhibitor framing that {ref}`sec-synaptic-properties` returns to in detail.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions GABAergic and cholinergic mechanisms in VIP INs but does not describe a ChAT+ VIP subpopulation with fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission onto pyramidal cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` reported that a ChAT-expressing subset of cortical VIP cells *excites* (rather than inhibits) neighbouring pyramidal and interneurons through fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission {cite:p}`Obermayer2019`. These observations do not overturn the basic intrinsic-feature consensus, but they establish that VIP intrinsic biophysics is plastic, conditional, and partially uncoupled from the canonical "disinhibitor" framing {cite:p}`Pi2013,Pfeffer2013,Karnani2016a` — a point that {ref}`sec-disinhibition-framework` and the concluding synthesis revisit.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not specifically report ChAT+ VIP cells exciting neighbours via cholinergic + GABA co-transmission.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The widespread textbook claim that "VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons" is contested {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a` against a robust but quantitatively heterogeneous canonical motif {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a`, and represents the first major divergence between canonical schematic and contemporary data.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not contest the claim that VIP cells inhibit only interneurons; it focuses on cooperative IN population activity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Connection probabilities from local L2/3 pyramidal cells onto VIP cells are comparable to (and in some preparations slightly lower than) those onto SST and PV interneurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a`, and unitary EPSC amplitudes are correspondingly small {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report connection probabilities from pyramidal cells onto VIP vs SST/PV interneurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Connection probabilities from local L2/3 pyramidal cells onto VIP cells are comparable to (and in some preparations slightly lower than) those onto SST and PV interneurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a`, and unitary EPSC amplitudes are correspondingly small {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report unitary EPSC amplitudes onto VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: What distinguishes the local pyramidal→VIP synapse is its short-term plasticity profile: VIP cells in mouse barrel and visual cortex receive predominantly *depressing* excitation from local pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014`, in contrast to the strongly facilitating Pyr→SST synapses described in earlier classical work {cite:p}`Pronneke2015, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not describe short-term plasticity profiles of pyramidal→VIP synapses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Paired recordings and SST-Cre optogenetics consistently show that SST interneurons provide the largest single source of local IPSCs onto VIP cells, with connection probabilities that meet or exceed the reciprocal VIP→SST direction in the same preparations {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Karnani2016a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions GABAergic disinhibition among INs but does not report SST→VIP connection probabilities or paired recordings.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Mutual inhibition between VIP cells themselves is sparse but reliably detected at the single-cell level {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Karnani2016a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not specifically report VIP→VIP mutual inhibition at the single-cell level.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Independent replication has come from {cite:t}`Pi2013` in auditory cortex, {cite:t}`Lee2013` in barrel cortex, {cite:t}`Karnani2016a` and {cite:t}`Karnani2016b` in V1 with single-cell two-photon resolution, and {cite:t}`Sabri2024` in V1 chemogenetics.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP/SST cooperative mechanisms but doesn't specifically report VIP→SST inhibitory charge transfer or replication of that finding.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The motif is not confined to mouse: pharmacological and optogenetic dissection in rat {cite:p}`Walker2016, Pronneke2019, Pronneke2015` and comparative meta-analytic syntheses across V1, A1 and S1 {cite:p}`Guetmccreight2020b, Pronneke2019, Karnani2016a` reach the same architectural conclusion.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't describe cross-species comparisons or meta-analytic syntheses across V1, A1, S1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Karnani2016a` directly compared local pyramidal-onto-VIP and pyramidal-onto-SOM synapses in V1 and reported similar connection rates but opposite short-term dynamics — pyramidal→VIP synapses were *depressing* while pyramidal→SOM synapses were facilitating.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't mention short-term plasticity dynamics (depressing vs facilitating) of pyramidal→VIP or pyramidal→SOM synapses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Cauli2014` similarly described that CR/VIP bipolar interneurons receive predominantly depressing excitatory input from local pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014, Walker2016, Pronneke2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't specifically describe CR/VIP bipolar interneurons receiving depressing excitatory input from pyramidal neurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Plasticity at the VIP→SST synapse, in turn, places experience-dependent gain control inside the disinhibitory loop rather than only at its pyramidal output {cite:p}`Mcfarlan2024a, Karnani2016a, Wiera2024, Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss plasticity at VIP→SST synapse or experience-dependent gain control within the disinhibitory loop.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The triple — source-dependent excitatory afferents (depressing locally, facilitating from long-range), plastic facilitating GABAergic outputs, and target-specific co-transmission — together gives the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr motif a distinctive temporal and learning signature that is not captured by treating it as a static schematic {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014, Mcfarlan2024a, Granger2020, Kaneko2024, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes cooperative IN mechanisms but not source-dependent plasticity, co-transmission, or learning signatures specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The Pfeffer 2013 paired-recording survey reported negligible *functional* VIP→PV connection probability in V1 L2/3 {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013`, whereas anatomical / synaptic-counting work — including the Pi 2013 framing of VIP cells as canonical disinhibitors of multiple interneuron classes — implies that VIP+ axons do form symmetric synapses onto PV soma and proximal dendrites at non-trivial incidence {cite:p}`Pi2013, Walker2016, Karnani2016a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss VIP→PV connection probability or anatomical vs functional connectivity discrepancies.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Once one accepts that VIP outputs include direct PV and pyramidal inhibition in some layers and areas, that long-range afferents are a structured asymmetry rather than a uniform "top-down→VIP" rule, and that neuromodulator pharmacology is mostly but not entirely excitatory, the apparent disagreements collapse to a smaller set of well-characterised quantitative gradients along axis variables (layer, area, source nucleus, behavioural state) that future work could in principle saturate {cite:p}`Wall2016, Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Sabri2024, Anastasiades2021a, Pronneke2019, Tasic2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss layer/area gradients, direct PV inhibition, or long-range afferent asymmetries in the manner claimed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't provide the comprehensive input/output/behavioural synthesis described in the claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A useful operational summary is the following. *Treat the VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibitory motif as a layer- and area-specific computation that a cortical microcircuit can perform when modulator-targeted afferents are active, not as a fixed wiring rule that all VIP cells obey under all conditions*. This formulation accommodates the canonical landmark results {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Fu2014, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b`, the long-range and thalamic asymmetries {cite:p}`Wall2016, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Cruikshank2012`, the modulator pharmacology {cite:p}`Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Arroyo2012, Vonengelhardt2007, Granger2020, Obermayer2019, Letzkus2011, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017`, the layer- and area-specific exceptions to canonical disinhibition {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`, the developmental and comparative qualifications {cite:p}`Favuzzi2019, Batistabrito2017, Qiu2020, Simacek2025a, Vormsteinschneider2020, Defelipe1999`, and the hippocampal divergence {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018, Bilash2023, Tamboli2024, Toth1993` within a single coherent picture.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses cooperative IN subnetworks but not the specific operational summary or layer/area exceptions described.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Karnani2016a` and {cite:t}`Karnani2016b` then showed that, when measured at higher resolution, VIP cells in V1 also generate measurable, behaviourally relevant inhibition onto pyramidal somata and dendrites, and that the magnitude of VIP→Pyr inhibition becomes large enough to mute pyramidal firing under volley activation.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't mention VIP→Pyr direct inhibition or volley activation muting pyramidal firing.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013`, in mouse auditory cortex, reported that VIP cells inhibit both SST and PV cells with kinetically distinct IPSCs (decay τ ≈ 18 ms onto SST, ≈ 6 ms onto PV; Pi 2013, error metric ‘± as printed’, n not reported in the cited row). **Resolution:** the metrics differ — connection probability versus within-pair IPSC waveform — and both can hold simultaneously {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses SOM/VIP cooperative subnetworks, not VIP→SST/PV IPSC kinetics or auditory cortex connectivity details.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The contemporary reading {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Schneidermizell2025` is that the direct channel is detectable in most preparations but is dominated, on average, by the disinhibitory channel; net pyramidal modulation reflects the balance and can flip sign with task condition. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss direct vs disinhibitory channel balance on pyramidal firing or net sign flipping with task condition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The direction of this effect is consistent across {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a,Pinto2015,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses IN cooperative activity/amplification, not optogenetic VIP recruitment enhancing pyramidal firing during attention in V1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition.** A subset of VIP cells, most prominently the calretinin-positive, bipolar morphological type, contact pyramidal somata and proximal dendrites and produce direct inhibitory currents large enough to suppress pyramidal firing under volley activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition, calretinin-positive bipolar morphology, or suppression of pyramidal firing.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In addition to these four families, a number of motif fragments are documented but less developed in the literature: VIP→Martinotti subtype-specific connections in deep layers {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`, VIP→long-range projecting interneuron contacts, and VIP-cell mediated inhibition of other VIP cells through CCK-positive subtypes {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Garrett2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP→Martinotti subtype-specific connections or VIP-cell mediated inhibition of other VIP cells via CCK subtypes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **The disinhibition motif and its alternatives.** **(A)** Canonical motif: VIP cells, recruited by long-range glutamatergic and ascending cholinergic inputs, preferentially inhibit SST cells, releasing pyramidal dendrites from inhibition; minor VIP→PV and VIP→pyramidal edges are drawn explicitly to flag that the canonical schematic is a tendency rather than an exclusive wiring rule {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a`. **(B)** Three alternative motifs documented in the same evidence base: direct VIP→PV control of perisomatic inhibition {cite:p}`Pi2013,Karnani2016b,Kuchibhotla2017`, direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition by bipolar/CR-positive VIP cells {cite:p}`Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`, and a layer-1 VIP→NDNF disinhibitory motif gating apical dendritic input {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`; the hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket motif is a homologue with a distinct oscillatory context {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020`. **(C)** Decision tree mapping behavioural context — locomotion, attentional top-down, reinforcement, fear/learning — to the motif most consistent with the empirical recruitment data and to the supporting cite-keys; the assignment is qualitative because the literature does not yet contain joint manipulations of multiple motifs under matched conditions {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Krabbe2019,Veit2023`. **(D)** Schematic legend distinguishing measured edges (solid) from inferred or context-dependent edges (dashed). *Caveat:* this figure is a schematic with no quantitative data substrate; assignment of behavioural context to dominant motif in Panel C is inferred from joint reading of the cited recruitment studies and is not the output of a meta-analytic weighting. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract focuses on IN cooperative subnetworks, not the specific canonical disinhibition motif wiring details claimed here.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **The disinhibition motif and its alternatives.** **(A)** Canonical motif: VIP cells, recruited by long-range glutamatergic and ascending cholinergic inputs, preferentially inhibit SST cells, releasing pyramidal dendrites from inhibition; minor VIP→PV and VIP→pyramidal edges are drawn explicitly to flag that the canonical schematic is a tendency rather than an exclusive wiring rule {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a`. **(B)** Three alternative motifs documented in the same evidence base: direct VIP→PV control of perisomatic inhibition {cite:p}`Pi2013,Karnani2016b,Kuchibhotla2017`, direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition by bipolar/CR-positive VIP cells {cite:p}`Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`, and a layer-1 VIP→NDNF disinhibitory motif gating apical dendritic input {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`; the hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket motif is a homologue with a distinct oscillatory context {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020`. **(C)** Decision tree mapping behavioural context — locomotion, attentional top-down, reinforcement, fear/learning — to the motif most consistent with the empirical recruitment data and to the supporting cite-keys; the assignment is qualitative because the literature does not yet contain joint manipulations of multiple motifs under matched conditions {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Krabbe2019,Veit2023`. **(D)** Schematic legend distinguishing measured edges (solid) from inferred or context-dependent edges (dashed). *Caveat:* this figure is a schematic with no quantitative data substrate; assignment of behavioural context to dominant motif in Panel C is inferred from joint reading of the cited recruitment studies and is not the output of a meta-analytic weighting. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cooperative Subnetworks of Molecularly Similar Interneurons in Mouse Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; abstract does not address canonical VIP disinhibition motif details or alternative motif taxonomy described.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two further details place the intrinsic profile in its developmental and circuit context: all cortical VIP cells originate from the CGE {cite:p}`Lim2018,Cauli2014`, segregating sharply from the MGE-derived PV/SST classes that account for the bulk of the rest of the inhibitory taxonomy {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Karnani2016a,Pfeffer2013`; and a small ChAT-expressing VIP subpopulation directly *excites* (rather than inhibits) neighbouring pyramidal and interneurons through fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission {cite:p}`Obermayer2019` — a counter-example to the strict-disinhibitor framing that {ref}`sec-synaptic-properties` returns to in detail.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP→SST inhibition but says nothing about CGE origins, ChAT+ VIP subpopulations, or cholinergic co-transmission.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` reported that a ChAT-expressing subset of cortical VIP cells *excites* (rather than inhibits) neighbouring pyramidal and interneurons through fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission {cite:p}`Obermayer2019`. These observations do not overturn the basic intrinsic-feature consensus, but they establish that VIP intrinsic biophysics is plastic, conditional, and partially uncoupled from the canonical "disinhibitor" framing {cite:p}`Pi2013,Pfeffer2013,Karnani2016a` — a point that {ref}`sec-disinhibition-framework` and the concluding synthesis revisit.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention ChAT-expressing VIP cells, cholinergic co-transmission, or GABA co-release.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cells deliberately encode categories, not magnitudes. **(B)** Qualitative VIP output matrix across cortex L2/3, L5/6 and CA1; "n.t." = not tested in the cited preparations. **(C)** The single Phase-6-audited quantitative comparison: VIP→SST IPSQ = 4.6 ± 1.5 pC versus VIP→Pyr IPSQ = 0.6 ± 0.2 pC (and INC 0.42 ± 0.14 pC vs 0.06 ± 0.02 pC, scaled ×10 for visibility) from V1 L2/3 paired recordings (n = 11) {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013`. **(D)** Schematic of cholinergic recruitment of VIP / 5-HT3AR cells: nicotinic (slow, β2-containing) excitation and a smaller muscarinic inhibitory effect onto a subset of VIP cells, after {cite:t}`Porter1999`, {cite:t}`Arroyo2012`, {cite:t}`Fu2014`, {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` and {cite:t}`Granger2020`. **Caveat — qualitative.** All cells in panels A and B are categorical consensus; only panel C carries an audited numeric comparison. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report specific IPSQ values (4.6±1.5 pC vs 0.6±0.2 pC). These quantitative details cannot be verified from the abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` — using paired recordings combined with VIP-Cre/ChR2 stimulation in mouse V1 L2/3 — quantified IPSCs onto simultaneously recorded SST and pyramidal targets and reported a roughly seven-fold larger inhibitory charge transfer onto SST cells than onto pyramids (IPSQ 4.6 ± 1.5 pC vs 0.6 ± 0.2 pC, n = 11; INC 0.42 ± 0.14 pC vs 0.06 ± 0.02 pC) (audited; {numref}`fig-vip-connectome`C).
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms VIP preferentially inhibits SST but does not report specific IPSQ numeric values or sample sizes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The resolution is layer- and area-specific: in V1 L2/3 the canonical schematic remains a defensible first approximation, but in deep layers and in mPFC the direct VIP→Pyr path is part of the wiring and ignoring it produces predictions that fail in vivo {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016, Pfeffer2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes V1 L2/3 and deep layers but does not discuss mPFC or direct VIP→Pyr path failures.
