insufficient_triples.claim_text: Once one accepts that VIP outputs include direct PV and pyramidal inhibition in some layers and areas, that long-range afferents are a structured asymmetry rather than a uniform "top-down→VIP" rule, and that neuromodulator pharmacology is mostly but not entirely excitatory, the apparent disagreements collapse to a smaller set of well-characterised quantitative gradients along axis variables (layer, area, source nucleus, behavioural state) that future work could in principle saturate {cite:p}`Wall2016, Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Sabri2024, Anastasiades2021a, Pronneke2019, Tasic2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes connectivity in V1 only; does not discuss layer/area gradients, modulators, or thalamic asymmetries.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract covers V1 interneuron connectivity only; synthesis across 467 papers, modulators, hippocampus not in abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Quantitatively, optogenetic activation of VIP cells produced inhibitory postsynaptic charge in simultaneously recorded SST cells that was an order of magnitude larger than in pyramidal cells, the canonical complementary-connectivity result {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract states VIP preferentially inhibits SST but does not mention order-of-magnitude charge ratio vs pyramidal cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` reported pooled connection probabilities in paired recordings, in which VIP→PV pairs do exist but are rare; {cite:t}`Pi2013` reported within-cell IPSC waveforms in cells that received any VIP input, normalising away the rarity.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not specify paired-recording methodology or that VIP→PV pairs exist but are rare.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` reported VIP→pyramidal IPSCs roughly an order of magnitude smaller than VIP→SST IPSCs in matched paired recordings.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibition of inhibition in visual cortex: the logic of connections between molecularly distinct interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract states VIP preferentially inhibits SST but does not report specific IPSC amplitude ratios or order-of-magnitude comparisons with VIP→Pyr.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The widespread textbook claim that "VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons" is contested {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a` against a robust but quantitatively heterogeneous canonical motif {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a`, and represents the first major divergence between canonical schematic and contemporary data.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2013 establishes the canonical VIP→SST motif; abstract doesn't contest 'VIP inhibits only interneurons' claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A monosynaptic-rabies survey by {cite:t}`Wall2016` formalised this point at scale: VIP cells in mouse V1 receive disproportionately more long-range cortical input — relative to local pyramidal input — than do SST or PV cells in the same preparation, an asymmetry that has since been reproduced across primary sensory cortices and prefrontal cortex {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2013 describes a VIP-mediated disinhibitory circuit in S1 driven by motor cortex, but does not quantify long-range vs local input asymmetry across cell types using monosynaptic rabies or similar methods.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: First, the vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio of ~2.9× reported by {cite:t}`Lee2013` is independently reproduced in similar form for vS1→Cg, ACC→V1 and OFC→sensory pathways {cite:p}`Zhang2014, Bilash2023, Stachniak2023, Pi2013, Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report a specific ~2.9× vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio; it says vM1 strongly recruited VIP cells but no exact number.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Once one accepts that VIP outputs include direct PV and pyramidal inhibition in some layers and areas, that long-range afferents are a structured asymmetry rather than a uniform "top-down→VIP" rule, and that neuromodulator pharmacology is mostly but not entirely excitatory, the apparent disagreements collapse to a smaller set of well-characterised quantitative gradients along axis variables (layer, area, source nucleus, behavioural state) that future work could in principle saturate {cite:p}`Wall2016, Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Sabri2024, Anastasiades2021a, Pronneke2019, Tasic2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes the canonical motif; doesn't discuss direct VIP→PV or VIP→Pyr inhibition in other layers/areas.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract reports canonical VIP→SST motif; doesn't address cholinergic/serotonergic pharmacology or broader synthesis claims.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; abstract doesn't discuss VIP→PV, VIP→Pyr, deeper layer outputs, or heterogeneity flattened by canonical schematic.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: :::{admonition} Conflict :class: conflict **Top-down glutamatergic vs neuromodulatory cholinergic drive as the primary VIP recruiter.** {cite:t}`Lee2013` reported in mouse barrel cortex (S1) that long-range glutamatergic input from primary motor cortex (vM1) was a dominant route for VIP recruitment driving disinhibition, with cholinergic blockade leaving the effect largely intact.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention cholinergic blockade experiments or whether cholinergic blockade left the effect intact.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Lee2013`, using VIP-specific optogenetics in S1 slices, recorded VIP-evoked IPSC amplitudes that were several-fold larger in SST postsynaptic cells than in pyramidal cells (sample sizes are not reported in the cited row of the curated literature), supporting the SST bias qualitatively but with absolute amplitudes much larger than those reported by {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` — a discrepancy attributable to opsin dose, recording conditions, and pyramidal subtype.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report VIP-evoked IPSC amplitudes in SST vs pyramidal cells or slice recording details of that kind.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The original {cite:t}`Lee2013` data, with measurable but smaller VIP→pyramidal IPSCs, sit between these extremes.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report VIP→pyramidal IPSC amplitudes or their relative size.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: :::{admonition} Conflict :class: conflict **Magnitude of direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition relative to VIP→SST disinhibition.** {cite:t}`Lee2013` reported VIP-evoked IPSC amplitudes onto pyramidal cells in mouse S1 slice that, while smaller than those onto SST cells (sample sizes not reported in the cited row of the curated literature), were large enough to imply a non-trivial direct VIP→pyramidal channel alongside the dominant VIP→SST channel.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report VIP-evoked IPSC amplitudes onto pyramidal vs SST cells or quantify direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The variability is attributable to (i) cortical area and layer, (ii) opsin dose and behavioural state during the perturbation, (iii) co-recruitment of VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b,Lee2013,Pi2013`, and (iv) co-recruitment of NDNF/Layer-1 interneurons whose top-down drive partially overlaps with that of VIP cells {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal cells as a source of variability.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition.** A subset of VIP cells, most prominently the calretinin-positive, bipolar morphological type, contact pyramidal somata and proximal dendrites and produce direct inhibitory currents large enough to suppress pyramidal firing under volley activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition, calretinin-positive subtypes, or direct inhibitory currents onto pyramidal cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Panel A places the VIP→SST IPSC measurements of {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013`, {cite:t}`Lee2013`, and {cite:t}`Pi2013` on a single axis.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report VIP→SST IPSC amplitude measurements or quantitative synaptic physiology data.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Together, four independent studies place IS firing as a minority but reproducible class in cortical VIP populations, with continuous-adapting as the modal phenotype {cite:p}`Cauli1997,Cauli2000,Pronneke2015,Vucurovic2010,Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report VIP firing-type classifications (adapting, bursting, IS fractions).

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Connection probabilities from local L2/3 pyramidal cells onto VIP cells are comparable to (and in some preparations slightly lower than) those onto SST and PV interneurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a`, and unitary EPSC amplitudes are correspondingly small {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report Pyr→VIP connection probabilities or EPSC amplitudes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: What distinguishes the local pyramidal→VIP synapse is its short-term plasticity profile: VIP cells in mouse barrel and visual cortex receive predominantly *depressing* excitation from local pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014`, in contrast to the strongly facilitating Pyr→SST synapses described in earlier classical work {cite:p}`Pronneke2015, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss pyramidal→VIP synaptic plasticity or depression profiles.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Paired recordings and SST-Cre optogenetics consistently show that SST interneurons provide the largest single source of local IPSCs onto VIP cells, with connection probabilities that meet or exceed the reciprocal VIP→SST direction in the same preparations {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Karnani2016a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses PV→MC and VIP→MC inhibition, not SST→VIP inhibition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: PV cells contribute a smaller but non-negligible fraction of VIP-targeted inhibition, again compatible across visual, somatosensory and prefrontal slices {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Walker2016, Pronneke2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses PV→MC inhibition, not PV→VIP inhibition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Mutual inhibition between VIP cells themselves is sparse but reliably detected at the single-cell level {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Karnani2016a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention VIP↔VIP mutual inhibition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The motif is not confined to mouse: pharmacological and optogenetic dissection in rat {cite:p}`Walker2016, Pronneke2019, Pronneke2015` and comparative meta-analytic syntheses across V1, A1 and S1 {cite:p}`Guetmccreight2020b, Pronneke2019, Karnani2016a` reach the same architectural conclusion.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is mouse S1 only, not rat, and not a meta-analytic synthesis across V1/A1/S1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The dominant cortical schematic — VIP cells inhibit other interneurons, principally SST — is supported in superficial layers but does not survive a deeper-layer or in vivo cell-attached test {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract focuses on PV/VIP→MC inhibition, not VIP outputs to non-SST targets across layers.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Independent reports of meaningful VIP→PV connection rates in barrel and frontal cortices {cite:p}`Walker2016, Anastasiades2021a, Pi2013` and of VIP synapses onto NDNF/L1 interneurons {cite:p}`Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Abs2018` further enlarge the output set.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP→MC (SST) inhibition, not VIP→PV connection rates in barrel cortex.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: VIP cells therefore inhibit pyramidal neurons less than they inhibit SST cells, but their direct pyramidal output is not zero, and the connection probability scales with cortical depth and source area in a way that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016, Pi2013, Schuman2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP→Pyr connectivity or connection probability scaling with cortical depth/area.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two-photon photostimulation by {cite:t}`Karnani2016b`, paired-recording with multi-target post-synaptic sampling by {cite:t}`Walker2016` and rabies-based output mapping by {cite:t}`Wall2016` all converge on the conclusion that individual VIP cells are not uniformly biased to a single target type — there is substantial cell-to-cell variability in the relative weight of VIP→SST, VIP→PV and VIP→Pyr outputs, and the population mean is dominated by a subset of strongly-connected cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Wall2016, Pronneke2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes paired recordings of VIP/PV→MC, not cell-to-cell variability in VIP output weights across target types.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two-photon photostimulation by {cite:t}`Karnani2016b`, paired-recording with multi-target post-synaptic sampling by {cite:t}`Walker2016` and rabies-based output mapping by {cite:t}`Wall2016` all converge on the conclusion that individual VIP cells are not uniformly biased to a single target type — there is substantial cell-to-cell variability in the relative weight of VIP→SST, VIP→PV and VIP→Pyr outputs, and the population mean is dominated by a subset of strongly-connected cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Wall2016, Pronneke2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; abstract does not address single-cell heterogeneity in VIP output targeting.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Cauli2014` similarly described that CR/VIP bipolar interneurons receive predominantly depressing excitatory input from local pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014, Walker2016, Pronneke2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP/PV inhibitory input onto MC, not excitatory input from pyramidal cells onto VIP/CR bipolar cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The convergence of three independent methodological lines — paired recording, optogenetic dissection and EM ultrastructure — on the same compartment-specific output rule strengthens the case that the canonical schematic is correct *as far as it goes*, while leaving open the layer- and area-specific exceptions documented in earlier subsections {cite:p}`Hajos1996, Defelipe1999, Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes optogenetics and paired recordings but not EM ultrastructure or compartment-specific output rules.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **(iv) Direct VIP→pyramid connectivity.** The schematic claim that VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons is contradicted by Yu 2019, Zhou 2017 and several intersectional in vivo dissections {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract focuses on VIP/PV→MC inhibition, not direct VIP→Pyr connectivity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The resolution is layer- and area-specific: in V1 L2/3 the canonical schematic remains a defensible first approximation, but in deep layers and in mPFC the direct VIP→Pyr path is part of the wiring and ignoring it produces predictions that fail in vivo {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016, Pfeffer2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not address direct VIP→Pyr path or layer-specific resolution of that connectivity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The Pfeffer 2013 paired-recording survey reported negligible *functional* VIP→PV connection probability in V1 L2/3 {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013`, whereas anatomical / synaptic-counting work — including the Pi 2013 framing of VIP cells as canonical disinhibitors of multiple interneuron classes — implies that VIP+ axons do form symmetric synapses onto PV soma and proximal dendrites at non-trivial incidence {cite:p}`Pi2013, Walker2016, Karnani2016a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parvalbumin- and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing neocortical interneurons impose differential inhibition on Martinotti cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP→PV connection probability or anatomical vs functional discrepancy.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cells deliberately encode categories, not magnitudes. **(B)** Qualitative VIP output matrix across cortex L2/3, L5/6 and CA1; "n.t." = not tested in the cited preparations. **(C)** The single Phase-6-audited quantitative comparison: VIP→SST IPSQ = 4.6 ± 1.5 pC versus VIP→Pyr IPSQ = 0.6 ± 0.2 pC (and INC 0.42 ± 0.14 pC vs 0.06 ± 0.02 pC, scaled ×10 for visibility) from V1 L2/3 paired recordings (n = 11) {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013`. **(D)** Schematic of cholinergic recruitment of VIP / 5-HT3AR cells: nicotinic (slow, β2-containing) excitation and a smaller muscarinic inhibitory effect onto a subset of VIP cells, after {cite:t}`Porter1999`, {cite:t}`Arroyo2012`, {cite:t}`Fu2014`, {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` and {cite:t}`Granger2020`. **Caveat — qualitative.** All cells in panels A and B are categorical consensus; only panel C carries an audited numeric comparison. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not discuss cholinergic recruitment schematics, muscarinic inhibition subsets, or the specific quantitative VIP→SST vs VIP→Pyr comparison attributed here.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cortical VIP and CCK interneurons are selectively depolarised by nicotinic agonists, whereas pyramidal cells, fast-spiking PV cells and SST cells are not — a result first established by {cite:t}`Porter1999` and {cite:t}`Kawaguchi1997b` and replicated across rodent neocortex with subtype-specific receptor profiling {cite:p}`Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions nicotinic inputs to VIP cells but does not detail selective depolarisation of VIP vs non-response of PV/SST/Pyr, nor subtype-specific receptor profiling.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The receptor mediating this excitation is predominantly a non-α7, β2-containing nAChR with a slow desensitisation profile that can sustain VIP firing for many seconds after a brief cholinergic transient {cite:p}`Arroyo2012, Porter1999, Fu2014, Letzkus2011`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract says 'largely through nicotinic inputs from basal forebrain' but does not specify non-α7, β2-containing nAChR subtype or slow desensitisation profile.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, the same conjunction recurs: arousal- or reinforcer-related signals from basal forebrain or LC raise VIP activity, the rise is sufficient to suppress local SST output, and the SST suppression in turn opens a dendritic disinhibitory window onto pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Fu2014, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pi2013, Pakan2016, Sabri2024, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Kamigaki2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract covers V1 only; does not address A1, mPFC, BLA, CA1, LC, or the full multi-region conjunction claimed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Across visual, auditory, somatosensory, prefrontal, and motor cortex, optogenetic and chemogenetic manipulations of VIP cells have produced phenomena consistent with this motif {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Zhang2014,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Fu2014 only studied V1; claim spans auditory, somatosensory, prefrontal, motor cortex areas not addressed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The downstream consequence is that the canonical motif is engaged most strongly when external salience and internal arousal coincide, and only weakly engaged by either alone. This reconciles the apparent disagreement between {cite:t}`Pi2013` and {cite:t}`Fu2014` over which input is ‘primary’: in their respective preparations and behavioural epochs, each was the dominant driver, but neither study tested the alternate input under the other's task structure.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss whether nicotinic input alone is sufficient or requires co-activation with glutamatergic input.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In V1, locomotion drives VIP cells to depolarise by tens of percent above baseline ΔF/F at running onset (Fu 2014; sample size not reported in the cited row) and to suppress concurrently imaged SST cells {cite:p}`Fu2014,Pakan2016,Garciadelmolino2017,Dipoppa2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms VIP activation by locomotion and V1 response enhancement but doesn't report SST suppression or ΔF/F magnitudes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Locomotion-coupled VIP activity in V1 is reproduced across studies {cite:p}`Fu2014,Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018`, but its downstream consequence for SST cells is not. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms locomotion-coupled VIP activity but doesn't discuss inconsistency of SST downstream consequences.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: VIP cells fire fast, narrow APs (half-width ≈ 0.95 ms in juvenile S1 {cite:p}`Vucurovic2010`; AP amplitude 65.1 ± 9.1 mV in barrel cortex {cite:p}`Pronneke2015`) — narrower than pyramidal neurons but broader than PV fast-spiking cells, again consistent with the broader cortical interneuron taxonomy {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Karnani2016a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions layer-specific membrane properties but does not report specific AP amplitude (65.1 ± 9.1 mV) or half-width values.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Sag/I_{h} is modest rather than absent: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` measured a sag index of 11.2 ± 6.8% across cortical VIP cells; {cite:t}`Vucurovic2010` reported 12.1 ± 7.8% in juvenile S1; CA1 IS3 cells show pronounced sag in some recordings and minimal sag in others {cite:p}`Bell2015,Michaud2024`; and inferior-colliculus VIP-Cre stellate cells (which are *glutamatergic*, not GABAergic) show essentially no sag {cite:p}`Goyer2019` — flagging that VIP-Cre lines tag biophysically distinct cell classes outside the cortex.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention sag index or specific numeric values for sag/Ih measurements.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Across these comparisons, the rule that survives is structural rather than numeric: the VIP/CGE subclass occupies a high-R_{in}, low-rheobase, narrow-AP corner of the cortical-interneuron biophysical manifold, with within-class scatter that is real but bounded {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Pronneke2015,Vucurovic2010,Wierenga2010,Karnani2016a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions layer-specific differences in membrane properties but not specific biophysical manifold positioning (high-Rin, low-rheobase, narrow-AP).

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` reproduced this taxonomy quantitatively in mouse barrel cortex (75.4% continuous-adapting, 14.5% IS, 5.8% bursting, 4.3% high-threshold-bursting non-adapting across layers) and added a laminar twist: bursting VIP+ neurons are confined to L2/3 (11.8%) while L4–L6 lack bursting cells but retain IS (14.7%) and high-threshold-bursting non-adapting (2.9%) variants alongside a dominant continuous-adapting population (82.4%) {cite:p}`Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions layer-specific differences in membrane properties but does not report firing-class percentages or laminar distribution of firing types.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` reproduced this taxonomy quantitatively in mouse barrel cortex (75.4% continuous-adapting, 14.5% IS, 5.8% bursting, 4.3% high-threshold-bursting non-adapting across layers) and added a laminar twist: bursting VIP+ neurons are confined to L2/3 (11.8%) while L4–L6 lack bursting cells but retain IS (14.7%) and high-threshold-bursting non-adapting (2.9%) variants alongside a dominant continuous-adapting population (82.4%) {cite:p}`Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; abstract lacks specific firing-class taxonomy percentages or laminar breakdown of IS/bursting subtypes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Together, four independent studies place IS firing as a minority but reproducible class in cortical VIP populations, with continuous-adapting as the modal phenotype {cite:p}`Cauli1997,Cauli2000,Pronneke2015,Vucurovic2010,Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report IS firing fraction or continuous-adapting as modal phenotype.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` found 14.5% IS firing across the whole-population mouse barrel cortex VIP-Cre/tdTomato sample {cite:p}`Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report 14.5% IS firing fraction.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` found 14.5% IS firing across the whole-population mouse barrel cortex VIP-Cre/tdTomato sample {cite:p}`Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; abstract lacks IS firing percentage data.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **VIP intrinsic electrophysiology: cross-study landscape and Patch-seq mapping.** *Panel A:* Schematic exemplar firing traces for the three dominant Petilla classes observed in cortical VIP cells (irregular-spiking, continuous-adapting, accommodating) at a matched supra-threshold current step, drawn from the qualitative descriptions in {cite:p}`Cauli1997,Cauli2000,Pronneke2015,Vucurovic2010`. *Panel B:* Forest-plot of input resistance for VIP+ interneurons across non-cortical regions (CA1 stratum radiatum vs basolateral amygdala).
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions membrane properties but not specific Petilla firing classes or qualitative firing-trace descriptions.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Per-entry denominators are annotated on the bars per Phase-6 audit requirement; entries: {cite:p}`Cauli1997` (15 IS cells / 97 nonpyramidal interneurons sampled = 15.5% of nonpyramidal pool; the 15 IS cells co-expressed CR+VIP, rat S1, single-cell RT-PCR + patch); {cite:p}`Cauli2000` (16/60 = 26.7% IS-VIP cluster, rat frontal cortex L2–3, fusiform interneurons clustered by RT-PCR + patch); {cite:p}`Pronneke2015` (14.5% of whole-population VIP-Cre/tdTomato barrel cortex); {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a` (5/17 = 29.4% of mPFC L1b VIP-Cre × Ai14+ cells, mouse). *Caveat (verbatim from Phase-6 audit):* Denominators differ across entries; per-entry denominators MUST be shown in the figure (cannot be presented as a single comparable percentage without that annotation). | Rat (Cauli) vs mouse (Anastasiades) species mismatch. | Different cortical areas (S1, frontal cortex, mPFC L1b) and slightly different firing-class taxonomies. *Panel D:* Single-row entry showing membrane time constant τ_{m} (ms) for the only entry retained after Phase-6 audit removed {cite:t}`Rhomberg2018` from the τ_{m} sub-comparison ({cite:p}`Michaud2024` reports τ_{m} = 36.5 ± 3.2 ms, n=7 VIP/CR+ I-S3 cells, mouse CA1 stratum radiatum); panel reduced to a single-row entry per the iter3 audit.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report 14.5% IS firing fraction for whole-population VIP-Cre/tdTomato barrel cortex.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Candidate ion-channel substrates of irregular-spiking firing in VIP cells.** *Panel A:* Cartoon VIP-cell membrane with annotated channel complement (Na_{V}, Kv1, Kv4, HCN/I_{h}, KCNQ/M, SK) and qualitative voltage-clamp traces; channel inventory is consistent with the cortical VIP intrinsic-property literature {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Pronneke2015,Vucurovic2010`. *Panel B:* Two competing mechanism schematics for irregular-spiking — *(i)* Kv1-dominant repolarisation kinetics (block by 4-AP / dendrotoxin converts to sustained firing) {cite:p}`Porter1998`; *(ii)* KCNQ-mediated M-current (block by linopirdine selectively converts IS to tonic in IS VIP cells) {cite:p}`Goff2019,Guetmccreight2020b` — with predicted current-clamp signatures. *Panel C:* Decision flow mapping intrinsic-feature combinations (high R_{in} + IS + low sag → cluster A; high R_{in} + adapting + moderate sag → cluster B) onto Patch-seq Vip-MET types {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a` and *Vip* t-type axes {cite:p}`Scala2021`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions membrane properties but not specific ion channel complement or intrinsic-property inventory.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Connection probabilities from local L2/3 pyramidal cells onto VIP cells are comparable to (and in some preparations slightly lower than) those onto SST and PV interneurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a`, and unitary EPSC amplitudes are correspondingly small {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss connection probabilities from pyramidal cells onto VIP cells or comparisons with SST/PV.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: What distinguishes the local pyramidal→VIP synapse is its short-term plasticity profile: VIP cells in mouse barrel and visual cortex receive predominantly *depressing* excitation from local pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014`, in contrast to the strongly facilitating Pyr→SST synapses described in earlier classical work {cite:p}`Pronneke2015, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss short-term plasticity or depressing excitation at Pyr→VIP synapses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The motif is not confined to mouse: pharmacological and optogenetic dissection in rat {cite:p}`Walker2016, Pronneke2019, Pronneke2015` and comparative meta-analytic syntheses across V1, A1 and S1 {cite:p}`Guetmccreight2020b, Pronneke2019, Karnani2016a` reach the same architectural conclusion.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Paper studies barrel cortex VIP neurons in mice; no meta-analytic synthesis across V1, A1, S1 is described.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two-photon photostimulation by {cite:t}`Karnani2016b`, paired-recording with multi-target post-synaptic sampling by {cite:t}`Walker2016` and rabies-based output mapping by {cite:t}`Wall2016` all converge on the conclusion that individual VIP cells are not uniformly biased to a single target type — there is substantial cell-to-cell variability in the relative weight of VIP→SST, VIP→PV and VIP→Pyr outputs, and the population mean is dominated by a subset of strongly-connected cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Wall2016, Pronneke2019`. This single-cell heterogeneity, which dovetails with the molecular subtype heterogeneity reviewed in {ref}`sec-development` {cite:p}`Tasic2018, Pronneke2019, Pronneke2015`, recasts the canonical motif as a *population-mean* property rather than a wiring rule that every VIP cell obeys.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Paper characterizes VIP neuron morphology/electrophysiology; no single-cell connectivity dissection or output mapping described.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The triple — source-dependent excitatory afferents (depressing locally, facilitating from long-range), plastic facilitating GABAergic outputs, and target-specific co-transmission — together gives the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr motif a distinctive temporal and learning signature that is not captured by treating it as a static schematic {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014, Mcfarlan2024a, Granger2020, Kaneko2024, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Paper characterizes VIP neuron properties, not synaptic dynamics, co-transmission, or temporal signatures of the VIP→SST→Pyr motif.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The picture from connectomics in primary visual cortex {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025` and from cross-species comparisons {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Vanderveer2021,Caulinorocha2022` is that VIP cells in different cortical areas and species have different output ratios, so there is no single answer to the question ‘how strong is VIP→pyramidal inhibition?’ in the way the canonical schematic implies.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Paper studies barrel cortex VIP neuron properties in mice; no cross-species or cross-area output ratio comparisons reported.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In primate and human cortex, comparative anatomy and Patch-seq data suggest that the VIP→SST bias is preserved but that the absolute density and laminar organisation of VIP cells differ substantially {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Vanderveer2021,Caulinorocha2022`, with implications discussed further in {ref}`sec-cross-areas`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Characterizing VIP Neurons in the Barrel Cortex of VIPcre/tdTomato Mice Reveals Layer-Specific Differences
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is mouse barrel cortex only; no primate/human data or Patch-seq comparative anatomy discussed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The widespread textbook claim that "VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons" is contested {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a` against a robust but quantitatively heterogeneous canonical motif {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a`, and represents the first major divergence between canonical schematic and contemporary data.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP disinhibiting E and FS neurons but doesn't explicitly contest the claim that VIP cells inhibit only interneurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The dominant cortical schematic — VIP cells inhibit other interneurons, principally SST — is supported in superficial layers but does not survive a deeper-layer or in vivo cell-attached test {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract says VIP neurons disinhibit E and FS neurons but doesn't explicitly test the canonical schematic across layers or via cell-attached methods.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Yu2019` performed in vivo VIP-Cre/ChR2 stimulation in barrel cortex and recorded from large populations of FS/PV and pyramidal cells across cortical layers; rather than a uniform PV-disinhibition signature, they found that a substantial subset of L5/6 FS-PV cells are *directly* inhibited by VIP activation, and that pyramidal cells in deep layers also receive direct VIP-evoked IPSCs.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't mention VIP-Cre/ChR2 stimulation, direct VIP→FS inhibition in L5/6, or VIP-evoked IPSCs onto pyramidal cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: These layer- and area-specific direct VIP→Pyr and VIP→PV connections are not at odds with {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` once the comparison is read carefully: the original measurements were made in V1 L2/3 paired recordings (where superficial pyramids receive only sparse VIP input) and the resolution-status entry in the conflicts table is layer-dependent, with L2/3 canonical disinhibition coexisting with L5/6 direct VIP→PV/Pyr connectivity {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss layer-dependent VIP→PV/Pyr connectivity or comparisons with Pfeffer 2013 V1 L2/3 data.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: VIP cells therefore inhibit pyramidal neurons less than they inhibit SST cells, but their direct pyramidal output is not zero, and the connection probability scales with cortical depth and source area in a way that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016, Pi2013, Schuman2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract states VIP disinhibits E and FS neurons but doesn't quantify VIP→Pyr output or discuss depth/area scaling.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The convergence of three independent methodological lines — paired recording, optogenetic dissection and EM ultrastructure — on the same compartment-specific output rule strengthens the case that the canonical schematic is correct *as far as it goes*, while leaving open the layer- and area-specific exceptions documented in earlier subsections {cite:p}`Hajos1996, Defelipe1999, Pfeffer2013, Yu2019, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss paired recording, EM ultrastructure, or compartment-specific output rules.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **(i) VIP→PV connectivity strength.** The classical Pfeffer 2013 measurement of negligible VIP→PV inhibition in V1 L2/3 is in tension with the Yu 2019 demonstration of direct VIP→FS inhibition in barrel cortex deep layers {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Yu2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP disinhibiting E and FS neurons but doesn't specifically demonstrate direct VIP→FS inhibition in deep layers.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The resolution from independent replications {cite:p}`Walker2016, Pi2013, Anastasiades2021a` is that VIP→PV connectivity is layer-dependent: superficial L2/3 microcircuits cleanly support the canonical interpretation, but L5/6 microcircuits — and especially in non-V1 cortices — recruit a direct VIP→PV path that the canonical schematic misses {cite:p}`Yu2019, Walker2016, Anastasiades2021a, Pi2013, Zhou2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss layer-dependent VIP→PV connectivity or compare with Pfeffer 2013 findings.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **(iv) Direct VIP→pyramid connectivity.** The schematic claim that VIP cells inhibit only other interneurons is contradicted by Yu 2019, Zhou 2017 and several intersectional in vivo dissections {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract says VIP disinhibits E and FS neurons but doesn't explicitly address direct VIP→pyramid connectivity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The resolution is layer- and area-specific: in V1 L2/3 the canonical schematic remains a defensible first approximation, but in deep layers and in mPFC the direct VIP→Pyr path is part of the wiring and ignoring it produces predictions that fail in vivo {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016, Pfeffer2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss layer-specific VIP→Pyr paths or predictions that fail in vivo without accounting for them.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Subsequent layer-resolved studies recover a measurable VIP→PV functional path in deeper layers and in non-V1 areas {cite:p}`Yu2019, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`, supporting the anatomical observation while preserving Pfeffer 2013 as a quantitatively correct L2/3-specific functional measurement. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't explicitly report layer-resolved VIP→PV functional connectivity or compare with Pfeffer 2013.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, the *minimum sufficient circuit model* needed to explain disease-relevant phenotypes (autism, schizophrenia, anxiety, neonatal injury) cannot rely on canonical disinhibition alone; it must accommodate the layer-dependent direct VIP→Pyr/PV path, the area-tuned long-range asymmetries and the modulator-recruited gain control reviewed here {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Letzkus2011, Sabri2024, Krabbe2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract discusses interneuron recruitment rules in barrel cortex but says nothing about disease models or minimum sufficient circuit models.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Third, the original measurements of VIP→pyramidal effects were almost all *net* effects — measured as changes in pyramidal firing or membrane potential after VIP-cell perturbation — and so confounded direct effects (VIP→Pyr inhibition), indirect effects (VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition), and parallel routes through PV cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract reports VIP disinhibit E and FS neurons but does not discuss confounding of direct vs indirect VIP→Pyr effects or net effect measurement issues.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013`, in mouse auditory cortex, reported that VIP cells inhibit both SST and PV cells with kinetically distinct IPSCs (decay τ ≈ 18 ms onto SST, ≈ 6 ms onto PV; Pi 2013, error metric ‘± as printed’, n not reported in the cited row). **Resolution:** the metrics differ — connection probability versus within-pair IPSC waveform — and both can hold simultaneously {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Yu2019 describes interneuron recruitment during tactile behavior but the abstract does not address VIP→SST vs VIP→PV connection probability, IPSC kinetics, or connectomic resolution of the specificity debate.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The contemporary reading {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Schneidermizell2025` is that the direct channel is detectable in most preparations but is dominated, on average, by the disinhibitory channel; net pyramidal modulation reflects the balance and can flip sign with task condition. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract reports VIP disinhibit E and FS neurons but doesn't discuss direct VIP→Pyr channel magnitude vs disinhibitory channel or sign-flipping.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The functional consequence is that VIP cells preferentially read *sustained or rhythmically-paced* inputs over transient ones — a property aligned with their behavioural recruitment by tonic state variables (locomotion, arousal, reinforcement) rather than by isolated transient events {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pakan2016, Sabri2024, Letzkus2011`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP activation by locomotion but says nothing about sustained vs transient input preferences or short-term plasticity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The reading converged on by recent chemogenetic work is that locomotion-related VIP recruitment is itself preserved across these conditions, but its *downstream consequence* on SST depends on the strength of visually driven SST input {cite:p}`Sabri2024, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Kaneko2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows context-dependence but does not discuss chemogenetic work or the specific mechanistic reading about visually driven SST input strength.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In higher visual areas, the locomotion-evoked VIP activation that is so reproducible in V1 is *not* uniformly disinhibitory: in mouse anterolateral and lateromedial areas, locomotion produces SST recruitment of comparable magnitude to VIP recruitment, complicating the simple disinhibition story {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Garrett2020,Dipoppa2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses V1 only, not higher visual areas like AL or LM. No data on anterolateral/lateromedial areas mentioned.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Panel C aggregates the *in vivo* effect of VIP perturbation on pyramidal firing across V1 studies and shows that the canonical net-disinhibitory effect is reproducible in V1 superficial layers under top-down or running-coupled drive, but is reduced or absent in higher visual areas and in stationary visual paradigms {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Garrett2020,Dipoppa2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss higher visual areas or VIP perturbation effects on pyramidal firing; only V1 layers 2/3 and 4 examined.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Mutual inhibition between VIP cells themselves is sparse but reliably detected at the single-cell level {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Karnani2016a, Walker2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss mutual inhibition between VIP cells themselves.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two-photon photostimulation by {cite:t}`Karnani2016b`, paired-recording with multi-target post-synaptic sampling by {cite:t}`Walker2016` and rabies-based output mapping by {cite:t}`Wall2016` all converge on the conclusion that individual VIP cells are not uniformly biased to a single target type — there is substantial cell-to-cell variability in the relative weight of VIP→SST, VIP→PV and VIP→Pyr outputs, and the population mean is dominated by a subset of strongly-connected cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Wall2016, Pronneke2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract focuses on VIP→SST disinhibition; does not discuss cell-to-cell variability in VIP→SST/PV/Pyr output weights or dominance by strongly-connected subset.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two-photon photostimulation by {cite:t}`Karnani2016b`, paired-recording with multi-target post-synaptic sampling by {cite:t}`Walker2016` and rabies-based output mapping by {cite:t}`Wall2016` all converge on the conclusion that individual VIP cells are not uniformly biased to a single target type — there is substantial cell-to-cell variability in the relative weight of VIP→SST, VIP→PV and VIP→Pyr outputs, and the population mean is dominated by a subset of strongly-connected cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Wall2016, Pronneke2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; abstract does not address cell-to-cell variability in output target type or strongly-connected subset dominance.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Karnani2016a` and {cite:t}`Karnani2016b` then showed that, when measured at higher resolution, VIP cells in V1 also generate measurable, behaviourally relevant inhibition onto pyramidal somata and dendrites, and that the magnitude of VIP→Pyr inhibition becomes large enough to mute pyramidal firing under volley activation.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract emphasizes VIP→SST disinhibition; does not report substantial VIP→Pyr direct inhibition or muting of pyramidal firing.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013`, in mouse auditory cortex, reported that VIP cells inhibit both SST and PV cells with kinetically distinct IPSCs (decay τ ≈ 18 ms onto SST, ≈ 6 ms onto PV; Pi 2013, error metric ‘± as printed’, n not reported in the cited row). **Resolution:** the metrics differ — connection probability versus within-pair IPSC waveform — and both can hold simultaneously {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss resolution of SST-bias vs PV-bias disagreements or IPSC kinetics onto different targets.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The variability is attributable to (i) cortical area and layer, (ii) opsin dose and behavioural state during the perturbation, (iii) co-recruitment of VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal cells {cite:p}`Karnani2016b,Lee2013,Pi2013`, and (iv) co-recruitment of NDNF/Layer-1 interneurons whose top-down drive partially overlaps with that of VIP cells {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP-mediated direct inhibition of pyramidal cells as a source of variability in effect size.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition.** A subset of VIP cells, most prominently the calretinin-positive, bipolar morphological type, contact pyramidal somata and proximal dendrites and produce direct inhibitory currents large enough to suppress pyramidal firing under volley activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention calretinin-positive VIP cells or direct VIP→pyramidal somatic inhibition suppressing firing under volley activation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Karnani2016b` demonstrated that VIP-cell volley activation can suppress PV-cell firing sufficiently to reduce perisomatic inhibition of pyramidal cells, providing a *second* disinhibitory channel that operates on a faster timescale than the dendritic VIP→SST channel.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP→PV connections, PV-cell suppression, or perisomatic inhibition reduction as a second disinhibitory channel.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In addition to these four families, a number of motif fragments are documented but less developed in the literature: VIP→Martinotti subtype-specific connections in deep layers {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`, VIP→long-range projecting interneuron contacts, and VIP-cell mediated inhibition of other VIP cells through CCK-positive subtypes {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Garrett2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP→SOM disinhibition in visual cortex but does not discuss Martinotti subtype-specific connections in deep layers specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In mouse barrel cortex, vibrissa motor cortex (vM1) projects more strongly onto VIP cells than onto neighbouring pyramidal neurons, with input ratios of roughly threefold {cite:p}`Lee2013, Williams2019, Audette2018`; analogous over-targeting of VIP by long-range cortical fibres has been most cleanly demonstrated for the cingulate→V1 projection {cite:p}`Zhang2014`, with related VIP-preferential top-down recruitment reported in mPFC and frontal-feedback contexts {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a, Wall2016`; orbital→sensory and other higher-order routes are not yet independently mapped at the same resolution and we treat them here as extrapolations from the cingulate→V1 result.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: The paper studies POm thalamic input, not vM1→VIP projections or a ~2.9× vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: First, the vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio of ~2.9× reported by {cite:t}`Lee2013` is independently reproduced in similar form for vS1→Cg, ACC→V1 and OFC→sensory pathways {cite:p}`Zhang2014, Bilash2023, Stachniak2023, Pi2013, Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Paper studies POm thalamic input, not vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio of ~2.9×. No data on that ratio in abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, the rabies-tracing asymmetry of {cite:t}`Wall2016` has been recapitulated across cortical areas with both pseudo-typed rabies and CTB injection assays, all converging on the same direction of effect even when absolute counts differ {cite:p}`Wall2016, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a, Ramamurthy2023, Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Paper does not discuss rabies-tracing asymmetry or CTB injection assays per the abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Long-range thalamic and cortico-cortical inputs onto VIP cells are likewise dominated by facilitation: anterior thalamic input onto VIP cells in mouse presubiculum is overtly facilitating across paired-pulse ratios {cite:p}`Nassar2025`, and POm input onto S1 VIP cells supports sustained activation rather than transient bursts {cite:p}`Williams2019, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss short-term plasticity, facilitation, or paired-pulse ratios of POm→VIP synapses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Paper supports parts of the synthesis but abstract doesn't address the full composite claim attributed here.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, the canonical recruitment story is a *composite*: top-down cortical glutamatergic drive {cite:p}`Lee2013,Zhang2014` and ascending cholinergic and noradrenergic neuromodulation {cite:p}`Fu2014,Hangya2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019` were originally described in different papers and different behavioural contexts but are now often spoken of as a single mechanism.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Paper describes thalamic (POm) glutamatergic drive, not top-down cortical drive as the claim attributes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pharmacological and optogenetic isolation of cholinergic transmission has since shown that VIP cells across V1, A1, S1, and PFC respond rapidly to ACh release through α4β2 and α7 nicotinic receptors {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019,Anastasiades2021a,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b`, with response amplitudes that can rival those produced by direct optogenetic stimulation of long-range glutamatergic axons.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss cholinergic transmission, nicotinic receptors, or ACh-driven VIP responses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The conflict largely dissolves once preparation and behavioural epoch are matched — top-down drive dominates during attentional or top-down sensory tasks, cholinergic drive during locomotion-coupled arousal — and joint manipulation experiments {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kamigaki2017,Williams2019` show the two routes are concurrently active and at least partly multiplicative {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss joint manipulation of cholinergic and top-down routes or multiplicative interactions.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` showed strong VIP→SST connectivity but emphasised the limited VIP→PV/Pyr coupling. **Resolution:** both descriptions are locally accurate, but the modal answer depends on cortical area and behavioural state — disinhibition dominates in V1 and S1 superficial layers under top-down drive {cite:p}`Zhang2014,Lee2013`; PV recruitment becomes important in cortices and tasks in which cholinergic drive is strong {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019` and in deep-layer connectomic reconstructions where direct VIP→pyramidal contacts are visible {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025`. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows VIP→SST disinhibition is critical for LTP but does not discuss limited VIP→PV/Pyr coupling specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In S1, top-down feedback in object detection and texture discrimination tasks recruits VIP cells whose suppression reduces task performance {cite:p}`Williams2019,Sermet2019,Bigelow2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes thalamocortical slice physiology showing VIP-mediated disinhibition gates LTP in S1, but does not mention object detection, texture discrimination tasks, or behavioral effects of VIP suppression.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In S1, the motif holds in object-localisation and active touch tasks {cite:p}`Williams2019,Sermet2019`, but layer-specific variants have been described in which thalamic inputs to deep-layer VIP cells engage a different downstream target set {cite:p}`Munoz2017,Sermet2019,Garciajuncoclemente2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Higher-Order Thalamocortical Inputs Gate Synaptic Long-Term Potentiation via Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Williams2019 studied thalamocortical LTP gating via VIP-mediated disinhibition in S1 slices, but the abstract doesn't mention object-localisation or active touch tasks. The motif claim in behavioral context isn't directly supported.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The reading converged on by recent chemogenetic work is that locomotion-related VIP recruitment is itself preserved across these conditions, but its *downstream consequence* on SST depends on the strength of visually driven SST input {cite:p}`Sabri2024, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Kaneko2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't specifically address whether VIP recruitment is preserved while SST consequence depends on visual input strength; it mentions modulating synaptic weights instead.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract covers V1 interneuron interactions and locomotion effects but doesn't address most input/output details claimed (cholinergic, serotonergic, thalamic, deep layer outputs).

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In higher visual areas, the locomotion-evoked VIP activation that is so reproducible in V1 is *not* uniformly disinhibitory: in mouse anterolateral and lateromedial areas, locomotion produces SST recruitment of comparable magnitude to VIP recruitment, complicating the simple disinhibition story {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Garrett2020,Dipoppa2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions V1 only; no explicit data on higher visual areas (AL, LM) or SST co-recruitment in those areas.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Panel C aggregates the *in vivo* effect of VIP perturbation on pyramidal firing across V1 studies and shows that the canonical net-disinhibitory effect is reproducible in V1 superficial layers under top-down or running-coupled drive, but is reduced or absent in higher visual areas and in stationary visual paradigms {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Garrett2020,Dipoppa2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss higher visual areas, stationary paradigms reducing disinhibition, or VIP perturbation effects on pyramidal cells across studies.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In V1, locomotion in darkness activates VIP cells and disinhibits pyramidal neurons through suppression of SST cells {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pakan2016, Sabri2024, Dipoppa2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses behavioral state modulation of cortical activity via VIP but does not specifically mention locomotion in darkness activating VIP and disinhibiting pyramidal neurons through SST suppression.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The reconciling reading is that the disinhibitory polarisation is context-dependent on visual drive: in darkness or during weak input the canonical sign holds, but bright structured stimuli decouple SST suppression from VIP activation {cite:p}`Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Kaneko2024, Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss visual drive conditions, darkness vs bright stimuli, or decoupling of SST suppression from VIP activation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, although Cg→V1 robustly recruits VIP {cite:p}`Zhang2014`, A1→V1 by contrast *suppresses* VIP cells {cite:p}`Ibrahim2016`, and direct optogenetic VIP perturbations do not always replicate the modulatory effects ascribed to attentional state in earlier work {cite:p}`Ibrahim2016, Sabri2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity
