insufficient_triples.note: Claim attributes 'denominator definitions' variation to this paper; abstract does not discuss denominator issues or cross-study methodological discrepancies.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In mouse barrel cortex, vibrissa motor cortex (vM1) projects more strongly onto VIP cells than onto neighbouring pyramidal neurons, with input ratios of roughly threefold {cite:p}`Lee2013, Williams2019, Audette2018`; analogous over-targeting of VIP by long-range cortical fibres has been most cleanly demonstrated for the cingulate→V1 projection {cite:p}`Zhang2014`, with related VIP-preferential top-down recruitment reported in mPFC and frontal-feedback contexts {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a, Wall2016`; orbital→sensory and other higher-order routes are not yet independently mapped at the same resolution and we treat them here as extrapolations from the cingulate→V1 result.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: POm Thalamocortical Input Drives Layer-Specific Microcircuits in Somatosensory Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Claim attributes vM1→VIP preferential targeting with ~3× ratio to this paper. Abstract discusses POm thalamic input, not vM1 cortical input or VIP cells specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Higher-order thalamic nuclei — POm in the somatosensory pathway and pulvinar/LP in the visual stream — innervate VIP cells robustly, often with response probabilities and amplitudes comparable to those onto pyramidal targets {cite:p}`Williams2019, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a, Ramamurthy2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: POm Thalamocortical Input Drives Layer-Specific Microcircuits in Somatosensory Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses POm input onto PV, 5HT3a-R, SST, and pyramidal neurons but does not mention VIP cells specifically or their response probabilities.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, the rabies-tracing asymmetry of {cite:t}`Wall2016` has been recapitulated across cortical areas with both pseudo-typed rabies and CTB injection assays, all converging on the same direction of effect even when absolute counts differ {cite:p}`Wall2016, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a, Ramamurthy2023, Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: POm Thalamocortical Input Drives Layer-Specific Microcircuits in Somatosensory Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Claim is about rabies-tracing asymmetry being recapitulated. Abstract does not discuss rabies tracing or CTB injection assays.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The afferent picture that emerges is therefore neither uniformly "VIP receives top-down" nor "VIP receives thalamic" — it is a structured asymmetry in which long-range and higher-order modulatory inputs preferentially read VIP cells over their interneuron neighbours, and primary feedforward inputs do not {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Williams2019, Cruikshank2012, Anastasiades2021a, Audette2018, Bastos2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: POm Thalamocortical Input Drives Layer-Specific Microcircuits in Somatosensory Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses POm (higher-order thalamic) input to specific cell types but doesn't explicitly contrast higher-order vs primary inputs onto VIP vs other interneurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Long-range thalamic and cortico-cortical inputs onto VIP cells are likewise dominated by facilitation: anterior thalamic input onto VIP cells in mouse presubiculum is overtly facilitating across paired-pulse ratios {cite:p}`Nassar2025`, and POm input onto S1 VIP cells supports sustained activation rather than transient bursts {cite:p}`Williams2019, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: POm Thalamocortical Input Drives Layer-Specific Microcircuits in Somatosensory Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes prolonged/weak activity in superficial layers and longer integration windows but doesn't explicitly discuss facilitation or paired-pulse ratios for VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: POm Thalamocortical Input Drives Layer-Specific Microcircuits in Somatosensory Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes layer-specific POm-driven microcircuits but doesn't address VIP-specific input/output or the broad synthesis claims attributed here.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Direct optogenetic VIP perturbations during cross-modal attention do not reliably replicate the modulation reported in earlier indirect manipulations {cite:p}`Ibrahim2016, Anastasiades2021a, Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cross-Modality Sharpening of Visual Cortical Processing through Layer-1-Mediated Inhibition and Disinhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss optogenetic VIP perturbations failing to replicate earlier indirect manipulation effects.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: First, in awake V1 *during* visual stimulation, locomotion increases activity of VIP, SST *and* PV cells in parallel rather than producing the canonical VIP-up/SST-down divergence — a finding originally reported by {cite:t}`Pakan2016` and converging with {cite:t}`Dipoppa2018` and {cite:t}`Kaneko2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses SSE and GABA vs VIP release, not locomotion-induced parallel activation of VIP/SST/PV during visual stimulation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The reconciling reading is that the disinhibitory polarisation is context-dependent on visual drive: in darkness or during weak input the canonical sign holds, but bright structured stimuli decouple SST suppression from VIP activation {cite:p}`Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Kaneko2024, Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss context-dependent decoupling of SST suppression from VIP activation under structured visual stimuli.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The honest synthesis — and one that the cluster_05 conflicts table forces us to make — is that "VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition" is a *available* circuit operation that different long-range inputs and brain states engage, suppress, or bypass; it is not the unique route by which top-down or arousal signals reach pyramidal output {cite:p}`Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Ibrahim2016, Anastasiades2021a, Kaneko2024, Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract focuses on GABA vs VIP peptide for SSE; does not discuss VIP→SST→Pyr as non-unique route for top-down signals.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Where the mapping breaks (Pakan, Dipoppa, Kaneko in V1 with structured visual drive; Ibrahim with cross-modal A1→V1; Anastasiades with cell-type specific direct VIP→Pyr in mPFC) it does so in *predictable* ways given the underlying afferent and output diagrams of {numref}`fig-vip-connectome` — by recruiting either non-canonical inputs to VIP or non-canonical outputs from VIP rather than abolishing the modulatory drive itself {cite:p}`Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Kaneko2024, Ibrahim2016, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss non-canonical inputs/outputs from VIP or predictable breakdowns of disinhibitory motif.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The triple — source-dependent excitatory afferents (depressing locally, facilitating from long-range), plastic facilitating GABAergic outputs, and target-specific co-transmission — together gives the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr motif a distinctive temporal and learning signature that is not captured by treating it as a static schematic {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014, Mcfarlan2024a, Granger2020, Kaneko2024, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows GABA vs peptide distinction but does not discuss temporal/learning signatures or source-dependent synaptic dynamics.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **(ii) Locomotion and the sign of SST modulation.** Fu 2014 reported the canonical locomotion-up-VIP / locomotion-down-SST pattern in V1 in darkness {cite:p}`Fu2014`; Pakan 2016 and Dipoppa 2018 showed that this sign reverses or vanishes in the presence of structured visual stimulation, with SST cells co-activated during locomotion when visual drive is strong {cite:p}`Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Kaneko2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions locomotion and high-gain state but not the sign reversal of SST modulation under structured visual stimulation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The reading converged on by recent chemogenetic work is that locomotion-related VIP recruitment is itself preserved across these conditions, but its *downstream consequence* on SST depends on the strength of visually driven SST input {cite:p}`Sabri2024, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Kaneko2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss how downstream SST consequences depend on visually driven SST input strength.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract covers only GABA vs VIP peptide for SSE; does not address the broad connectivity synthesis described here.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Across visual, auditory, somatosensory, prefrontal, and motor cortex, optogenetic and chemogenetic manipulations of VIP cells have produced phenomena consistent with this motif {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Zhang2014,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
insufficient_triples.note: The paper is specific to auditory cortex. Claim attributes findings across visual, somatosensory, prefrontal, and motor cortex to this cite.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pharmacological and optogenetic isolation of cholinergic transmission has since shown that VIP cells across V1, A1, S1, and PFC respond rapidly to ACh release through α4β2 and α7 nicotinic receptors {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019,Anastasiades2021a,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b`, with response amplitudes that can rival those produced by direct optogenetic stimulation of long-range glutamatergic axons.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions cholinergic axons depolarizing inhibitory cells but does not specify nicotinic receptor subtypes (α4β2, α7) or responses across V1, S1, PFC.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` showed strong VIP→SST connectivity but emphasised the limited VIP→PV/Pyr coupling. **Resolution:** both descriptions are locally accurate, but the modal answer depends on cortical area and behavioural state — disinhibition dominates in V1 and S1 superficial layers under top-down drive {cite:p}`Zhang2014,Lee2013`; PV recruitment becomes important in cortices and tasks in which cholinergic drive is strong {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019` and in deep-layer connectomic reconstructions where direct VIP→pyramidal contacts are visible {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025`. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not specifically discuss VIP→PV/Pyr coupling or limited connectivity. It describes parallel interneuron modulation but not connectivity details.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In some cortical areas, this faster channel may dominate: {cite:t}`Kuchibhotla2017` reported that the cholinergic-driven re-routing of inhibition in auditory cortex is mediated in part by VIP→PV and not solely by VIP→SST.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes parallel interneuron modulation but does not specifically mention VIP→PV connectivity or attribute re-routing partly to VIP→PV pathway.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: More recent EM-based connectomic reconstructions in mouse V1 confirm that the VIP→SST edge is over-represented relative to a null wiring model, but they also resolve substantial VIP→pyramidal and VIP→PV outputs that were not visible in the lower-throughput surveys {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP-like disinhibitory specialists targeting basket cells but doesn't explicitly quantify VIP→pyramidal or VIP→PV outputs vs null model.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The picture from connectomics in primary visual cortex {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025` and from cross-species comparisons {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Vanderveer2021,Caulinorocha2022` is that VIP cells in different cortical areas and species have different output ratios, so there is no single answer to the question ‘how strong is VIP→pyramidal inhibition?’ in the way the canonical schematic implies.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss cross-area or cross-species variation in VIP output ratios or whether VIP→pyramidal inhibition strength varies by area.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pfeffer2013` showed strong VIP→SST connectivity but emphasised the limited VIP→PV/Pyr coupling. **Resolution:** both descriptions are locally accurate, but the modal answer depends on cortical area and behavioural state — disinhibition dominates in V1 and S1 superficial layers under top-down drive {cite:p}`Zhang2014,Lee2013`; PV recruitment becomes important in cortices and tasks in which cholinergic drive is strong {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019` and in deep-layer connectomic reconstructions where direct VIP→pyramidal contacts are visible {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025`. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions disinhibitory specialists targeting basket cells but doesn't explicitly characterize limited VIP→PV/Pyr coupling or deep-layer VIP→Pyr contacts.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The contemporary reading {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Schneidermizell2025` is that the direct channel is detectable in most preparations but is dominated, on average, by the disinhibitory channel; net pyramidal modulation reflects the balance and can flip sign with task condition. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss the balance between direct VIP→Pyr and disinhibitory channels or task-condition-dependent sign flipping.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In PFC, the motif is engaged in attention and working-memory tasks {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Pinto2015,Anastasiades2021a`, but PFC VIP cells receive a richer mix of long-range inputs and project to a broader set of postsynaptic targets, including direct VIP→pyramidal connections that may be more prominent than in sensory cortex {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract describes inhibitory connectivity in mouse visual cortex, including disinhibitory specialists targeting basket cells, but does not mention direct VIP→pyramidal connections or comparisons between PFC and sensory cortex connectivity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition.** A subset of VIP cells, most prominently the calretinin-positive, bipolar morphological type, contact pyramidal somata and proximal dendrites and produce direct inhibitory currents large enough to suppress pyramidal firing under volley activation {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions disinhibitory specialists but doesn't specifically describe calretinin-positive VIP cells contacting pyramidal somata with suppressive currents.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In addition to these four families, a number of motif fragments are documented but less developed in the literature: VIP→Martinotti subtype-specific connections in deep layers {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`, VIP→long-range projecting interneuron contacts, and VIP-cell mediated inhibition of other VIP cells through CCK-positive subtypes {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Garrett2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions inhibitory targeting specificity but doesn't specifically discuss VIP→Martinotti deep-layer connections or VIP→VIP via CCK subtypes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **The disinhibition motif and its alternatives.** **(A)** Canonical motif: VIP cells, recruited by long-range glutamatergic and ascending cholinergic inputs, preferentially inhibit SST cells, releasing pyramidal dendrites from inhibition; minor VIP→PV and VIP→pyramidal edges are drawn explicitly to flag that the canonical schematic is a tendency rather than an exclusive wiring rule {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a`. **(B)** Three alternative motifs documented in the same evidence base: direct VIP→PV control of perisomatic inhibition {cite:p}`Pi2013,Karnani2016b,Kuchibhotla2017`, direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition by bipolar/CR-positive VIP cells {cite:p}`Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`, and a layer-1 VIP→NDNF disinhibitory motif gating apical dendritic input {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`; the hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket motif is a homologue with a distinct oscillatory context {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020`. **(C)** Decision tree mapping behavioural context — locomotion, attentional top-down, reinforcement, fear/learning — to the motif most consistent with the empirical recruitment data and to the supporting cite-keys; the assignment is qualitative because the literature does not yet contain joint manipulations of multiple motifs under matched conditions {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Krabbe2019,Veit2023`. **(D)** Schematic legend distinguishing measured edges (solid) from inferred or context-dependent edges (dashed). *Caveat:* this figure is a schematic with no quantitative data substrate; assignment of behavioural context to dominant motif in Panel C is inferred from joint reading of the cited recruitment studies and is not the output of a meta-analytic weighting. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory specificity from a connectomic census of mouse visual cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract identifies disinhibitory specialists but doesn't address the specific multi-motif framework or decision-tree mapping described in this figure legend.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Sag/I_{h} is modest rather than absent: {cite:t}`Pronneke2015` measured a sag index of 11.2 ± 6.8% across cortical VIP cells; {cite:t}`Vucurovic2010` reported 12.1 ± 7.8% in juvenile S1; CA1 IS3 cells show pronounced sag in some recordings and minimal sag in others {cite:p}`Bell2015,Michaud2024`; and inferior-colliculus VIP-Cre stellate cells (which are *glutamatergic*, not GABAergic) show essentially no sag {cite:p}`Goyer2019` — flagging that VIP-Cre lines tag biophysically distinct cell classes outside the cortex.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Scala2021` profiled mouse primary motor cortex by Patch-seq and reported that intrinsic-electrophysiological properties of *Vip* transcriptomic types vary continuously rather than discretely along the transcriptomic axis — for instance, τ_{m} peaks at the Sncg-adjacent end of the *Vip* manifold and decreases toward Vip-Gpc3 — supporting a graded mapping rather than crisp e-type boundaries within the subclass {cite:p}`Scala2021`.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Scala2021` profiled mouse primary motor cortex by Patch-seq and reported that intrinsic-electrophysiological properties of *Vip* transcriptomic types vary continuously rather than discretely along the transcriptomic axis — for instance, τ_{m} peaks at the Sncg-adjacent end of the *Vip* manifold and decreases toward Vip-Gpc3 — supporting a graded mapping rather than crisp e-type boundaries within the subclass {cite:p}`Scala2021`.

insufficient_triples.paper_title: Phenotypic variation of transcriptomic cell types in mouse motor cortex

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Candidate ion-channel substrates of irregular-spiking firing in VIP cells.** *Panel A:* Cartoon VIP-cell membrane with annotated channel complement (Na_{V}, Kv1, Kv4, HCN/I_{h}, KCNQ/M, SK) and qualitative voltage-clamp traces; channel inventory is consistent with the cortical VIP intrinsic-property literature {cite:p}`Tremblay2016,Pronneke2015,Vucurovic2010`. *Panel B:* Two competing mechanism schematics for irregular-spiking — *(i)* Kv1-dominant repolarisation kinetics (block by 4-AP / dendrotoxin converts to sustained firing) {cite:p}`Porter1998`; *(ii)* KCNQ-mediated M-current (block by linopirdine selectively converts IS to tonic in IS VIP cells) {cite:p}`Goff2019,Guetmccreight2020b` — with predicted current-clamp signatures. *Panel C:* Decision flow mapping intrinsic-feature combinations (high R_{in} + IS + low sag → cluster A; high R_{in} + adapting + moderate sag → cluster B) onto Patch-seq Vip-MET types {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a` and *Vip* t-type axes {cite:p}`Scala2021`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Phenotypic variation of transcriptomic cell types in mouse motor cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss specific ion channels, Kv1, KCNQ, or channel inventory for VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: What distinguishes the local pyramidal→VIP synapse is its short-term plasticity profile: VIP cells in mouse barrel and visual cortex receive predominantly *depressing* excitation from local pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014`, in contrast to the strongly facilitating Pyr→SST synapses described in earlier classical work {cite:p}`Pronneke2015, Walker2016`.

insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss short-term plasticity profiles (depressing vs facilitating) of pyramidal→VIP synapses.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The triple — source-dependent excitatory afferents (depressing locally, facilitating from long-range), plastic facilitating GABAergic outputs, and target-specific co-transmission — together gives the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr motif a distinctive temporal and learning signature that is not captured by treating it as a static schematic {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014, Mcfarlan2024a, Granger2020, Kaneko2024, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Revisiting enigmatic cortical calretinin-expressing interneurons

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **The disinhibition motif and its alternatives.** **(A)** Canonical motif: VIP cells, recruited by long-range glutamatergic and ascending cholinergic inputs, preferentially inhibit SST cells, releasing pyramidal dendrites from inhibition; minor VIP→PV and VIP→pyramidal edges are drawn explicitly to flag that the canonical schematic is a tendency rather than an exclusive wiring rule {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a`. **(B)** Three alternative motifs documented in the same evidence base: direct VIP→PV control of perisomatic inhibition {cite:p}`Pi2013,Karnani2016b,Kuchibhotla2017`, direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition by bipolar/CR-positive VIP cells {cite:p}`Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`, and a layer-1 VIP→NDNF disinhibitory motif gating apical dendritic input {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`; the hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket motif is a homologue with a distinct oscillatory context {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020`. **(C)** Decision tree mapping behavioural context — locomotion, attentional top-down, reinforcement, fear/learning — to the motif most consistent with the empirical recruitment data and to the supporting cite-keys; the assignment is qualitative because the literature does not yet contain joint manipulations of multiple motifs under matched conditions {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Krabbe2019,Veit2023`. **(D)** Schematic legend distinguishing measured edges (solid) from inferred or context-dependent edges (dashed). *Caveat:* this figure is a schematic with no quantitative data substrate; assignment of behavioural context to dominant motif in Panel C is inferred from joint reading of the cited recruitment studies and is not the output of a meta-analytic weighting. :::

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cells deliberately encode categories, not magnitudes. **(B)** Qualitative VIP output matrix across cortex L2/3, L5/6 and CA1; "n.t." = not tested in the cited preparations. **(C)** The single Phase-6-audited quantitative comparison: VIP→SST IPSQ = 4.6 ± 1.5 pC versus VIP→Pyr IPSQ = 0.6 ± 0.2 pC (and INC 0.42 ± 0.14 pC vs 0.06 ± 0.02 pC, scaled ×10 for visibility) from V1 L2/3 paired recordings (n = 11) {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013`. **(D)** Schematic of cholinergic recruitment of VIP / 5-HT3AR cells: nicotinic (slow, β2-containing) excitation and a smaller muscarinic inhibitory effect onto a subset of VIP cells, after {cite:t}`Porter1999`, {cite:t}`Arroyo2012`, {cite:t}`Fu2014`, {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` and {cite:t}`Granger2020`. **Caveat — qualitative.** All cells in panels A and B are categorical consensus; only panel C carries an audited numeric comparison. :::

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The triple — source-dependent excitatory afferents (depressing locally, facilitating from long-range), plastic facilitating GABAergic outputs, and target-specific co-transmission — together gives the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr motif a distinctive temporal and learning signature that is not captured by treating it as a static schematic {cite:p}`Karnani2016a, Cauli2014, Mcfarlan2024a, Granger2020, Kaneko2024, Walker2016, Pronneke2015`.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cluster_13 of the cholinergic literature contains an explicit opposite-sign disagreement: {cite:t}`Arroyo2012` and convergent work establish that the dominant ACh effect on cortical VIP / 5-HT3AR cells is nicotinic excitation through slow, β2-containing nAChRs {cite:p}`Arroyo2012, Porter1999, Fu2014`, while {cite:t}`Poorthuis2018` and matched studies emphasise a muscarinic *inhibition* of a VIP subset {cite:p}`Poorthuis2018, Kawaguchi1997b, Obermayer2019, Granger2020`.

insufficient_triples.note: The abstract does not discuss nicotinic vs muscarinic pharmacology or β2-containing nAChR excitation on VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A useful operational summary is the following. *Treat the VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibitory motif as a layer- and area-specific computation that a cortical microcircuit can perform when modulator-targeted afferents are active, not as a fixed wiring rule that all VIP cells obey under all conditions*. This formulation accommodates the canonical landmark results {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Fu2014, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b`, the long-range and thalamic asymmetries {cite:p}`Wall2016, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Cruikshank2012`, the modulator pharmacology {cite:p}`Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Arroyo2012, Vonengelhardt2007, Granger2020, Obermayer2019, Letzkus2011, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017`, the layer- and area-specific exceptions to canonical disinhibition {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`, the developmental and comparative qualifications {cite:p}`Favuzzi2019, Batistabrito2017, Qiu2020, Simacek2025a, Vormsteinschneider2020, Defelipe1999`, and the hippocampal divergence {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018, Bilash2023, Tamboli2024, Toth1993` within a single coherent picture.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical ChAT+ neurons co-transmit acetylcholine and GABA in a target- and brain-region-specific manner
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract supports region-specific ChAT+ neuron heterogeneity but doesn't address the broader operational summary framework cited.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Top-down feedback from cingulate cortex drives a centre-surround disinhibitory architecture in V1 via Cg→VIP→SST {cite:p}`Zhang2014, Stachniak2023, Bastos2023a`, and in mPFC, mediodorsal-thalamic recruitment of VIP underlies disinhibitory control of pyramidal output during goal-directed behaviour {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP responses to action outcomes in mPFC but does not mention mediodorsal-thalamic recruitment of VIP or disinhibitory control mechanism.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows VIP cells respond to outcomes in mPFC but says nothing about synaptic connectivity, input sources, or output targets described in the claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pharmacological and optogenetic isolation of cholinergic transmission has since shown that VIP cells across V1, A1, S1, and PFC respond rapidly to ACh release through α4β2 and α7 nicotinic receptors {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019,Anastasiades2021a,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b`, with response amplitudes that can rival those produced by direct optogenetic stimulation of long-range glutamatergic axons.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention cholinergic transmission, nicotinic receptors, or pharmacological/optogenetic isolation of ACh pathways.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pinto2015` recorded VIP and SST cells during a frontal-cortex attention task and found that VIP recruitment depended on both top-down glutamatergic drive and concurrent cholinergic tone, with each being individually insufficient.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes Ca2+ imaging of cell types in mPFC but does not mention cholinergic tone or glutamatergic drive dependence for VIP recruitment.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The conflict largely dissolves once preparation and behavioural epoch are matched — top-down drive dominates during attentional or top-down sensory tasks, cholinergic drive during locomotion-coupled arousal — and joint manipulation experiments {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kamigaki2017,Williams2019` show the two routes are concurrently active and at least partly multiplicative {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss joint manipulation of top-down and cholinergic routes or multiplicative interactions.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The direction of this effect is consistent across {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a,Pinto2015,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report VIP-mediated disinhibition of pyramidal cells or effects on pyramidal firing rates via VIP→SST suppression.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In PFC, the motif is engaged in attention and working-memory tasks {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Pinto2015,Anastasiades2021a`, but PFC VIP cells receive a richer mix of long-range inputs and project to a broader set of postsynaptic targets, including direct VIP→pyramidal connections that may be more prominent than in sensory cortex {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows cell-type activity in mPFC during goal-directed behavior but does not discuss VIP input sources, VIP→pyramidal connections, or working memory.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Detailed paired-recording, electron-microscopic and intersectional optogenetic dissection of the IS3 class shows that — unlike its cortical VIP counterpart — its principal output is not the SST cell but the *oriens-lacunosum moleculare* (OLM) interneuron, with strong, facilitating IPSCs that suppress OLM firing during sharp-wave ripples and during theta-rhythmic dendritic input from entorhinal cortex {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Francavilla2018a, Tamboli2024, Turi2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mouse hippocampal CA1 VIP interneurons detect novelty in the environment and support recognition memory
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP-IN disinhibition of PNs via SST/PV suppression but does not detail IS3→OLM synapses, paired recordings, EM, or SWR mechanisms.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The hippocampal evidence therefore argues for an *area-specific connectivity logic* in which the molecular identity is conserved but the wiring is repurposed: VIP cells are deployed wherever a cortical or hippocampal microcircuit needs a modulator-targeted, IN-targeting disinhibitor, but the *which-IN* and *which-pyramidal-population* are tuned to local computational requirements {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018, Bilash2023, Tamboli2024, Toth1993`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mouse hippocampal CA1 VIP interneurons detect novelty in the environment and support recognition memory
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss area-specific connectivity logic or molecular identity conservation with repurposed wiring across regions.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Hippocampal VIP→OLM and VIP→basket-cell disinhibition.** In CA1 and dentate gyrus, VIP cells, including the calretinin-positive and CCK-positive subsets, form a structurally distinct motif: VIP/CR cells inhibit OLM cells (the SST analogue) and basket cells (the PV analogue) in a temporally precise, theta-rhythmic manner that gates Schaffer-collateral and entorhinal input streams {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020,Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mouse hippocampal CA1 VIP interneurons detect novelty in the environment and support recognition memory
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP→SST/PV disinhibition but not VIP/CR→OLM specifics, theta-rhythmic gating, or dentate gyrus involvement.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The afferent picture mirrors the output one: lateral entorhinal cortex provides the dominant glutamatergic drive onto VIP/IS3 cells in stratum lacunosum-moleculare {cite:p}`Bilash2023, Turi2019, Tyan2014, Booker2018`, and arousal- or novelty-related signals robustly increase IS3 activity during free spatial exploration {cite:p}`Tamboli2024, Francavilla2018a, Turi2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphological diversity and connectivity of hippocampal interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses interneuron morphology/connectivity broadly but doesn't specifically mention entorhinal input to VIP/IS3 cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The CCK+/VIP+ basket subset, by contrast, deploys a perisomatic inhibition reminiscent of cortical PV cells but with the cannabinoid- and opioid-modulated short-term plasticity of CCK basket cells {cite:p}`Booker2018, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Caputi2009`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphological diversity and connectivity of hippocampal interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses interneuron diversity generally but doesn't specifically detail CCK+/VIP+ basket cell cannabinoid/opioid modulation or short-term plasticity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A subset of VIP/CR interneurons in CA1 projects out of the hippocampus to the medial septum {cite:p}`Toth1993, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`, recapitulating the rare "GABAergic projection neuron" wiring described in earlier classical anatomy {cite:p}`Toth1993, Booker2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphological diversity and connectivity of hippocampal interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions interneuron diversity and connectivity but doesn't specifically discuss VIP/CR projections to medial septum.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A subset of VIP/CR interneurons in CA1 projects out of the hippocampus to the medial septum {cite:p}`Toth1993, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`, recapitulating the rare "GABAergic projection neuron" wiring described in earlier classical anatomy {cite:p}`Toth1993, Booker2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphological diversity and connectivity of hippocampal interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't specifically mention GABAergic projection neurons or long-range hippocampal-to-septal projections.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The hippocampal evidence therefore argues for an *area-specific connectivity logic* in which the molecular identity is conserved but the wiring is repurposed: VIP cells are deployed wherever a cortical or hippocampal microcircuit needs a modulator-targeted, IN-targeting disinhibitor, but the *which-IN* and *which-pyramidal-population* are tuned to local computational requirements {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018, Bilash2023, Tamboli2024, Toth1993`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphological diversity and connectivity of hippocampal interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses interneuron diversity but doesn't specifically address area-specific VIP connectivity logic or repurposing of wiring.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphological diversity and connectivity of hippocampal interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract covers hippocampal interneuron diversity broadly; doesn't address the specific synthesis claims about VIP inputs, outputs, and behavioral roles.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A useful operational summary is the following. *Treat the VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibitory motif as a layer- and area-specific computation that a cortical microcircuit can perform when modulator-targeted afferents are active, not as a fixed wiring rule that all VIP cells obey under all conditions*. This formulation accommodates the canonical landmark results {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Fu2014, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b`, the long-range and thalamic asymmetries {cite:p}`Wall2016, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Cruikshank2012`, the modulator pharmacology {cite:p}`Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Arroyo2012, Vonengelhardt2007, Granger2020, Obermayer2019, Letzkus2011, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017`, the layer- and area-specific exceptions to canonical disinhibition {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`, the developmental and comparative qualifications {cite:p}`Favuzzi2019, Batistabrito2017, Qiu2020, Simacek2025a, Vormsteinschneider2020, Defelipe1999`, and the hippocampal divergence {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018, Bilash2023, Tamboli2024, Toth1993` within a single coherent picture.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Morphological diversity and connectivity of hippocampal interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses hippocampal interneuron diversity generally but doesn't specifically address hippocampal divergence from canonical VIP disinhibitory motif.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Conditional microRNA-pathway deletion in VIP+ cells produces qualitatively similar circuit-level dysfunction {cite:p}`Qiu2020, Batistabrito2017, Favuzzi2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Distinct molecular programs regulate synapse specificity in cortical inhibitory circuits
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention microRNA-pathway deletion in VIP+ cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The minimal sufficient circuit model that {ref}`sec-in-vivo-behavior` will need — for autism, schizophrenia, neonatal injury and epilepsy — must accommodate developmental construction of VIP connectivity, and the comparative literature demands that quantitative rodent ratios be flagged as such when extrapolated to human cortex {cite:p}`Vormsteinschneider2020, Defelipe1999, Batistabrito2017, Qiu2020, Simacek2025a, Favuzzi2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Distinct molecular programs regulate synapse specificity in cortical inhibitory circuits
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss disease models, human cortex extrapolation, or comparative literature caveats.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Top-down feedback from cingulate cortex drives a centre-surround disinhibitory architecture in V1 via Cg→VIP→SST {cite:p}`Zhang2014, Stachniak2023, Bastos2023a`, and in mPFC, mediodorsal-thalamic recruitment of VIP underlies disinhibitory control of pyramidal output during goal-directed behaviour {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows VIP activation enhances mPFC performance but doesn't mention mediodorsal thalamus or thalamic recruitment of VIP specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, the same conjunction recurs: arousal- or reinforcer-related signals from basal forebrain or LC raise VIP activity, the rise is sufficient to suppress local SST output, and the SST suppression in turn opens a dendritic disinhibitory window onto pyramidal neurons {cite:p}`Fu2014, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pi2013, Pakan2016, Sabri2024, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Kamigaki2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract supports VIP activation improving mPFC performance, but doesn't discuss basal forebrain/LC arousal signals or dendritic disinhibitory windows.

insufficient_triples.paper_title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows VIP activation enhances pyramidal output in mPFC but doesn't detail input sources, receptor types, or layer-specific output patterns described in the claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Kamigaki2017` reported that PFC VIP cells in delayed-response tasks integrated cue and reward-related inputs that arrived through both pathways.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows VIP cells are active during delay and enhance performance, but doesn't mention integration of cue and reward inputs through cholinergic and glutamatergic pathways.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The conflict largely dissolves once preparation and behavioural epoch are matched — top-down drive dominates during attentional or top-down sensory tasks, cholinergic drive during locomotion-coupled arousal — and joint manipulation experiments {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kamigaki2017,Williams2019` show the two routes are concurrently active and at least partly multiplicative {cite:p}`Garciadelmolino2017,Hertag2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss joint manipulation of top-down and cholinergic routes or multiplicative interactions.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In PFC, the motif is engaged in attention and working-memory tasks {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Pinto2015,Anastasiades2021a`, but PFC VIP cells receive a richer mix of long-range inputs and project to a broader set of postsynaptic targets, including direct VIP→pyramidal connections that may be more prominent than in sensory cortex {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Delay activity of specific prefrontal interneuron subtypes modulates memory-guided behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract supports VIP engagement in working-memory tasks in PFC but doesn't discuss long-range input diversity or direct VIP→pyramidal connections.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cortical 5-HT3AR-expressing GABAergic interneurons — the molecular umbrella that contains nearly all VIP cells {cite:p}`Rudy2011, Lee2010, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017` — are excited by serotonin via 5-HT3 receptors that produce a fast inward current absent from PV and SST cells {cite:p}`Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and anatomic parcellation of 5-HT3AR expressing cortical interneuron subtypes revealed by single-cell RNA sequencing
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes molecular subtypes and transcriptomics, not electrophysiological responses (fast inward current) or PV/SST comparisons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: As with nAChR excitation, the response is layer- and area-broad and is detectable in vivo as a short-latency increase in VIP firing following raphe stimulation {cite:p}`Frazer2017, Lee2010, Ressurreicao2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and anatomic parcellation of 5-HT3AR expressing cortical interneuron subtypes revealed by single-cell RNA sequencing
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss in vivo raphe stimulation, layer/area-broad serotonergic responses, or VIP firing increases.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Favuzzi2019` showed that the compartment specificity of the major interneuron classes — PV→soma/AIS, SST→dendrites, VIP→other interneurons — is determined by cell-type-specific cell-adhesion and wiring molecules whose expression patterns recapitulate the molecular taxonomy of {ref}`sec-development` {cite:p}`Tasic2018, Pronneke2019, Frazer2017, Favuzzi2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and anatomic parcellation of 5-HT3AR expressing cortical interneuron subtypes revealed by single-cell RNA sequencing
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses transcriptomic parcellation of 5-HT3AR interneurons, not PV→soma/SST→dendrite compartment specificity or cell-adhesion molecules.

insufficient_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and anatomic parcellation of 5-HT3AR expressing cortical interneuron subtypes revealed by single-cell RNA sequencing
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not address synaptic connectivity, modulator pharmacology, or disinhibitory motifs described in the claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A useful operational summary is the following. *Treat the VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibitory motif as a layer- and area-specific computation that a cortical microcircuit can perform when modulator-targeted afferents are active, not as a fixed wiring rule that all VIP cells obey under all conditions*. This formulation accommodates the canonical landmark results {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Fu2014, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b`, the long-range and thalamic asymmetries {cite:p}`Wall2016, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Cruikshank2012`, the modulator pharmacology {cite:p}`Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Arroyo2012, Vonengelhardt2007, Granger2020, Obermayer2019, Letzkus2011, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017`, the layer- and area-specific exceptions to canonical disinhibition {cite:p}`Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Walker2016`, the developmental and comparative qualifications {cite:p}`Favuzzi2019, Batistabrito2017, Qiu2020, Simacek2025a, Vormsteinschneider2020, Defelipe1999`, and the hippocampal divergence {cite:p}`Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018, Bilash2023, Tamboli2024, Toth1993` within a single coherent picture.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Transcriptomic and anatomic parcellation of 5-HT3AR expressing cortical interneuron subtypes revealed by single-cell RNA sequencing
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract addresses transcriptomic diversity, not developmental/comparative qualifications of the disinhibitory motif per se.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The afferent picture mirrors the output one: lateral entorhinal cortex provides the dominant glutamatergic drive onto VIP/IS3 cells in stratum lacunosum-moleculare {cite:p}`Bilash2023, Turi2019, Tyan2014, Booker2018`, and arousal- or novelty-related signals robustly increase IS3 activity during free spatial exploration {cite:p}`Tamboli2024, Francavilla2018a, Turi2019`.

insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention lateral entorhinal cortex drive onto IS3 cells or arousal/novelty-related activity during exploration.

insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows hippocampal VIP wiring differs from cortex but doesn't discuss area-specific connectivity logic or modulator-targeted deployment broadly.
