insufficient_triples.claim_text: In S1, the motif holds in object-localisation and active touch tasks {cite:p}`Williams2019,Sermet2019`, but layer-specific variants have been described in which thalamic inputs to deep-layer VIP cells engage a different downstream target set {cite:p}`Munoz2017,Sermet2019,Garciajuncoclemente2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Pathway-, layer- and cell-type-specific thalamic input to mouse barrel cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows VIP cells receive intermediate thalamic input with less layer specificity; does not discuss downstream target set differences for deep-layer VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Smith2019` framed VIP as one of the most widely expressed neuropeptide-precursor transcripts across mouse VISp/ALM single-cell transcriptomes, embedding the VIP intrinsic-feature class within a cortex-wide peptidergic-signalling network {cite:p}`Smith2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Single-cell transcriptomic evidence for dense intracortical neuropeptide networks
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses NPP/NP-GPCR expression broadly but does not single out VIP as one of the most widely expressed NPP transcripts.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Smith2019` framed VIP as one of the most widely expressed neuropeptide-precursor transcripts across mouse VISp/ALM single-cell transcriptomes, embedding the VIP intrinsic-feature class within a cortex-wide peptidergic-signalling network {cite:p}`Smith2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Single-cell transcriptomic evidence for dense intracortical neuropeptide networks
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses NPP/NP-GPCR expression broadly but does not single out VIP as one of the most widely expressed NPP transcripts.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Vip t-types are distributed in a layer-stratified pattern (Vip Col15a1 Pde1a at the L1–L2/3 border, Vip Chat Htr1f spanning L2/3, Sncg Vip Itih5 across multiple layers), so laminar location is partially diagnostic of within-VIP type {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Tasic2018,Tasic2016,Yao2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A taxonomy of transcriptomic cell types across the isocortex and hippocampal formation
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions continuous/graded cell-type variations but does not detail specific Vip t-type laminar distributions.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Values are mean ± SEM with n given per entry; entries are {cite:p}`Michaud2024` (R_{in} 517 ± 37 MΩ, n=7 VIP/CR+ I-S3 cells, mouse CA1) and {cite:p}`Rhomberg2018` (R_{in} 350.3 ± 28.3 MΩ, n=30 VIP+ IS-INs, mouse BLA). *Caveat (verbatim from Phase-6 audit):* Cross-region (CA1 stratum radiatum vs BLA) — must be flagged in any figure caption. | n=2 entries — minimal cross-paper comparison. *Panel C:* Stacked bar of reported irregular-spiking fraction across studies.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive Intestinal Polypeptide-Immunoreactive Interneurons within Circuits of the Mouse Basolateral Amygdala
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report specific R_in values (350.3 ± 28.3 MΩ) or sample size (n=30) for VIP+ IS-INs.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Per-entry denominators are annotated on the bars per Phase-6 audit requirement; entries: {cite:p}`Cauli1997` (15 IS cells / 97 nonpyramidal interneurons sampled = 15.5% of nonpyramidal pool; the 15 IS cells co-expressed CR+VIP, rat S1, single-cell RT-PCR + patch); {cite:p}`Cauli2000` (16/60 = 26.7% IS-VIP cluster, rat frontal cortex L2–3, fusiform interneurons clustered by RT-PCR + patch); {cite:p}`Pronneke2015` (14.5% of whole-population VIP-Cre/tdTomato barrel cortex); {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a` (5/17 = 29.4% of mPFC L1b VIP-Cre × Ai14+ cells, mouse). *Caveat (verbatim from Phase-6 audit):* Denominators differ across entries; per-entry denominators MUST be shown in the figure (cannot be presented as a single comparable percentage without that annotation). | Rat (Cauli) vs mouse (Anastasiades) species mismatch. | Different cortical areas (S1, frontal cortex, mPFC L1b) and slightly different firing-class taxonomies. *Panel D:* Single-row entry showing membrane time constant τ_{m} (ms) for the only entry retained after Phase-6 audit removed {cite:t}`Rhomberg2018` from the τ_{m} sub-comparison ({cite:p}`Michaud2024` reports τ_{m} = 36.5 ± 3.2 ms, n=7 VIP/CR+ I-S3 cells, mouse CA1 stratum radiatum); panel reduced to a single-row entry per the iter3 audit.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Vasoactive Intestinal Polypeptide-Immunoreactive Interneurons within Circuits of the Mouse Basolateral Amygdala
insufficient_triples.note: The claim attributes specific fractions/denominators to other cited papers; Rhomberg2018's abstract lacks these quantitative details.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Higher-order thalamic nuclei — POm in the somatosensory pathway and pulvinar/LP in the visual stream — innervate VIP cells robustly, often with response probabilities and amplitudes comparable to those onto pyramidal targets {cite:p}`Williams2019, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a, Ramamurthy2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: VIP interneurons in sensory cortex encode sensory and action signals but not direct reward signals
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses VIP cell responses to sensory/action/reward signals, not thalamic (POm) synaptic innervation of VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, the rabies-tracing asymmetry of {cite:t}`Wall2016` has been recapitulated across cortical areas with both pseudo-typed rabies and CTB injection assays, all converging on the same direction of effect even when absolute counts differ {cite:p}`Wall2016, Audette2018, Anastasiades2021a, Ramamurthy2023, Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: VIP interneurons in sensory cortex encode sensory and action signals but not direct reward signals
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not mention rabies tracing, CTB injections, or long-range input asymmetries to VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Independent reports of meaningful VIP→PV connection rates in barrel and frontal cortices {cite:p}`Walker2016, Anastasiades2021a, Pi2013` and of VIP synapses onto NDNF/L1 interneurons {cite:p}`Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Abs2018` further enlarge the output set.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Increased Callosal Connectivity in Reeler Mice Revealed by Brain-Wide Input Mapping of VIP Neurons in Barrel Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses brain-wide inputs to VIP neurons, not VIP→PV connection rates or VIP output connectivity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The synaptic-connectivity picture that emerges from 467 papers is a structured composite. **On the input side**, VIP interneurons are a preferential target of long-range cortical and higher-order thalamic glutamatergic input, of basal-forebrain cholinergic input via slow nicotinic receptors, of raphe serotonergic input via 5-HT3 receptors, and of mixed-sign noradrenergic input — while local pyramidal drive is comparable to that onto SST and PV cells {cite:p}`Wall2016, Lee2013, Zhang2014, Williams2019, Anastasiades2021a, Cruikshank2012, Audette2018, Stachniak2023, Bilash2023, Porter1999, Kawaguchi1997b, Kawaguchi1998, Vonengelhardt2007, Arroyo2012, Fu2014, Ferezou2002, Vucurovic2010, Frazer2017, Letzkus2011`. **On the output side**, VIP cells preferentially inhibit SST interneurons, but they also inhibit PV cells in deeper layers, NDNF/L1 interneurons, pyramidal cells in deep cortex and OLM cells in CA1, with substantial cell-to-cell heterogeneity that the canonical schematic flattens {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013, Pi2013, Lee2013, Karnani2016a, Karnani2016b, Walker2016, Yu2019, Zhou2017, Anastasiades2021a, Schuman2019, Hafner2021, Tyan2014, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Booker2018`. **On the behavioural side**, the consequence of this wiring is a deployable disinhibitory operation that is recruited by arousal and reinforcement signals across V1, A1, mPFC, BLA and CA1, but which is sign-dependent on visual context, source-area-specific in cross-modal attention, and layer-dependent in its non-SST outputs {cite:p}`Fu2014, Pi2013, Letzkus2011, Krabbe2019, Pakan2016, Dipoppa2018, Sabri2024, Kamigaki2017, Pinto2015, Szadai2022, Tamboli2024, Ibrahim2016, Kaneko2024, Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Increased Callosal Connectivity in Reeler Mice Revealed by Brain-Wide Input Mapping of VIP Neurons in Barrel Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract addresses inputs to VIP neurons in reeler mice, not the broad synaptic connectivity synthesis claimed here.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Noradrenergic recruitment is the least monolithic of the three: α-adrenergic stimulation excites a fraction of VIP/CR cells in slice {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1998, Cauli2014, Caputi2009`, while β-adrenergic and tonic LC-driven effects vary across area, behavioural state and recording configuration {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1998, Cauli2014, Letzkus2011, Szadai2022`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Two Calretinin-Positive GABAergic Cell Types in Layer 2/3 of the Mouse Neocortex Provide Different Forms of Inhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses CR+ GABAergic cells, gap junctions, and oscillations; no mention of α-adrenergic stimulation of VIP/CR cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The CCK+/VIP+ basket subset, by contrast, deploys a perisomatic inhibition reminiscent of cortical PV cells but with the cannabinoid- and opioid-modulated short-term plasticity of CCK basket cells {cite:p}`Booker2018, Turi2019, Francavilla2018a, Caputi2009`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Two Calretinin-Positive GABAergic Cell Types in Layer 2/3 of the Mouse Neocortex Provide Different Forms of Inhibition
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses cortical CR+ interneuron subtypes; no mention of CCK+/VIP+ basket cells, perisomatic inhibition, or cannabinoid/opioid modulation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pharmacological and optogenetic isolation of cholinergic transmission has since shown that VIP cells across V1, A1, S1, and PFC respond rapidly to ACh release through α4β2 and α7 nicotinic receptors {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019,Anastasiades2021a,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b`, with response amplitudes that can rival those produced by direct optogenetic stimulation of long-range glutamatergic axons.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: The short-term plasticity of VIP interneurons in motor cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses short-term plasticity at VIP synapses in motor cortex; no mention of cholinergic transmission, nicotinic receptors, or ACh.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Recent paired-recording work by {cite:t}`Mcfarlan2024a` demonstrates that VIP→Martinotti and VIP→basket synapses in motor cortex undergo spike-timing-dependent plasticity, providing the first formal demonstration that the VIP-driven disinhibitory operation is itself plastic — a finding that complicates any "fixed canonical motif" reading of cluster_05 {cite:p}`Mcfarlan2024a, Mcfarlan2024b, Wiera2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Input specificity of NMDA-dependent GABAergic plasticity in the hippocampus
insufficient_triples.note: Wiera2024 addresses NMDA-dependent GABAergic plasticity at SST/PV→pyramidal cell synapses in hippocampus, not VIP→Martinotti or VIP→basket synapse plasticity. No mention of VIP interneurons or spike-timing-dependent plasticity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Plasticity at the VIP→SST synapse, in turn, places experience-dependent gain control inside the disinhibitory loop rather than only at its pyramidal output {cite:p}`Mcfarlan2024a, Karnani2016a, Wiera2024, Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Input specificity of NMDA-dependent GABAergic plasticity in the hippocampus
insufficient_triples.note: Wiera2024 studies NMDA-dependent plasticity at inhibitory synapses from SST/PV interneurons onto CA1 pyramidal cells in hippocampus, not plasticity at VIP→SST synapses or experience-dependent gain control within a disinhibitory loop.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cluster_13 of the cholinergic literature contains an explicit opposite-sign disagreement: {cite:t}`Arroyo2012` and convergent work establish that the dominant ACh effect on cortical VIP / 5-HT3AR cells is nicotinic excitation through slow, β2-containing nAChRs {cite:p}`Arroyo2012, Porter1999, Fu2014`, while {cite:t}`Poorthuis2018` and matched studies emphasise a muscarinic *inhibition* of a VIP subset {cite:p}`Poorthuis2018, Kawaguchi1997b, Obermayer2019, Granger2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Rapid Neuromodulation of Layer 1 Interneurons in Human Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms nicotinic recruitment of L1-INs but does not mention VIP cells, β2-containing nAChRs, or muscarinic inhibition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Cluster_13 of the cholinergic literature contains an explicit opposite-sign disagreement: {cite:t}`Arroyo2012` and convergent work establish that the dominant ACh effect on cortical VIP / 5-HT3AR cells is nicotinic excitation through slow, β2-containing nAChRs {cite:p}`Arroyo2012, Porter1999, Fu2014`, while {cite:t}`Poorthuis2018` and matched studies emphasise a muscarinic *inhibition* of a VIP subset {cite:p}`Poorthuis2018, Kawaguchi1997b, Obermayer2019, Granger2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Rapid Neuromodulation of Layer 1 Interneurons in Human Neocortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms nicotinic recruitment of L1-INs but does not mention VIP cells, β2-containing nAChRs, or muscarinic inhibition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The connectivity rules described in earlier subsections are therefore not given but built; they are what an experience-tuned, modulator-targeted cell class looks like once the postnatal program has run to completion {cite:p}`Favuzzi2019, Batistabrito2017, Qiu2020, Simacek2025a, Abbah2022`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Oxidative Stress-Induced Damage to the Developing Hippocampus Is Mediated by GSK3
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows postnatal vulnerability and reversibility of interneuron development but doesn't specifically address connectivity rules being 'built' vs 'given' as framed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Yet the same body of work also exposes its limits: the same VIP perturbation yields different sign and magnitude of pyramidal modulation across area, layer, and behavioural state, and inhibition of SST cells is neither necessary nor sufficient to explain observed gain effects in several preparations {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Garrett2020,Stachniak2021`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Postmitotic Prox1 Expression Controls the Final Specification of Cortical VIP Interneuron Subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: Paper addresses Prox1 role in VIP subtype specification, not VIP perturbation effects on pyramidal cells or gain modulation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pakan2016` reported, in the same cortical area but a broader behavioural range, that locomotion-evoked VIP and SST activity were not simple mirrors and that SST cells were not uniformly suppressed during locomotion. **Resolution:** the canonical motif holds as a *component* explanation in V1 superficial layers under top-down or cholinergic drive but does not generalise as a *sole* explanation across all behavioural contexts; cortical area, layer, and visual input together determine whether SST cells follow the canonical disinhibition trajectory or move independently {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Garrett2020,Stachniak2021,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Postmitotic Prox1 Expression Controls the Final Specification of Cortical VIP Interneuron Subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: Stachniak2021 is about Prox1 regulation of VIP interneuron subtypes in development, not about behavioral contexts, SST suppression during locomotion, or disinhibition motif generalization across cortical areas.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The picture from connectomics in primary visual cortex {cite:p}`Schneidermizell2025` and from cross-species comparisons {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Vanderveer2021,Caulinorocha2022` is that VIP cells in different cortical areas and species have different output ratios, so there is no single answer to the question ‘how strong is VIP→pyramidal inhibition?’ in the way the canonical schematic implies.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Role for Somatostatin-Positive Interneurons in Neuro-Oscillatory and Information Processing Deficits in Schizophrenia
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract focuses on SST interneurons in schizophrenia; does not discuss VIP→pyramidal contact variability or connectomics across species.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In primate and human cortex, comparative anatomy and Patch-seq data suggest that the VIP→SST bias is preserved but that the absolute density and laminar organisation of VIP cells differ substantially {cite:p}`Pronneke2015,Vanderveer2021,Caulinorocha2022`, with implications discussed further in {ref}`sec-cross-areas`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Role for Somatostatin-Positive Interneurons in Neuro-Oscillatory and Information Processing Deficits in Schizophrenia
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP cell density, laminar organization, Patch-seq data, or primate/human comparative anatomy of VIP→SST bias.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In S1, the motif holds in object-localisation and active touch tasks {cite:p}`Williams2019,Sermet2019`, but layer-specific variants have been described in which thalamic inputs to deep-layer VIP cells engage a different downstream target set {cite:p}`Munoz2017,Sermet2019,Garciajuncoclemente2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: An inhibitory pull--push circuit in frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is about frontal cortex VIP circuits, not S1 or layer-specific thalamic inputs to deep-layer VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Cadwell2016` and {cite:t}`Fuzik2016` independently demonstrated the technique on cortical interneurons and showed concordance between firing-pattern class and transcriptomic class at the L1 level (n=67 cells in {cite:t}`Cadwell2016`).
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Integration of electrophysiological recordings with single-cell RNA-seq data identifies neuronal subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't specify n=67 cells or concordance at 'L1 level'; it describes Patch-seq on cortical neurons but lacks these specifics.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Vip t-types are distributed in a layer-stratified pattern (Vip Col15a1 Pde1a at the L1–L2/3 border, Vip Chat Htr1f spanning L2/3, Sncg Vip Itih5 across multiple layers), so laminar location is partially diagnostic of within-VIP type {cite:p}`Gouwens2020a,Tasic2018,Tasic2016,Yao2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Adult mouse cortical cell taxonomy revealed by single cell transcriptomics
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions 49 transcriptomic types and VIP subtypes implicitly but does not detail specific VIP subtype laminar distributions.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Bakken2021a`, in the BICCN cross-species motor-cortex taxonomy, showed that *Vip* GABAergic subtypes are markedly more diversified between humans/marmosets and mice than between primates, indicating species-specific expansion of the VIP transcriptomic axis {cite:p}`Bakken2021a,Braininitiativecellcensusnetworkbiccn2021`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A multimodal cell census and atlas of the mammalian primary motor cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions conserved consensus taxonomy across species but does not specifically discuss VIP subtype diversification or species-specific VIP expansion.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two further details place the intrinsic profile in its developmental and circuit context: all cortical VIP cells originate from the CGE {cite:p}`Lim2018,Cauli2014`, segregating sharply from the MGE-derived PV/SST classes that account for the bulk of the rest of the inhibitory taxonomy {cite:p}`Rudy2011,Karnani2016a,Pfeffer2013`; and a small ChAT-expressing VIP subpopulation directly *excites* (rather than inhibits) neighbouring pyramidal and interneurons through fast cholinergic + GABA co-transmission {cite:p}`Obermayer2019` — a counter-example to the strict-disinhibitor framing that {ref}`sec-synaptic-properties` returns to in detail.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Development and Functional Diversification of Cortical Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms CGE origin and interneuron diversity but does not specifically state all VIP cells originate from CGE.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In thalamo-prefrontal pathways, mediodorsal thalamus directly recruits VIP cells in mPFC L2/3 and supports goal-directed disinhibition during cognitive tasks {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a, Kim2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Brain-wide Maps Reveal Stereotyped Cell-Type-Based Cortical Architecture and Subcortical Sexual Dimorphism
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes cortical cell-type distributions and sexual dimorphism, not thalamo-prefrontal VIP recruitment or cognitive disinhibition.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In S1, the motif holds in object-localisation and active touch tasks {cite:p}`Williams2019,Sermet2019`, but layer-specific variants have been described in which thalamic inputs to deep-layer VIP cells engage a different downstream target set {cite:p}`Munoz2017,Sermet2019,Garciajuncoclemente2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: The paper studies layer-specific Sst modulation in neocortex but doesn't specifically describe S1 thalamic inputs to deep-layer VIP cells targeting different downstream sets.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The numerical asymmetry is part of why the canonical motif works: a small VIP population can disinhibit a much larger pyramidal population if its synaptic targeting is sufficiently selective, but the same numerical asymmetry constrains how much absolute gain can be produced through any single motif and forces the engagement of multiple parallel motifs to scale gain with task demand {cite:p}`Hertag2019,Garciadelmolino2017,Litwinkumar2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory stabilization and visual coding in cortical circuits with multiple interneuron subtypes
insufficient_triples.note: The abstract discusses disinhibition and interneuron subtypes but does not address numerical asymmetry constraining gain or requiring multiple parallel motifs to scale gain.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The picture from these and related studies {cite:p}`Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022,Veit2023,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b,Garciajuncoclemente2017` is that the canonical motif's *behavioural* contribution is largely about gating plasticity windows and sustaining attentional or learning-related disinhibition, not about producing transient gain on a millisecond-to-second timescale. This is consistent with the kinetic profile of VIP→SST IPSCs, which is slower than VIP→PV {cite:p}`Pi2013`, and with the fact that VIP-cell perturbation typically produces slow-timescale changes in pyramidal firing rather than fast modulation of stimulus-evoked responses {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Kuchibhotla2017,Pinto2015,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss VIP→SST vs VIP→PV IPSC kinetics or decay constants; specific kinetic comparison not reported.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The privilege is real: of the documented VIP outputs, the SST contact carries the largest mean charge per spike and the longest decay constant {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Pi2013,Lee2013`, the SST cell is the inhibitory class whose dendritic targeting most directly limits pyramidal stimulus-evoked gain {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Karnani2016a,Veit2023`, and the recruitment routes that drive VIP cells (top-down glutamatergic and ascending cholinergic) overlap closely with the routes that signal behavioural state {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Hangya2015,Pinto2015,Kamigaki2017,Williams2019`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not report charge per spike or decay constant comparisons for VIP→SST vs other outputs.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Pi2013` provided the first such test: optogenetic inhibition of VIP cells during a discrimination task disrupted both reinforcement-aligned activity and behavioural performance.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP recruitment by reinforcement and gain modulation but does not explicitly describe optogenetic VIP inhibition disrupting task performance.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The {cite:t}`Pi2013` vs Ramamurthy heterogeneity contrast and the {cite:t}`Fu2014` vs {cite:t}`Pakan2016` SST direction contrast are well supported by the in-set evidence and are presented as full conflict admonitions above; the remaining two conflicts from earlier work are presented as gap-flagged conflict admonitions.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions uniform VIP activation; heterogeneity contrast with Ramamurthy not assessable from abstract alone.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The motif is layer-biased, with strongest disinhibitory effects in L2/3, and is supported by convergent long-range cortical and subcortical drive consistent with VIP cells being broadcast targets of neuromodulatory and top-down signals {cite:p}`Pi2013,Wall2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not specifically discuss layer-biased effects (strongest in L2/3) or long-range/neuromodulatory convergent drive onto VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, a layer-1 subset of VIP cells (overlapping with NDNF) gates auditory critical-period plasticity: {cite:t}`Takesian2018` showed that L1 VIP cells inhibit L2/3 PV cells, that nicotinic cholinergic input from basal forebrain activates them, and that reactivating L1 VIP cells in adults reopens tonotopic plasticity — extending the motif from instantaneous gain to developmental and recovery time-scales {cite:p}`Takesian2018,Pi2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical interneurons that specialize in disinhibitory control
insufficient_triples.note: Pi2013 abstract describes VIP→SST disinhibition in A1 but does not discuss L1 VIP subset, NDNF, nicotinic input, or critical-period plasticity.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The canonical motif is a useful first hypothesis whenever a salient external event coincides with arousal in a cortical task; it is a misleading default when the task involves stationary visual processing in which locomotion is absent {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Garrett2020`, when the cortical area engages a parallel cholinergic stream {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017,Williams2019`, when learning-related plasticity rather than acute gain is the readout {cite:p}`Cichon2017,Krabbe2019,Abs2018,Tamboli2024`, or when the behavioural context engages NDNF-mediated apical dendritic disinhibition that may operate on its own timescale {cite:p}`Abs2018,Veit2023,Anastasiades2021a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms
insufficient_triples.note: Veit2023 addresses VIP→SST effects on gamma, not NDNF-mediated apical dendritic disinhibition specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The non-uniqueness is also real: VIP cells form measurable contacts on PV, pyramidal, NDNF, and CCK targets, all of which are behaviourally engaged in some condition documented in the literature reviewed here {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms
insufficient_triples.note: Veit2023 focuses on VIP→SST effects on gamma; abstract doesn't mention VIP contacts on PV, pyramidal, NDNF, or CCK targets.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In darkness or at low contrast, SST cells are less active than VIP cells, recovering an apparent VIP-up/SST-down profile; with strong visual drive the two are co-activated, and the net effect on pyramidal gain still shifts because VIP recruitment is proportionally larger {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss darkness/low-contrast conditions or co-activation profiles of SST and VIP at different contrasts.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The cartoon overlay (panel C) shows a head-fixed mouse on a wheel with V1/A1/S1 sketches indicating concurrent expected VIP-vs-SST polarity by area, with the auditory-cortex cell highlighted as the explicit area-paradox leaf {cite:p}`Yavorska2021`. {cite:p}`Veit2023,Garciajuncoclemente2017,Kannan2022` :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract doesn't discuss area-specific VIP-vs-SST polarity differences or paradoxical effects across cortical areas.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Anaesthetic-induced burst suppression provides a counterpoint that bounds the VIP role {cite:p}`Yin2025,Bohannon2018,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses gamma modulation in awake V1, not anaesthetic-induced burst suppression.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Fourth, the motif is not the *defining* function of the VIP class: VIP cells participate in oscillatory pacing {cite:p}`Turi2019,Bastos2023a`, plasticity gating {cite:p}`Cichon2017,Krabbe2019,Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022`, developmental wiring {cite:p}`Batistabrito2017,Priya2018,Ferguson2023a,Ferguson2023b`, and direct pyramidal control {cite:p}`Karnani2016b,Lee2013,Schneidermizell2025`, none of which are captured by the canonical motif alone.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2013 focuses on VIP→SST disinhibition; abstract does not explicitly document direct VIP→pyramidal control.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The picture from these and related studies {cite:p}`Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022,Veit2023,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b,Garciajuncoclemente2017` is that the canonical motif's *behavioural* contribution is largely about gating plasticity windows and sustaining attentional or learning-related disinhibition, not about producing transient gain on a millisecond-to-second timescale. This is consistent with the kinetic profile of VIP→SST IPSCs, which is slower than VIP→PV {cite:p}`Pi2013`, and with the fact that VIP-cell perturbation typically produces slow-timescale changes in pyramidal firing rather than fast modulation of stimulus-evoked responses {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Kuchibhotla2017,Pinto2015,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2013 used optogenetic activation of vM1→S1; abstract doesn't specifically discuss slow-timescale pyramidal firing changes from VIP perturbation.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Lee2013` showed that whisking-induced motor inputs from M1 activate S1 VIP cells, which inhibit SST cells and disinhibit L2/3 pyramidal neurons during active sensing; optogenetic VIP silencing during active whisking reduced pyramidal firing while VIP activation mimicked the whisking-induced enhancement, establishing the VIP→SST motif as canonical in S1 as well as A1 and mPFC {cite:p}`Lee2013,Pi2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms VIP activation and SST inhibition during whisking via vM1, but does not mention optogenetic VIP silencing reducing pyramidal firing or VIP activation mimicking whisking enhancement.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Lee2013` showed that whisking-induced motor inputs from M1 activate S1 VIP cells, which inhibit SST cells and disinhibit L2/3 pyramidal neurons during active sensing; optogenetic VIP silencing during active whisking reduced pyramidal firing while VIP activation mimicked the whisking-induced enhancement, establishing the VIP→SST motif as canonical in S1 as well as A1 and mPFC {cite:p}`Lee2013,Pi2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Same claim as T_6458ee2fdf7e: abstract doesn't mention optogenetic VIP silencing/activation experiments on pyramidal firing.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Sermet2019` mapped POm and VPM inputs and found that S1 VIP cells in superficial layers receive prominent higher-order POm input but minimal first-order VPM drive, contrasting with PV/SST distributions, and providing a circuit substrate for integrating motor context and sensory feedback {cite:p}`Sermet2019,Lee2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2013 shows vM1→VIP pathway but does not address POm vs VPM thalamic inputs to VIP cells. Role as contradicts is unclear.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Arroyo2023` recorded VIP cells in mouse motor cortex through skilled-reach learning and reported that VIP cells develop preparatory activity and acquire selectivity for upcoming actions in parallel with pyramidal preparatory dynamics, suggesting that VIP recruitment is a learned component of motor preparation rather than a simple reactive disinhibition; the authors flag the result as a single-paper claim awaiting independent confirmation in other motor regions and tasks {cite:p}`Arroyo2023,Lee2013`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A disinhibitory circuit mediates motor integration in the somatosensory cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Lee2013 is about motor-to-sensory disinhibition in S1, not about VIP preparatory activity in motor cortex during skilled-reach learning.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The state-messenger reading was crystallised by {cite:t}`Fu2014`, whose V1 recordings emphasised that the locomotion-evoked VIP response was largely insensitive to grating orientation or contrast and behaved as a multiplicative gain knob.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP activation independent of visual stimulation but does not explicitly discuss orientation/contrast insensitivity or multiplicative gain.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The {cite:t}`Pi2013` vs Ramamurthy heterogeneity contrast and the {cite:t}`Fu2014` vs {cite:t}`Pakan2016` SST direction contrast are well supported by the in-set evidence and are presented as full conflict admonitions above; the remaining two conflicts from earlier work are presented as gap-flagged conflict admonitions.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: A Cortical Circuit for Gain Control by Behavioral State
insufficient_triples.note: Claim references a 'Fu2014 vs Pakan2016 SST direction contrast'; Fu2014 focuses on VIP, not SST direction conflict specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The cells therefore have direct anatomical access to apical dendrites of L5 pyramidal neurons, providing one route by which top-down signals from cingulate cortex can be funnelled into deep-layer pyramidal output via VIP recruitment {cite:p}`Bastos2023a,Wall2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Top-down input modulates visual context processing through an interneuron-specific circuit
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows ACa→V1 targets VIP cells, but abstract doesn't specifically discuss apical dendrites of L5 pyramidal neurons or that route.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The morphological substrate identified by {cite:t}`Kawaguchi1996` and {cite:t}`Gonchar2008` — bipolar VIP cells with vertical apical-targeting axons — provides the direct anatomical route from this top-down input onto deep-layer pyramidal output {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1996,Gonchar2008,Bastos2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Top-down input modulates visual context processing through an interneuron-specific circuit
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows ACa→V1 activates VIP cells but abstract doesn't discuss bipolar morphology or deep-layer pyramidal targeting by VIP axons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Within the available evidence the same asymmetry can be partly reconstructed: VIP-receptor blockade in rat neocortical slices nearly abolishes spike-and-wave discharges {cite:p}`Hall2015`, indicating that VIP peptidergic signalling is *necessary* for one form of epileptiform activity, while broader perturbation studies in non-seizure contexts find that VIP activation reshapes oscillation power and coherence without abolishing the underlying rhythm {cite:p}`Veit2023,Bastos2023a,Sabri2024`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Top-down input modulates visual context processing through an interneuron-specific circuit
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows VIP silencing abolishes synchrony/deviance detection, not merely 'reshapes without abolishing the underlying rhythm.'

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The drive into these cells comes from the basal nucleus and thalamus, providing an arousal-linked substrate for state-dependent recruitment {cite:p}`Krabbe2019,Wall2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Adaptive disinhibitory gating by VIP interneurons permits associative learning
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP in BLA activated by aversive events but does not specify basal nucleus/thalamic input as arousal-linked substrate.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Yet the same lineage implements opposite-sign locomotion gating in V1 versus A1 (V1 up, A1 down — see {ref}`sec-oscillatory-dynamics`) {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016,Pi2013,Krabbe2019`, motor-copy disinhibition in S1 {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019`, sustained working-memory dynamics in mPFC {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Anastasiades2021a`, an interneuron-selective IS-3 motif in CA1 {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Gulyas1996`, and peptidergic synchronisation in SCN {cite:p}`Maywood2006,Cutler2003,Webb2009,Jones2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Adaptive disinhibitory gating by VIP interneurons permits associative learning
insufficient_triples.note: Krabbe2019 is about BLA, not A1. It does not address opposite-sign locomotion gating between V1 and A1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The picture from these and related studies {cite:p}`Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022,Veit2023,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b,Garciajuncoclemente2017` is that the canonical motif's *behavioural* contribution is largely about gating plasticity windows and sustaining attentional or learning-related disinhibition, not about producing transient gain on a millisecond-to-second timescale. This is consistent with the kinetic profile of VIP→SST IPSCs, which is slower than VIP→PV {cite:p}`Pi2013`, and with the fact that VIP-cell perturbation typically produces slow-timescale changes in pyramidal firing rather than fast modulation of stimulus-evoked responses {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Kuchibhotla2017,Pinto2015,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Paper focuses on context-dependent modulation via parallel inhibition; slow-timescale VIP perturbation effects not specifically addressed in abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Many *in vivo* perturbation studies use short, intense optogenetic activation that bypasses the gating logic by which VIP cells are normally recruited, producing larger and less selective effects than chemogenetic or behaviourally evoked recruitment {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Pakan2016,Kuchibhotla2017,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not specifically discuss optogenetic vs chemogenetic activation or bypassing gating logic.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Kuchibhotla2017` showed that disrupting PV, SST, and VIP cells produces dissociable, behaviour-specific deficits — VIP loss impairing context-dependent gating rather than basic discrimination — supporting separable inhibitory channels.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes parallel cholinergic modulation of all three interneuron types but does not mention VIP-specific loss impairing context-dependent gating or dissociable behaviour-specific deficits from cell-type disruption.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In layer 5 and layer 6, the motif is engaged differently: deep-layer VIP cells receive a higher proportion of thalamic input and contact a different distribution of postsynaptic targets, including projection-class pyramidal neurons that are themselves heterogeneous {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Sermet2019,Munoz2017,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper focuses on L1 VIP and NDNF cells; does not specifically address deep-layer (L5/L6) VIP thalamic input proportions or targets.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Yet the same lineage implements opposite-sign locomotion gating in V1 versus A1 (V1 up, A1 down — see {ref}`sec-oscillatory-dynamics`) {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016,Pi2013,Krabbe2019`, motor-copy disinhibition in S1 {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019`, sustained working-memory dynamics in mPFC {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Anastasiades2021a`, an interneuron-selective IS-3 motif in CA1 {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Gulyas1996`, and peptidergic synchronisation in SCN {cite:p}`Maywood2006,Cutler2003,Webb2009,Jones2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows thalamic engagement of VIP in PFC L1 but does not specifically address sustained working-memory delay-period dynamics.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Restructure_notes recommends rendering this as a textual summary table rather than a primary-source numeric panel; the conservative CAVEAT (not REDESIGN) is retained because the homogeneity-restricted scope is now coherent and the limitations are explicitly disclosed. **Panel C**: Hippocampal VIP/IS-3 motif schematic (VIP/CR/CCK distinctions; OLM and bistratified targeting in CA1) {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Gulyas1996,Kawaguchi1996`. **Panel D**: Cross-area conflict table summarising top-down, neuromodulatory, and sensory drivers of VIP recruitment by region {cite:p}`Wall2016,Pi2013,Lee2013,Dipoppa2018,Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Maywood2006`. *Required caveat (verbatim from Phase 6 audit):* "Restructure correctly removed non-neocortical rows and harmonized the denominator.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Role is 'contradicts' but the claim is about a figure panel description; the paper supports VIP recruitment by thalamus, unclear what is contradicted.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: (ii) The recruiting variable, not the local microcircuit, is what differs most across regions, and many cross-area apparent contradictions in VIP "polarity" or "selectivity" dissolve once the recruiting variable is specified {cite:p}`Wall2016,Anastasiades2021a,Lee2013,Pakan2016,Jones2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mediodorsal and Ventromedial Thalamus Engage Distinct L1 Circuits in the Prefrontal Cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Role is 'contradicts' but claim says apparent contradictions dissolve once recruiting variable is specified; paper shows region-specific thalamic recruitment of VIP, plausible support but contradiction role is unclear.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: A subtler issue, often glossed in summary diagrams, is that the V1 locomotion signal is composite: it carries cholinergic, noradrenergic, and arousal-related components that load differently on VIP cells depending on whether the animal is running, simply pupil-dilated, or actively engaged in a task {cite:p}`Reimer2014,Garciajuncoclemente2017,Collins2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: An inhibitory pull--push circuit in frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is in frontal cortex, not V1; discusses arousal but not decomposition of locomotion signal components in V1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Critically, however, reinforcement responses are not exclusive: the same cells co-encode locomotion and arousal, and disentangling reinforcement-specific from state-driven components requires task designs that orthogonalise the two {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Szadai2022,Garciajuncoclemente2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: An inhibitory pull--push circuit in frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper discusses arousal-driven VIP activity but does not address reinforcement signals or orthogonalizing state vs reinforcement.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The cartoon overlay (panel C) shows a head-fixed mouse on a wheel with V1/A1/S1 sketches indicating concurrent expected VIP-vs-SST polarity by area, with the auditory-cortex cell highlighted as the explicit area-paradox leaf {cite:p}`Yavorska2021`. {cite:p}`Veit2023,Garciajuncoclemente2017,Kannan2022` :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: An inhibitory pull--push circuit in frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is about frontal cortex, not auditory/visual/somatosensory cortex; its role as an 'area-paradox' reference is unclear from abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: PV–pyramidal interactions set gamma frequency, OLM and septal inputs set hippocampal theta, and PV cells dominate burst-suppression synchrony — VIP cells modulate the *power, coherence, and phase coupling* of these rhythms via SST/PV control {cite:p}`Veit2023,Wagatsuma2023,Tyan2014,Bell2021,Yin2025,Bohannon2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
insufficient_triples.note: Tyan2014 shows IS3→OLM control but does not address gamma/theta power, coherence, or phase coupling modulation by VIP cells directly.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **Schematic mechanism: VIP→SST and VIP→PV motifs differentially shape gamma versus theta rhythms.** Schematic with no quantitative data substrate. **(A)** PV-driven gamma rhythm with VIP-mediated SST suppression: VIP activation predicted to scale local gamma power {cite:p}`Veit2023,Wagatsuma2023` and tune long-range gamma coherence {cite:p}`Veit2023`. **(B)** Hippocampal theta-rhythm circuit with VIP/IS3 cell phase-coupled to OLM disinhibition; CR/IS3 cells fire on the rising phase and peak of theta and selectively innervate OLM dendrites {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Luo2020,Chamberland2010`. **(C)** Cortical Up-state schematic with VIP cells as a permissive (slice-silent, awake-recruited) versus causal gate, capturing the {cite:t}`Tahvildari2012` versus {cite:t}`Szadai2022` disagreement about Up-state generation. **Caveat:** Schematic only — no audited quantitative substrate. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
insufficient_triples.note: Tyan2014 shows IS3→OLM dendritic innervation and firing control but does not report IS3 cells firing on rising phase/peak of theta rhythm in vivo.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Long-range tracing places these cells at the receiving end of cholinergic, serotonergic, and frontal cortical inputs disproportionately enriched relative to PV/SST cells {cite:p}`Wall2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows cholinergic (basal nucleus) and thalamic inputs enriched onto VIP, but does not specifically address serotonergic or frontal cortical enrichment.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The cells therefore have direct anatomical access to apical dendrites of L5 pyramidal neurons, providing one route by which top-down signals from cingulate cortex can be funnelled into deep-layer pyramidal output via VIP recruitment {cite:p}`Bastos2023a,Wall2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Paper maps long-range inputs to VIP cells but does not discuss cingulate-to-VIP-to-L5 pyramidal routing or apical dendrite access.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Within the present packet, the available evidence shows that A1 VIP cells are robustly recruited by *reinforcement* and *learning* drives {cite:p}`Pi2013,Krabbe2019,Takesian2018,Wall2016` rather than by locomotion per se, which is consistent with — but does not in isolation prove — an A1/V1 polarity divergence in locomotion gating relative to V1 {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Paper maps anatomical inputs but does not address reinforcement, learning, or locomotion recruitment of VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The thalamic gating of these dynamics has been resolved at the input level by {cite:t}`Anastasiades2021a`, who showed that mediodorsal and ventromedial thalamic afferents engage distinct L1 microcircuits in PFC and that VIP cells (rather than NDNF cells) are the primary L1 disinhibitory translators of long-range thalamic input onto deep-layer pyramidal cells, with input strength scaling with afferent identity {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Wall2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows thalamic input to VIP cells generally but does not address mediodorsal/ventromedial thalamic specifics or PFC L1 microcircuits.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Obermayer2019` characterised a subset of mPFC VIP interneurons that co-express choline acetyltransferase and that release acetylcholine onto neighbouring pyramidal neurons via nicotinic receptors, in addition to GABAergic transmission, providing a local intracortical source of cholinergic excitation independent of basal forebrain projections {cite:p}`Obermayer2019,Wall2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Paper does not discuss ChAT-expressing VIP cells or local ACh release; it addresses basal forebrain cholinergic input to VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Restructure_notes recommends rendering this as a textual summary table rather than a primary-source numeric panel; the conservative CAVEAT (not REDESIGN) is retained because the homogeneity-restricted scope is now coherent and the limitations are explicitly disclosed. **Panel C**: Hippocampal VIP/IS-3 motif schematic (VIP/CR/CCK distinctions; OLM and bistratified targeting in CA1) {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Gulyas1996,Kawaguchi1996`. **Panel D**: Cross-area conflict table summarising top-down, neuromodulatory, and sensory drivers of VIP recruitment by region {cite:p}`Wall2016,Pi2013,Lee2013,Dipoppa2018,Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Maywood2006`. *Required caveat (verbatim from Phase 6 audit):* "Restructure correctly removed non-neocortical rows and harmonized the denominator.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Brain-Wide Maps of Synaptic Input to Cortical Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Claim is about a cross-area conflict table; Wall2016 is one of many citations. Role 'contradicts' is unclear—paper supports VIP recruitment by long-range inputs but claim context is ambiguous.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: First, the canonical motif is reproducible: connectivity asymmetry favouring SST targets, recruitment by salient external events combined with arousal, and net pyramidal gain enhancement under top-down or running-coupled drive have been independently replicated across V1, A1, S1, M1, and PFC {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Kamigaki2017,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Pinto2015 shows VIP outcome responses in PFC but does not directly demonstrate connectivity asymmetry favoring SST targets or net pyramidal gain enhancement.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The picture from these and related studies {cite:p}`Tamboli2024,Ramosprats2022,Veit2023,Mcfarlan2024a,Mcfarlan2024b,Garciajuncoclemente2017` is that the canonical motif's *behavioural* contribution is largely about gating plasticity windows and sustaining attentional or learning-related disinhibition, not about producing transient gain on a millisecond-to-second timescale. This is consistent with the kinetic profile of VIP→SST IPSCs, which is slower than VIP→PV {cite:p}`Pi2013`, and with the fact that VIP-cell perturbation typically produces slow-timescale changes in pyramidal firing rather than fast modulation of stimulus-evoked responses {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Kuchibhotla2017,Pinto2015,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Pinto2015 shows VIP outcome responses but abstract doesn't address slow-timescale changes in pyramidal firing from VIP perturbation specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: This circuit-level reading also clarifies what the canonical motif explains and what it does not. It explains why salient external events combined with arousal produce gain enhancement of pyramidal stimulus responses in superficial cortex {cite:p}`Lee2013,Fu2014,Zhang2014,Pinto2015,Williams2019,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Pinto2015 reports VIP outcome responses but does not directly demonstrate gain enhancement of pyramidal stimulus responses from salient events plus arousal.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Critically, however, reinforcement responses are not exclusive: the same cells co-encode locomotion and arousal, and disentangling reinforcement-specific from state-driven components requires task designs that orthogonalise the two {cite:p}`Pinto2015,Szadai2022,Garciajuncoclemente2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cell-Type-Specific Activity in Prefrontal Cortex during Goal-Directed Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract shows VIP cells encode outcomes but doesn't explicitly discuss co-encoding of locomotion/arousal or difficulty disentangling reinforcement from state signals.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Response polarity, sensory selectivity, and behavioural correlates differ across cortical area, task structure, and individual VIP subtype, and the inferred VIP→somatostatin-interneuron (SST) suppression mechanism does not always obtain — for example, several preparations report concurrent activation rather than reciprocal inhibition of SST cells during locomotion {cite:p}`Pakan2016,Dipoppa2018,Yavorska2021`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Effects of Locomotion in Auditory Cortex Are Not Mediated by the VIP Network
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows VIP and running act independently in ACtx, but does not report concurrent VIP+SST activation during locomotion.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The unifying observation is that bidirectional perturbation of VIP cells reshapes oscillation *power, coherence, and phase coupling* far more reliably than it abolishes or generates the rhythm itself, with two consequences: (i) most rhythms continue when VIP cells are silenced, but their amplitude, inter-areal synchrony, and state dependence are altered {cite:p}`Veit2023,Bastos2023a,Sabri2024,Lee2025`; and (ii) causal attribution remains contested — bidirectional perturbation studies disagree on whether VIP cells are necessary, sufficient, or merely permissive for oscillatory state changes, with conflicts in particular over locomotion polarity across sensory cortices {cite:p}`Fu2014,Pakan2016`, seizure susceptibility {cite:p}`Hall2015`, Up-state generation {cite:p}`Tahvildari2012,Kawaguchi2001`, and the VIP-versus-SST locus of cholinergic state changes {cite:p}`Chen2015a,Szadai2022,Kuchibhotla2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortex-wide response mode of VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons by reward and punishment
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not address cholinergic state changes, VIP-vs-SST locus, or Up-state generation disputes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The originally cited counter-pair (Pi 2013 awake-cortex disinhibition versus Neske 2016 slice Up-state dispensability) lies outside this section's filtered citation map; both keys are flagged in the section's unmet citation list and the conflict here uses {cite:t}`Tahvildari2012` and {cite:t}`Szadai2022` as the available proxies for the same disagreement. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Cortex-wide response mode of VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons by reward and punishment
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss Up-state dispensability or the specific disagreement being proxied.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The ChAT-VIP subpopulation {cite:p}`Obermayer2019,Dudai2020,Granger2020` adds a further mode: roughly 0.5–1% of cortical VIP cells co-express choline acetyltransferase and locally release ACh, and these cells fire faithfully to whisker stimuli.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Prefrontal cortical ChAT-VIP interneurons provide local excitation by cholinergic synaptic transmission and control attention
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms ChAT-VIP co-expression and ACh release in mPFC, but says nothing about whisker stimuli or firing to whisker stimuli.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Driver-line off-targets, anatomical-vs-functional methodological mismatches, and modality choice are flagged as methodological caveats throughout the review (see also {ref}`sec-disinhibition-framework`); explicit reporting of these covariates would resolve a substantial fraction of the residual cross-study heterogeneity {cite:p}`Obermayer2019,Dudai2020,Kannan2022,Yavorska2021`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Prefrontal cortical ChAT-VIP interneurons provide local excitation by cholinergic synaptic transmission and control attention
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss driver-line caveats, off-targets, or cross-study heterogeneity explicitly.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Garrett2020` reported that V1 VIP cells are stimulus-driven by novel images but suppressed by familiar images during a change-detection task — a form of stimulus-history dependence that survives matched arousal and locomotion.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Experience shapes activity dynamics and stimulus coding of VIP inhibitory cells
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract confirms novel vs familiar VIP responses but doesn't explicitly state that this survives matched arousal and locomotion controls.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Within the present packet, the available evidence shows that A1 VIP cells are robustly recruited by *reinforcement* and *learning* drives {cite:p}`Pi2013,Krabbe2019,Takesian2018,Wall2016` rather than by locomotion per se, which is consistent with — but does not in isolation prove — an A1/V1 polarity divergence in locomotion gating relative to V1 {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Inhibitory circuit gating of auditory critical-period plasticity
insufficient_triples.note: Paper focuses on L1 interneuron gating of critical-period plasticity, not on VIP recruitment by reinforcement or learning drives.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The cartoon overlay (panel C) shows a head-fixed mouse on a wheel with V1/A1/S1 sketches indicating concurrent expected VIP-vs-SST polarity by area, with the auditory-cortex cell highlighted as the explicit area-paradox leaf {cite:p}`Yavorska2021`. {cite:p}`Veit2023,Garciajuncoclemente2017,Kannan2022` :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Dual-polarity voltage imaging of the concurrent dynamics of multiple neuron types
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions neocortical VIP-SST antagonism but does not specify area-specific polarity differences across V1/A1/S1.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Kannan2022` reported state-dependent antagonism within the VIP population, refining the textbook reading of VIP cells as a class-level monolith.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Dual-polarity voltage imaging of the concurrent dynamics of multiple neuron types
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract describes VIP-vs-SST antagonism between populations, but does not clearly describe heterogeneity *within* the VIP population itself.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pupil-tracked passive arousal does still recruit VIP cells in ACtx {cite:p}`Reimer2014,Munoz2017`, and active engagement during auditory recognition produces cholinergic axon activation that simultaneously engages PV/SST/VIP networks {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Pupil Fluctuations Track Fast Switching of Cortical States during Quiet Wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: Reimer2014 studied V1 and possibly other cortex but abstract doesn't specify auditory cortex (ACtx) specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Munoz2017` and {cite:t}`Reimer2014` documented that VIP-class layer-1 interneurons are preferentially active during active wakefulness and during pupil-tracked desynchronisation.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Pupil Fluctuations Track Fast Switching of Cortical States during Quiet Wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: Reimer2014 mentions interneuron subtypes but abstract doesn't specifically name VIP or layer-1 interneurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The morphological substrate identified by {cite:t}`Kawaguchi1996` and {cite:t}`Gonchar2008` — bipolar VIP cells with vertical apical-targeting axons — provides the direct anatomical route from this top-down input onto deep-layer pyramidal output {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1996,Gonchar2008,Bastos2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Physiological and morphological identification of somatostatin- or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-containing cells among GABAergic cell subtypes in rat frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Paper describes VIP bipolar cells with descending axons but does not specifically discuss apical-dendrite targeting of deep-layer pyramidal neurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The morphological substrate identified by {cite:t}`Kawaguchi1996` and {cite:t}`Gonchar2008` — bipolar VIP cells with vertical apical-targeting axons — provides the direct anatomical route from this top-down input onto deep-layer pyramidal output {cite:p}`Kawaguchi1996,Gonchar2008,Bastos2023a`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Physiological and morphological identification of somatostatin- or vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-containing cells among GABAergic cell subtypes in rat frontal cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Same as above; descending axons described but specific apical-targeting of deep pyramidal output not explicitly established.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Maywood2006` and {cite:t}`Maywood2011` showed that VIP/VPAC2 paracrine signalling synchronises circadian gene expression among SCN neurons and that VIP/VPAC2 is the dominant non-redundant pathway maintaining cellular synchrony, with co-cultured wild-type and VIP-knockout slices partially rescuing rhythmicity {cite:p}`Maywood2006,Maywood2011`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Synchronization and Maintenance of Timekeeping in Suprachiasmatic Circadian Clock Cells by Neuropeptidergic Signaling
insufficient_triples.note: The co-culture rescue experiment (wild-type and VIP-knockout slices) is not described in this paper's abstract; may be from Maywood2011.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: But VIP cells also (i) send long-range axons to subcortical reward targets {cite:p}`Lee2014b`, (ii) co-release acetylcholine in PFC {cite:p}`Obermayer2019`, (iii) selectively target other interneurons rather than principal cells in hippocampus {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Gulyas1996`, and (iv) act as peptidergic projection neurons in SCN {cite:p}`Maywood2006,Cutler2003,Jones2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Synchronization and Maintenance of Timekeeping in Suprachiasmatic Circadian Clock Cells by Neuropeptidergic Signaling
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows VIP acts as a paracrine neuropeptide in SCN; calling SCN VIP cells 'peptidergic projection neurons' is not directly supported by this abstract.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The non-uniqueness is also real: VIP cells form measurable contacts on PV, pyramidal, NDNF, and CCK targets, all of which are behaviourally engaged in some condition documented in the literature reviewed here {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Schneidermizell2025,Veit2023`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract focuses on VIP→SOM→Pyr pathway; no mention of VIP contacts on PV, NDNF, or CCK targets.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Two-photon imaging during head-fixed running has converged on a basic phenomenology: VIP cells in V1 show the strongest run-related rise of any genetically defined interneuron class, while SST cells move in the opposite direction, and PV cells track stimulus contrast more than locomotion {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016,Karnani2016b`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition: Localized Lateral Disinhibition by VIP Interneurons
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract does not discuss locomotion, running, or state-dependent activity modulation of VIP/SST/PV cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The within-hippocampus consensus that emerges from {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Luo2020,Tamboli2024,Neubrandt2025a,Lenkey2025` is that VIP recruitment is state-dependent but its sign and downstream effect differ between subregions and between IS-cell subtypes — consistent with the cortical reading but cautioning against a single "VIP enables learning" rule. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Mouse hippocampal CA1 VIP interneurons detect novelty in the environment and support recognition memory
insufficient_triples.note: Paper focuses on CA1 only and shows consistent VIP novelty response; abstract doesn't discuss subregion differences or differing signs across subtypes.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In layer 5 and layer 6, the motif is engaged differently: deep-layer VIP cells receive a higher proportion of thalamic input and contact a different distribution of postsynaptic targets, including projection-class pyramidal neurons that are themselves heterogeneous {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Sermet2019,Munoz2017,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses layer-specific SST/VIP modulation but does not address thalamic input proportions or deep-layer VIP postsynaptic targets.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pupil-tracked arousal alone — without locomotion — is sufficient to elevate VIP activity in V1 and frontal cortex, indicating that the relevant variable is broader than wheel velocity {cite:p}`Reimer2014,Garciajuncoclemente2017,Munoz2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP inhibition of SST and active wakefulness transitions, but does not explicitly discuss pupil-tracked arousal without locomotion.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Pupil-tracked passive arousal does still recruit VIP cells in ACtx {cite:p}`Reimer2014,Munoz2017`, and active engagement during auditory recognition produces cholinergic axon activation that simultaneously engages PV/SST/VIP networks {cite:p}`Kuchibhotla2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses active wakefulness transitions and VIP-SST interactions but does not mention pupil-tracked passive arousal specifically.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: {cite:t}`Garciajuncoclemente2017` and {cite:t}`Munoz2017` showed that arousal alone — without locomotion — recruits VIP cells in frontal cortex and dorsal-cortex layer-1 networks.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions active wakefulness and VIP/cholinergic modulation but does not specifically address arousal without locomotion.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Sleep-state recruitment of VIP cells is highly state-specific and distinct from PV/SST {cite:p}`Brecier2022,Rolle2025,Reimer2014,Munoz2017`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract discusses active wakefulness transitions but does not address sleep-state recruitment of VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Theta (4–10 Hz) in hippocampus is paced primarily by septal and entorhinal inputs interacting with OLM and other principal-cell-targeting interneurons, but VIP/calretinin "IS3" cells provide a specialised disinhibitory layer that controls the rate and timing of OLM output {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Chamberland2010,Luo2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Synaptic Mechanisms Underlying the Network State-Dependent Recruitment of VIP-Expressing Interneurons in the CA1 Hippocampus
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows VIP-IS recruitment during theta but does not specifically address control of OLM output rate/timing.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Second, the motif is *not* exclusive: in the same preparations, VIP→PV, VIP→pyramidal, and VIP→NDNF connections are present, behaviourally engaged, and quantitatively non-trivial {cite:p}`Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Yu2019,Abs2018,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Abstract mentions VIP disinhibiting E and FS neurons but does not detail specific VIP→PV, VIP→pyramidal, or VIP→NDNF connections quantitatively.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Earlier-work paper key Yu2021 was substituted with the in-set {cite:t}`Yu2019` finding on whisker-stimulus encoding by S1 VIP cells, which carries the same methodological direction.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Recruitment of GABAergic Interneurons in the Barrel Cortex during Active Tactile Behavior
insufficient_triples.note: Cannot verify substitution claim about Yu2021 vs Yu2019 from the abstract alone; the paper does address whisker-stimulus encoding by S1 VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The transcriptomic decomposition implies that "VIP cells in M1" is not a single functional unit and that any cross-area generalisation needs to specify which transcriptomic subtype is being assayed {cite:p}`Scala2021,Munozmanchado2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Diversity of Interneurons in the Dorsal Striatum Revealed by Single-Cell RNA Sequencing and PatchSeq
insufficient_triples.note: Paper focuses on striatal interneurons, not M1 cortical VIP transcriptomic subtypes. Does not directly support the M1-specific claim.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: CGE origin and 5HT3aR expression mark VIP+ cells from cortex, hippocampus, amygdala, and striatum {cite:p}`Miyoshi2010,Vucurovic2010,Tricoire2011,Munozmanchado2018`, and transcriptomic taxonomies reveal homologous Vip-Mybpc1 / Vip-Lect1 subtypes across mouse cortical areas {cite:p}`Scala2021`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Diversity of Interneurons in the Dorsal Striatum Revealed by Single-Cell RNA Sequencing and PatchSeq
insufficient_triples.note: Paper identifies striatal VIP interneurons but does not discuss CGE origin or 5HT3aR expression for these cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: But VIP cells also (i) send long-range axons to subcortical reward targets {cite:p}`Lee2014b`, (ii) co-release acetylcholine in PFC {cite:p}`Obermayer2019`, (iii) selectively target other interneurons rather than principal cells in hippocampus {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Gulyas1996`, and (iv) act as peptidergic projection neurons in SCN {cite:p}`Maywood2006,Cutler2003,Jones2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: The mouse VPAC2 receptor confers suprachiasmatic nuclei cellular rhythmicity and responsiveness to vasoactive intestinal polypeptide in vitro
insufficient_triples.note: Paper shows VPAC2 receptor role in SCN rhythmicity but does not address VIP cells acting as projection neurons per se.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: **The disinhibition motif and its alternatives.** **(A)** Canonical motif: VIP cells, recruited by long-range glutamatergic and ascending cholinergic inputs, preferentially inhibit SST cells, releasing pyramidal dendrites from inhibition; minor VIP→PV and VIP→pyramidal edges are drawn explicitly to flag that the canonical schematic is a tendency rather than an exclusive wiring rule {cite:p}`Pfeffer2013,Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Karnani2016a`. **(B)** Three alternative motifs documented in the same evidence base: direct VIP→PV control of perisomatic inhibition {cite:p}`Pi2013,Karnani2016b,Kuchibhotla2017`, direct VIP→pyramidal inhibition by bipolar/CR-positive VIP cells {cite:p}`Lee2013,Karnani2016a,Karnani2016b,Schneidermizell2025`, and a layer-1 VIP→NDNF disinhibitory motif gating apical dendritic input {cite:p}`Abs2018,Anastasiades2021a,Veit2023`; the hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket motif is a homologue with a distinct oscillatory context {cite:p}`Turi2019,Francavilla2018a,Dudai2020`. **(C)** Decision tree mapping behavioural context — locomotion, attentional top-down, reinforcement, fear/learning — to the motif most consistent with the empirical recruitment data and to the supporting cite-keys; the assignment is qualitative because the literature does not yet contain joint manipulations of multiple motifs under matched conditions {cite:p}`Pi2013,Lee2013,Fu2014,Pinto2015,Kuchibhotla2017,Cichon2017,Williams2019,Krabbe2019,Veit2023`. **(D)** Schematic legend distinguishing measured edges (solid) from inferred or context-dependent edges (dashed). *Caveat:* this figure is a schematic with no quantitative data substrate; assignment of behavioural context to dominant motif in Panel C is inferred from joint reading of the cited recruitment studies and is not the output of a meta-analytic weighting. :::
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Barrel cortex VIP/ChAT interneurons suppress sensory responses in vivo
insufficient_triples.note: Paper is about VIP/ChAT cells in barrel cortex, not hippocampal VIP→OLM/basket motif as claimed.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: In layer 5 and layer 6, the motif is engaged differently: deep-layer VIP cells receive a higher proportion of thalamic input and contact a different distribution of postsynaptic targets, including projection-class pyramidal neurons that are themselves heterogeneous {cite:p}`Anastasiades2021a,Sermet2019,Munoz2017,Schneidermizell2025`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Pathway-, layer- and cell-type-specific thalamic input to mouse barrel cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Sermet2019 shows VIP receive intermediate thalamic input without strong layer specificity, but doesn't specifically address deep-layer VIP targeting projection-class pyramidal neurons.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Primary visual cortex emphasises locomotion-gated, contrast-dependent gain enhancement {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Millman2020,Pakan2016`; auditory cortex deploys VIP cells for reinforcement-linked disinhibition and critical-period plasticity {cite:p}`Pi2013,Krabbe2019,Takesian2018`; somatosensory cortex recruits them via motor copy during active sensing {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019,Kuchibhotla2017`; medial prefrontal and anterior cingulate cortex implement top-down control of attention, working memory, and pain {cite:p}`Bastos2023a,Anastasiades2021a,Kamigaki2017,Li2022a`; and hippocampal VIP/calretinin "IS-3" cells assemble a circuit motif distinct from neocortex, targeting other interneurons rather than principal cells {cite:p}`Gulyas1996,Tyan2014,Tricoire2011,Kawaguchi1996`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Pathway-, layer- and cell-type-specific thalamic input to mouse barrel cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Sermet2019 maps thalamic input to VIP cells in S1 but doesn't specifically address motor-copy or active-sensing recruitment of VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: Yet the same lineage implements opposite-sign locomotion gating in V1 versus A1 (V1 up, A1 down — see {ref}`sec-oscillatory-dynamics`) {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016,Pi2013,Krabbe2019`, motor-copy disinhibition in S1 {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019`, sustained working-memory dynamics in mPFC {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Anastasiades2021a`, an interneuron-selective IS-3 motif in CA1 {cite:p}`Tyan2014,Gulyas1996`, and peptidergic synchronisation in SCN {cite:p}`Maywood2006,Cutler2003,Webb2009,Jones2018`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Pathway-, layer- and cell-type-specific thalamic input to mouse barrel cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Sermet2019 maps thalamic inputs to S1 interneurons but does not demonstrate motor-copy disinhibition by VIP cells.

insufficient_triples.claim_text: The dominant *driver* of VIP recruitment is therefore region-specific: locomotion and arousal in V1 {cite:p}`Dipoppa2018,Pakan2016`; reinforcement and learning in A1 {cite:p}`Pi2013,Krabbe2019,Takesian2018`; motor command in S1 {cite:p}`Lee2013,Sermet2019`; working-memory and thalamic context in mPFC {cite:p}`Kamigaki2017,Anastasiades2021a`; light/photic input in SCN {cite:p}`Jones2018,Todd2020`.
insufficient_triples.paper_title: Pathway-, layer- and cell-type-specific thalamic input to mouse barrel cortex
insufficient_triples.note: Sermet2019 shows thalamic input patterns to VIP cells in S1 but doesn't demonstrate motor command as the dominant driver of VIP recruitment.
